Summary: Crash on Mac platform in libmwm_interpreter.dylib:jitCodeGenerator::ClearUnusedBailouts().
Description: MATLAB crashes with the following stack trace: [0] libmwm_interpreter.dylib:jitCodeGenerator::ClearUnusedBailouts() + 52 bytes  [1] libmwm_interpreter.dylib:Jit::Compile(jitCodeGenerator&, jitCodegenStack&, cg_stack_id*, int*, int, _jitSegInfo*, int**, bool) + 364 bytes  [2] libmwm_interpreter.dylib:std::basic_filebuf<wchar_t, std::char_traits<wchar_t> >::_M_underflow_common(bool) + 1116084 bytes  [3] libmwm_interpreter.dylib:std::basic_filebuf<wchar_t, std::char_traits<wchar_t> >::_M_underflow_common(bool) + 557940 bytes  [4] libmwm_interpreter.dylib:std::basic_filebuf<wchar_t, std::char_traits<wchar_t> >::_M_underflow_common(bool) + 545028 bytes
Workaround: Run the following command before executing your code:feature jit off;

Summary: Scheduling problem with models containing continuous sample times
Description: Models containing continuous sample times may not execute all sub-rates during real-time execution due to a scheduler bug in the mpc555dk_main.c  file. The bug relates to incorrect handling of the TID01EQ  preprocessor define in the mpc555dk_main.c  file.
Workaround: Do not use continuous sample times in your model.

Summary: Warning about 'TiledPaperMargins' when opening a model saved to an earlier version of Simulink
Description: When loading from Simulink 6.4 (R2006a) a model that was saved as an R14sp3 or earlier model, you will get the following warning:Warning: block_diagram does not have a parameter named 'TiledPaperMargins'
Workaround: It is permissible to ingnore this warning: The model will load successfully.

Summary: Connecting to xPC Target via Simulink external mode might cause errors
Description: Attempts to connect to a target PC via Simulink external mode might display on the host PC warnings like the following : Parameter : XX does not exist on Target
Workaround: These warning messages are benign; you can ignore these messages. After you receive these messages, the connection via Simulink external mode and the  running of external mode simulation should procede as expected.

Summary: Deployed GUIDE GUIs with ActiveX controls may not work properly
Description: The deployed version of a working GUIDE GUI with ActiveX controls, generated by the MATLAB compiler, may not work properly when running  inside MATLAB, from the operating system, or both. An error dialog may appear or a segmentation violation may occur. The filename format of the deployed GUI is platform specific. For example, it has an .exe  extension on Windows. Please refer to the MATLAB compiler document for more information.
Workaround: If an error dialog appears when running the deployed GUI, copy any files for the ActiveX controls in your GUI to the following directory: DEPLOY_DIR\GUINAME_mcr\GUINAME\, where DEPLOY_DIR is the location of the deployed version of the GUI (.exe file on Windows for example) and GUINAME is the name of your GUIDE GUI. Files for any ActiveX controls in your GUI have names of this pattern: GUINAME_activexX, where X (uppercase) is the serial number.If a segmentation violation occurs, that problem may be addressed by Bug Report 290531. Please apply the solution or workaround suggested there.

Summary: MCR intitialization appears to modify system properties in Java application
Description: After an MCC-generated shared library is loaded or Builder for Java generated class is created inside a user's Java application, System.getProperties().getSize() may return the wrong number of properties.This is due to a bug in the java.util.Properties class.  See http://bugs.sun.com/bugdatabase/view_bug.do?bug_id=4880089 for details.
Workaround: Ignore the value returned by System.getProperties().getSize().

Summary: MAT files not included in compilation with dotnettool and mxltool
Description: The MAT files added to a DOTNETTOOL or MXLTOOL project are not compiled into the application.
Workaround: The following workaround is available for MATLAB Compiler 4.4 (R2006a) and later:1) Backup your existing basicui.m file, located in  <matlabroot>/toolbox/compiler, by renaming it to basicui.m_bckup.2) Download the attached basicui.m file and copy it to: <matlabroot>/toolbox/compiler directory.3) Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Speed deviation parameter of the Synchronous Machine block is incorrect
Description: There is an error in the calculation of the speed deviation parameter of the mechanical internal model of the Synchronous Machine block when the mechanical input parameter is set to: Speed w.This error results in incorrect frequency calculation of the output voltage of the machine when the speed input of the block is varying.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached version of the powerlib_models_updatedfor R6a.mdl file. Here are the steps:   Quit MATLAB  Rename the following file: $MATLAB\toolbox\physmod\powersys\powersys\powerlib_models.mdl  (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing  matlabroot  at the MATLAB Command Prompt.)  Rename the file to powerlib_models.mdl.old  Download the attached file. Rename it to powerlib_models.mdl. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2.  Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Passing an uninitialized dynamic array from Excel to your COM object cause the application to exit
Description: When an uninitialized dynamic array is passed from Excel to a COM Object built with MATLAB Builder for Excel or Builder for .Net, Excel exits abnormally. Executing the following code will demonstrate this problem: Sub FooDim x() as Doublecall myclass.foo(1, x);End Sub
Workaround: Ensure that your arrays are allocated before passing them into the COM Object. The following code illustrates how to do this:Sub FooDim x() as DoubleReDim Preserve x(0 To 0)call myclass.foo(1, x);End Sub

Summary: c281x SCI Transmit interrupt for module A does not rearm and is executed only once.
Description: Once an SCITXA interrupt is fired, the wrong flag is cleared inside the c281x SCI Transmit interrupt for module A, preventing any new SCITXA interrupts from being processed. This error occurs during code execution.
Workaround: Close MATLAB Browse to the following directory: [matlabroot]\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000 Backup the following file by renaming it: ti_c281x_interrupts.tlc   Copy the attached file into the following directory: [matlabroot]\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000blks  Open MATLAB At the command prompt, type: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Segmentation violation occurs when calling the init function of block connected to a bus
Description: Calling the init fcn of a block connected to a bus causes a segmentation violation. The Bus Object for the bus signal must be specified by the block for the crash to occur. The init fcn of the block is run during model compilation, so the crash can occur during Update Diagram or Simulation Start.

Summary: Unexpected termination or fatal error for large or complex MATLAB files in the Editor/Debugger or with mlint function
Description: MATLAB might terminate unexpectedly or experience a fatal error when you run mlint  for a large or complex MATLAB file. This can also occur if you open or work with a large or complex MATLAB file in the Editor/Debugger when M-Lint enabled. A large or complex MATLAB file is one that strains system resources. Examples includeThousands of variables Thousands of functions Tens of thousands of statements Hundreds of language keyword pairs, such as if/else  statements
Workaround: If the problem happens when working with an MATLAB file in the Editor/Debugger, you can disable M-Lint prior to opening the file, however, you will lose the benefits of M-Lint.Another way to address the problem is to make the file smaller or less complex. This will also improve performance and reliability. Ways to do so includeBreaking up the file into a set of smaller filesUsing subfunctions or nested functionsUsing more efficient programming practices. For example, use the switch function instead of if/else pairs.

Summary: Text objects cropped on MAC with Java 1.5 in zbuffer/opengl
Description: Upgrading Java to 1.5 on MAC in MATLAB 2006a causes text objects to be cropped when using the zbuffer or opengl renderers.
Workaround: Use version 1.4 of Java.

Summary: Adjust Contrast tool in Image Tool does not work with images of class single
Description: If you display a single-precision image in the Image Tool (imtool ) and then activate the Adjust Contrast tool on the image, the Adjust Contrast tool outputs the following error when you try to move the contrast adjustment window.  ??? Error using ==> intminInvalid class name.Error in ==> imcontrast>createHistogramPalette/validateClim at 1040            minOfDataType = double(intmin(getClassType(imgModel)));...
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the version of the imcontrast function in your current installation with the updated version contained in the attached file.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot\toolbox\images\imuitools\imcontrast.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, imcontrast.m.OLD.Download the attached file, extract imcontrast.m, storing it the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Body dialog column data swapped: Translated from Origin of and Components in Axes of
Description: In the Body block dialog, on the Position  panel, SimMechanics reads and saves the data from the columns Translated from Origin of  and Components in Axes of  as swapped. This bug occurs only in Version 2.4 (R2006a). Furthermore, Body blocks from models built from SimMechanics prior to Version 2.4 display  the column values as swapped in Version 2.4. But such models will simulate  correctly, that is, according to the saved values, not the swapped values displayed.
Workaround: To avoid confusion, in the Version 2.4 Body dialog, use the same set of values for both columns, Translated from Origin of and Components in Axes of. If you must use different sets of values in the two columns, swap the intended value sets.Alternatively, you can use the attached workaround, which is intended ONLY for the R2006a release. Download and unzip the attached file, and copy the P-files to the following directory, overwriting the files with the same names already there:$MATLAB/toolbox/physmod/mech/mech/@MECH/@MechFrameHelper

Summary: cp command on UNIX systems:  Real-Time Workshop can become inoperable if command is aliased
Description: On UNIX systems, it is common practice to define the alias cp -i  for the cp  command. However, this alias can cause Real-Time Workshop to become inoperable. Specifically, this command can cause Real-Time Workshop to hang while generating code if the model includes model references and code has been already generated for the model.
Workaround: Do one of the following: Start MATLAB from a command shell where the cp command is not aliased. Delete files from the build directory manually before generating code to the same directory.

Summary: EMBLREAD/GETEMBL returns incomplete/invalid structures when reading an EMBL flat file created in release 87 of the EMBL-EBI database.
Description: Bioinformatics functions EMBLREAD and GETEMBL return incomplete or invalid structures when reading an EMBL flat file created in release 87 of the EMBL-EBI database. For more information, see EMBL release 87 notes .
Workaround: If you have Bioinformatics Toolbox version 2.3 or earlier, you can update the function EMBLREAD by using the following procedure.1. In the MATLAB Command Window type which emblread.mThis displays the location of the file emblread.m.2. Rename emblread.m to emblread.bak.3. Download the updated file attached to this bug report to the directory identified with the which command.

Summary: Bus Selector incorrectly warns of unconnected input signals
Description: A Bus Selector block incorrectly warns about unconnected input signals in the following circumstances: The bus entering the Bus Selector includes a bus element whose ultimate source is an Inport block. The model creates the bus element by selecting and combining elements of the Inport bus, using upstream Bus Selector and Bus Creator blocks.
Workaround: Specify a bus object for the bus element, using the parameter dialog box of the upstream Bus Creator blocks that create the bus element. You do not need to select the Bus Creator blocks' Output as non-virtual bus option.

Summary: Invalid code generation for iterator block
Description: Real-Time Workshop  and Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder  may generate invalid code for an iterator block that has a selector block as a signal source. The problem occurs under the following conditions:  The model contains a For Iterator  subsystem or a While Iterator  subsystem. If the model contains a For Iterator  subsystem, the For-Iterator  block has an external iteration limit that is connected through an input port to a Selector  block. If the model contains a While Iterator  subsystem, the While Iterator block  has an external condition that is connected through an input port to a Selector  block.  The Selector  block has a vector input signal and a constant internal element index.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the input port and the iterator block.  Set the Signal Conversion block's Output parameter to Contiguous copy and select Override optimizations and always copy signal.

Summary: MATLAB hangs on exit
Description: Closing a figure in a Simulink model CloseFcn  callback can cause MATLAB to hang on exit.
Workaround: Do not delete or close figures in the model CloseFcn callback.

Summary: Editor/Debugger: line space not proportional for small font sizes
Description: There is extra space between lines of the text in the Editor/Debugger when the font size is smaller than the default.

Summary: Incorrect sample time propagation across subsystem boundary
Description: Simulink's sample time propagation is incorrect in the following case: A set of constant blocks are combined into a single signal (either by mux or bus creator). The signal is passed across an atomic subsystem boundary. The blocks reading the constant values do not all have the same sample time. Inline parameters is off.
Workaround: Any of the following:Specify the desired sample time in the constant blocks instead of leaving the sample time as inf.Use a goto-from pair to pass the signal across the subsystem boundary instead of the input port.Move the constant block sources inside the subsystem.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop: Incorrect number of tunable parameters generated in C-API file
Description: A C-API file generated by Real-Time Workshop may contain incorrect information about tunable parameters if the model fulfills the following conditions:  "Inline parameters" is set to "on". A workspace parameter is set to "tunable" with storage class SimulinkGlobal. The workspace parameter is never accessed during simulation or code execution.(For example, it is used on Stateflow Transitions and States that are never activated.)  In this case, the tunable parameter is optimized in the generated code. However, an incorrect entry for the parameter appears in the C-API file.
Workaround: To workaround the problem in Version 6.4 (R2006a):Download the attached TLC file: globalmaplib.tlcReplace the file matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/globalmaplib.tlc with the downloaded file,where matlabroot is the directory where MATLAB R2006a is installed.Note: this download will not work for earlier releases of MATLAB and Real-Time Workshop.

Summary: Data type conversions: code generation error can occur when redundant type casts are removed for optimization
Description: Real-Time Workshop can fail to generate code for a model or cause MATLAB to crash if all of the following conditions exist:  The model contains a chain of Data Type Conversion blocks. The data type of the input signal or the specified output data type for at least one of the Data Type Conversion blocks is a non-integer type, such as double  or single . The Configuration Parameter dialog optimization option Ignore integer downcasts in folded expressions  is selected.

Summary: From RTDX block returns invalid sample time error in simulations
Description: The From RTDX block in the RTDX Instrumentation library (rtdxBlocks ) fails in simulation under the following conditions: You select Frame-based  on the block mask.  You add the block to a function call subsystem. For a frame of 64 elements, Simulink reports the following error message:Sample time [-64 0] of 'untitled/Function-Call Subsystem/From RTDX' is invalid.
Workaround: Clear the Frame-based check box on the block mask and use a Frame Conversion block from Signal Processing Blockset to convert the output of the From RTDX block to a frame. To see an example that uses this conversion technique, refer to the attached model rtdx_workaround.mdl model.

Summary: MVNCDF computes the univariate normal CDF incorrectly
Description: The MVNCDF function, usually used to compute a multivariate normal CDF, computes the CDF incorrectly in the univariate (1-D) case when the variance parameter is not 1. It should give identical results as NORMCDF, but does not.This bug is only present for the univariate case. MVNCDF computes the CDF correctly in two or more dimensions.
Workaround: This bug is only present in the univariate (1-D) case. No workaround is needed in two or more dimensions.To work around the bug in the univariate case, do one of the following:(1) Replace calls such as mvncdf(x,mu,sigma^2) with mvncdf(x/sigma,mu/sigma,1)Note that the third input to MVNCDF is the covariance matrix, which in the univariate case is the variance. This is in contrast to NORMCDF, where the third input is the standard deviation.(2) Replace line 196 of mvncdf.m withy = normcdf(XU0,0,1) - normcdf(XL0,0,1);

Summary: Code generation fails when Code Composer Studio (CCS) install path or Simulink model path contains spaces
Description: Code generation may fail in the following situations: Your CCS install directory contains spaces. This results in a DOS command execution error while you are generating code from your model. Your Simulink model is in a directory whose full path contains one or more directories that have spaces in their names. This situation generates an error when the code generation process calls the Texas Instruments TCONF utility to generate a CDB file for DSP/BIOS configuration.  In both instances, code generation cannot be completed. The code generation process never makes it to the compilation phase or beyond.
Workaround: Make sure that the CCS install path or the path to the Simulink model you are generating code from does not include spaces.If either path has spaces in a directory name or names, do one of the following:Rename the problem directories to remove the spaces.Resave the model to a different directory without spaces.Reinstall CCS into a directory that does not have spaces on the directory path.

Summary: applycform returns the wrong answer when PCS is XYZ and conversion is BToA3 (absolute colorimetric)
Description: applycform  gives the wrong answer when the PCS is XYZ and when the conversion specified in makecform  is BToA3  (absolute colormetric).
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the version of the applyabsolute.m function in your current installation with the updated version contained in the attached file.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. MATLABROOT\toolbox\images\images\private\applyabsolute.mMATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "OLD" to the file extension, applyabsolute.m.OLD.Download the attached file, extract applyabsolute.m, storing it the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Error using ATI Vision calibration import feature
Description: In CAGE, if you try and import calibration data directly from ATI Vision, the following error is displayed in the MATLAB Command Window and the data is not imported:??? Undefined function or variable 'pm'.Error in ==> cgcalinput.ATI_Vision at 38    varargout{1} = pGuiConvertCalData(cal, true, pm);Error in ==> cgcalinput.import at 12    out = feval(obj.inputFcn, obj);Error in ==> D:\MATLAB\MATLABR2006a\toolbox\mbc\mbcview\@cgtypes\@cgfeaturetype\schema.p>i_importcal at 133Error in ==> D:\MATLAB\MATLABR2006a\toolbox\mbc\mbcview\@cgtypes\@cgfeaturetype\schema.p>@(nd) i_importcal(nd, fcns{n}) at 112Error in ==> D:\MATLAB\MATLABR2006a\toolbox\mbc\mbcview\@cgtools\@cgbrowser\docreateimportmenu.p>i_importmenucb at 88??? Error while evaluating uimenu Callback
Workaround: A patch is available that will fix this error in Version 3.0. To install this fix:Rename the file $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcdata\@cgcalinput\ATI_Vision.m to $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcdata\@cgcalinput\ATI_Vision.m.old.Download the file ATI_Vision.m that is listed in the Attachments section and save it to $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcdata\@cgcalinput\ATI_Vision.m.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Segmentation violation when using the HDF4 library and setting Vdata.fields to more than 256 fields
Description: Calling the hdfVSsetfields  function with more than 256 fields causes a segmentation violation. For example:hdfvs( 'setfields', idVdata, data_field_name );Stack Trace:  [0] hdf.mexw32:_scanattrs(0x656d6954, 0x32676174, 0x7065442c, 0x412c6874) + 288 bytes  [1] 0x2c676174Error in ==> hdfvs at 195[varargout{1:max(1,nargout)}] = hdf('VS',varargin{:});Error in ==> WriteVdataTable at 54hdfvs( 'setfields', idVdata, Vdata.fields );Error in ==> hdftest at 60WriteVdataTable( Vdata );
Workaround: Because the HDF4 library imposes a limit of 256 fieldnames, you must use fewer fieldnames.

Summary: Incorrect simulation results with certain Simulink-PS Converter block naming
Description: When the full name of a Simulink-PS Converter block begins with a numeral, the simulation results may be incorrect.
Workaround:
To work around this issue, download the attached version of the nesl_solverinitialization.p file. Here are the steps:   Quit MATLAB  Rename the following file: $MATLAB	oolbox\physmod
etwork_engine
e_sli\private
esl_solverinitialization.p  (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing  matlabroot  at the MATLAB Command Prompt.)  Rename the file to nesl_solverinitialization.p.old    Download the attached file. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2.  Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Undeclared Identifier compile-time error involving Initial Condition block and S-function block
Description: When Real-Time Workshop generated code is compiled, an Undeclared Identifier error can occur if the following conditions are met: An Initial Condition block is connected to an input port of an S-function block. The input port of the S-function block sets optimization options usingssSetInputPortOptimOpts(S, idx, SS_REUSABLE_AND_LOCAL).
Workaround: Insert a Signal Copy block between the Initial Condition block and the S-function block.

Summary: Using sprintf to label ports on mask icons fails
Description: Using sprintf('') to label a port on a mask icon causes the icon display to fail.
Workaround: Replaceport_label('input', 1, sprintf(''))withport_label('input', 1, '')

Summary: Data retrieval problems: data truncated or Java exception in some cases
Description: For certain ODBC/JDBC database drivers, if you use fetch or the VQB to retrieve NUMERIC or DECIMAL data, the data might be truncated at 2 decimal places (for some MS SQL Server databases), or you might see the following Java exception:  ??? Java exception occurred:java.lang.NumberFormatException        atjava.math.BigDecimal.(UnknownSource)        atjava.math.BigDecimal.(UnknownSource)        atsun.jdbc.odbc.JdbcOdbcResultSet.getBigDecimal(UnknownSource)        atsun.jdbc.odbc.JdbcOdbcResultSet.getObject(UnknownSource)        atcom.mathworks.toolbox.database.fetchTheData.dataFetch(fetchTheData.java:607)
Workaround: Download the attached database.jar file to replace the existing matlabroot/java/jar/toolbox/database.jar file, where matlabroot is the directory in which MATLAB is installed.

Summary: Incorrect initialization of signals using custom storage classes with "Static" initialization.
Description: In R2006a, if a signal object uses a custom storage class with 'Static' initialization, the corresponding variable in the generated code is always statically initialized to zero, even if the signal object's InitialValue is specified as a non-zero value.The resulting code is still numerically correct, because the signal's value is set correctly by the model's initialization/step function.

Summary: RSim target: wrong answer generated when using -i option and data type of inport signal is a type other than double
Description: Under the following conditions, rapid simulations of a model can generate wrong answers:  The model's input data sources contain Inport block(s)  The data type specified for the inport signal is a type other than double   You use the -i  option to feed the Inport block with data from a MAT-file during simulation

Summary: Missing assignment of the .switch section could lead to unpredictable behavior of the code execution for Flash-based models
Description: This problem affects c281x-based models using Flash Memory map and containing switch statements of at least 12 cases.Under these conditions, CCS compiler will create a switch table and store data in the .switch memory section of the linker command file.The linker command file automatically generated by the Embedded Target for TIC2000 DSP doesn't contain a specific memory assignment for the .switch section.  A warning is issued by CCS, and an arbitrary location is chosen by the CCS compiler for the .switch section. This default location is not compatible with stand alone applications. The behavior of the switch case will be unpredictable during code execution out of Flash memory.
Workaround: Close MATLAB Browse to the following directory:[matlabroot]\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000 Backup these the following file by copying it to a different folder:ti_c281x_cmd.tlcCopy the attached file into the \tic2000 directory:ti_c281x_cmd.tlcStart MATLAB

Summary: Documentation of the cost matrix for TREEFIT incorrect.
Description: The documentation for TREEFIT describes the rows and columns of the 'cost' parameter this way: "C(i,j) is the cost of classifying a point into class i if its true class is j" The description is incorrect.
Workaround: The correct description is: "C(i,j) is the cost of classifying a point into class j if its true class is i" Note that the MATLAB file help for TREEFIT gives the correct description.

Summary: MATLAB crashes during figure creation if there is a user-created file called handle.m file on the path
Description: If a user creates a file called handle.m and adds the folder containing the file to the MATLAB path, then creating any figures crashes MATLAB with a segmentation violation in libuij.dll .  When this happens, a stack trace is generated with the top frame of the stack reading: libuij.dll:public: void __thiscall FigureJavaEventManager::AddViewListeners
Workaround: When a user creates a file called handle.m and adds the folder containing the file to the MATLAB path, any calls to the MATLAB built-in function handle are now routed to handle.m instead.  Renaming handle.m resolves the issue.

Summary: Using the Model Advisor with the Random Source block causes a segmentation violation
Description: A segmentation violation occurs when you try to use the Model Advisor to analyze a model containing a Random Source block.

Summary: Signal logging does not preserve Mux hierarchy
Description: Logging a bus containing a muxed signal preserves only the first element of the mux signal.
Workaround: Replace the Mux block with a Bus Creator block. In general, you should not use Mux blocks as Bus Creators. See bug report 250660 for more information.

Summary: Models with Sensoray 626 blocks hang during download
Description: Models that contain Sensoray 626 blocks hang when you build the target application and download it to the target PC.
Workaround: To work around this issue:    Quit MATLAB.   Rename the following files:adsensoray626.ccapsensoray626.cdisensoray626.censensoray626.cinitsensoray626.cin the directory $MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks   (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing  matlab   at the MATLAB command prompt.)  Rename the files to filename.c.old.  Rename the following file  $MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\include\s626core.c (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabat the MATLAB command prompt.)Rename the file to s626core.c.old. Download the attached files. Place the new files in the same directories mentioned in Steps 2 and 3, as appropriate.   Restart MATLAB.After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolbox

Summary: flatearthpoly discards parts of polygons
Description: Function flatearthpoly  can discard part of a NaN-separated polygon if does not turn clockwise and is not a simple closed curve, even if it does not wrap a pole. Users can easily create polygon parts having such parts and flatearthpoly  needs to preserve them in its output.
Workaround: In Mapping Toolbox Version 2.3 (R2006a), replace line 58 in the fileMATLABROOT/toolbox/map/map/flatearthpoly.m,[x,y]=mfwdtran(mstruct,lat,lon,[],'patch');with the following:[x,y]= pcarree(mstruct,lat,lon,'none','polyfwd');

Summary: Constructor MWCellArray(MWCharArray strings) returns a cell array with empty elements.
Description: The constructor MWCellArray(MWCharArray strings) returns a cell array but the elements are empty. MWCharArray strings= new MWCharArray(new string[] {"abc", "de", "fgh"}); MWCellArray cellArray= new MWCellArray(strings); for (int idx= 1; idx <= cellArray.NumberOfElements; idx++)   {       Console.Writeline           ("cellArray[{0}]: {1}\n", idx, (MWCharArray)cellArray[idx]));   }
Workaround: To workaround this bug, create an empty cell array and then use array indexing to set the value of each element in the array.  For example, the previous example could be rewritten as follows: string[] strings= new string[] {"abc", "de", "fgh"}; int numStrings= strings.Length;  MWCellArray cellArray= new MWCellArray(numStrings);  for (int idx= 1; idx <= numStrings; idx++)    {      cellArray[idx]= (MWCharArray)(strings[idx-1]);   } for (int idx= 1; idx <= numStrings; idx++)    {      Console.WriteLine       ("cellArray[{0}]: {1}\n", idx, (MWCharArray)cellArray[idx]);    }

Summary: Inaccuracy in nctcdf function for larger parameter values
Description: The output from nctcdf  can be incorrect for very large values of the nu  and delta  parameters. For example, in the following plot the curve should increase smoothly from 0 to 1. Instead, it jumps suddenly from 0 to 1 and back.x = linspace(0,60);plot(x,nctcdf(x,500,55))
Workaround: No workarounds for versions of the Statistics Toolbox prior to 5.3 (R2006b).

Summary: The UI for MATLAB Builder for .NET 2.0 does not allow you to add more than 2 classes to a saved project.
Description: If you save a MATLAB Builder for .NET 2.0 (R2006a) project with at least 2 classes and then reopen it and try to add more classes to it, you get the following error in the MATLAB window and no class is added to the project:??? Error using ==> vertcatCAT arguments dimensions are not consistent.Error in ==> basicui>addclass at 325classes = unique([classes;{classname}]);??? Error while evaluating javax.swing.JButton ActionPerformedCallback
Workaround: Rename <matlabroot>\toolbox\compiler\basicui.m to <matlabroot>\toolbox\compiler\basicui.m_oldDownload the attaced file to <matlabroot>\toolbox\compiler directoryTo use the original projects that you created do the following:Open the project.Toggle the verbose settings for the project.Save and close the project.Reopen the project.You should now be able to add more than 2 classes to your projects.Note: This workaround is to be used with MATLAB Builder for .NET 2.0 (R2006a) and prior releases of the product. With MATLAB Builder for .NET 2.1 (R2006b) the GUI has changed and this workaround is not applicable.

Summary: Incorrect decision variable values in the output function for fminsearch
Description: In the fminsearch  function, the values of the decision variable x  passed into the output function are incorrect. The values in x  correspond to the last added point of the simplex but should correspond to the minimum function value.

Summary: Segment violation occurs if undefined variables are used in edit boxes of specific C2000 lib blocks' masks
Description: When edit boxes of specific blocks in C2000 library contain undefined variables, and you click OK on the block mask, a segment violation is issued on the MATLAB command window. Here is a list of blocks where this type of error occurs:C280x DSP Chip Support Library: ePWM, eQEP C281x DSP Chip Support Library: PWM, QEP C24xx DSP Chip Support Library: C24x PWM, C24xQEP
Workaround: Close MATLAB Browse to the following directory: [matlabroot]\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000blks Backup these six files by renaming them:c24xpwm.mexw32c24xqep.mexw32c28xpwm.mexw32c28xqep.mexw32c280xpwm.mexw32c280xqep.mexw32 Copy the six attached files (updated versions of the renamed files) into the following directory: [matlabroot]\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000blks  Open MATLAB At the command prompt, type: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: dicomwrite errors when the parameter 'CreateMode' is set to 'Create'
Description: When dicomwrite  is called with the parameter 'CreateMode'  set to the value 'Create' , it results in an error.In releases R14 and R14SP1, the error generated is: ??? Error using ==> dicomwrite>write_messageUnsupported value for CreateMode In releases R14SP2 and later, the error is: ??? Undefined function or variable "SOP_UID".
Workaround: To workaround this issue, use a lower-case "c" when specifying 'create' as the value of the 'CreateMode', option. For example,dicomwrite(....,'CreateMode','create');

Summary: Rules with MATLAB functions cause an error during dimensional analysis
Description: A Rule  that contains MATLAB commands can cause an error when dimensional analysis is on.  For example, if a model contains a rate rule with the MATLAB command exp , dimensional analysis displays an error. x = -20 * y * exp(-y^2)  This is not an issue if you are not using units.
Workaround: Turn off dimensional analysis.In the SimBiology desktop, Project Explorer: Expand Simulation and Analysis. Double click Simulation Settings to open the Simulation Settings pane. Click the Solver tab. Clear the DimensionalAnalysis checkbox.  At the command line:In the MATLAB Command Window,type cs = getconfigset(mymodel, ˇ®activeˇŻ);set(cs.CompileOptions, ˇ®DimensionalAnalysisˇŻ, false);

Summary: Help broken for DSP/BIOS Library blocks and C6000 DSP Core Support Library block
Description: For some of the blocks in the product block libraries, the links to block help are broken. This includes both the Help button in the block dialog box (where applicable) and the Help item in the context menu that appears when you right-click the block icon in its library.  Broken help links affect the following blocks:   Hardware Interrupt block in the DSP/BIOS libraryHardware Interrupt block in the C6000 DSP Core Support library
Workaround: To view the help topics for these blocks, open the online Help system and locate the block help.  Select Help > Full Product Family Help from the MATLAB menu bar to open the Help browser.  In the Contents tree of the Help Navigator, scroll to Embedded Target for TI C6000? DSP and double-click on the name to expand the entry. Click BlocksˇŞBy Category or BlocksˇŞAlphabetical List. If you clicked BlocksˇŞBy Category, click the appropriate block category on the BlocksˇŞBy Category list on the right and then click the block name to open the help for the block. If you clicked BlocksˇŞAlphabetical List, click the block name on the list on the right to open the block help.

Summary: Integrator block with level-reset option does not reset when reset signal falls to zero
Description: In releases before Simulink 6.3, an Integrator block with the level-reset option enabled resets when the reset signal is nonzero or  changes from nonzero in the previous time step to zero in the current time step. Beginning in Simulink 6.3, the Integrator block resets only when the reset signal is nonzero. This prevents resumption of integration with a new initial condition at the current time step.

Summary: dicomanon strips the attribute "ReferringPhysicianName"
Description: dicomanon  removes the type 2 attribute "ReferringPhysicianName".  According to the DICOM standard, type 2 attributes must be present, though they can be empty.

Summary: depfun function is not listed in Unsupported Functions table in MATLAB Compiler documentation
Description: The depfun  function is not deployable and should be listed as such in the MATLAB Compiler documentation.

Summary: Accessing scalar global variables in nested functions
Description: Accessing scalar global variables in nested functions sometimes gives incorrect results. The following MATLAB-file should return a 1 and then a 2 in xyz . Because of this bug however, it returns a 1 and then a 1:    function test_runner()      global xyz;      xyz = 0;      test2();      test2();   end   function test2()      global xyz;      xyz = xyz+1;      test_inner();      function test_inner()         xyz   % should be 1 then 2      end   end  Now run the program:    test_runner   xyz =        1   xyz =        1
Workaround: Either avoid using scalar global variables in nested functions, or turn off the JIT in the outer function using the statement "feature scopedAccelEnablement off". Running the same MATLAB-file, but the JIT disabled, returns the expected results:   function test_runner()      global xyz;      feature scopedAccelEnablement off      xyz = 0;      test2();      test2();   end   function test2()      global xyz;      xyz = xyz+1;      test_inner();      function test_inner()         xyz   % should be 1 then 2      end   end Now run the program:   test_runner   xyz =        1   xyz =        2

Summary: MVTCDF errors out under some circumstances for 3D integrals
Description: If you call MVTCDF to compute more than one integral (i.e., X or XL and XU have more than one row) in three dimensions, and one of the integration limits is [0,0,0], MVTCDF errors out.
Workaround: Change line 318 of MVTCDF from   p2(i) = (hiLimit - loLimit)*T(b1,nu); funevals = 0;to   p2(i) = (hiLimit - loLimit)*T(b1,nu(i)); funevals = 0;

Summary: cpsd produces conjugate of expected result
Description: The cpsd  of two vectors x  and y  produces the FFT of y  times the conjugate of the FFT of x . It should produce the FFT of x  times the conjugate of the FFT of y .
Workaround: Conjugate the output of cpsd to get the correct result.

Summary: Navigating from DOORS to Simulink causes DMI error
Description: There is an incompatibility between Simulink Verification and Validation and:     Telelogic DOORS version 8.1     Telelogic DOORS version 8.0 with Patch 004 or later  This incompatibility prevents navigation from DOORS to Simulink.? If you attempt to navigate from DOORS to Simulink by selecting a requirement in DOORS and then selecting the MATLAB > Select Item  menu option, DOORS displays the following error message: DMI Error : Failed to connect to Matlab. Would you like to try again?
Workaround: Download the attached version of the dmi.inc file that matches your MATLAB release and use it to replace the corresponding file in your MATLAB installation. Here are the steps:    Quit MATLAB and DOORS.    Rename the following file:        MATLAB\toolbox\slvnv\reqmgt\dmi.inc        where MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine. To locate your MATLAB root directory, type matlabroot at the MATLAB prompt.    Rename the file as dmi.inc.old.    Download the version of the attached file that corresponds to your MATLAB release. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in Step 2, and rename the file as dmi.inc.    Restart MATLAB.    After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:        rmi setup        Select the affected version of DOORS when the setup procedure prompts you to do so.    Restart DOORS. You should now be able to navigate from DOORS to Simulink.

Summary: Frequent short acquisitions on National Instruments Devices can time out
Description: The following can occasionally time out unexpectedly on some computers during the wait  function:ai = analoginput('nidaq', 'Dev1');addchannel(ai,0);ai.SamplesPerTrigger = 200;ai.SampleRate = 70000;for k = 1:200    start(ai)    wait(ai,5);end
Workaround: This bug is the result of a race condition between the threads during the cleanup at the end of the acquisition. The software does not finish stopping before being asked to start again. A simple work-around is to add a call to pause for .2 seconds just before the call to start. This will allow the acquisition to come to a complete halt before the next start.Modifying the code above:ai = analoginput('nidaq', 'Dev1');addchannel(ai,0);ai.SamplesPerTrigger = 200;ai.SampleRate = 70000;for k = 1:200    pause(.2)  %%

Summary: Editing certain very large files can cause MATLAB to disappear
Description: When M-Lint runs as part of the MATLAB editor, certain very large or complicated files can cause a stack overflow exception.  Unfortunately, it is impossible to recover from this error, and MATLAB goes away. To cause this problems, the files need not only be large (typically megabytes in size) but also to stress some aspect of MATLAB (for example, a two dimensional array with rows that are 50,000 elements long, or 1500 IF/ELSEIF/...  statements).
Workaround: Reducing the complexity of the file by using multiple files is the simplest workaround for this problem.  Also, turning off M-Lint in the editor using the preferences section can avoid the problem.Note that this problem may persist--if MATLAB tries to come up with the large file in the editor it may crash again and again.  In this case, temporarily moving or removing the file may be necessary.

Summary: PWM blocks reset their timer period and duty cycle values to default settings
Description: In some cases, PWM blocks for c280x, c281x, and c24xx may experience an undesired reset of the timer period and the duty cycle values to their default settings.
Workaround: Close MATLAB Browse to the following directory:[matlabroot]\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000blks Backup these three files by copying them to a different folder:c280xdspchiplib.mdlc281xdspchiplib.mdlc2400dspchiplib.mdl Copy these three attached files into the \tic2000blks directory:c2400dspchiplib_patch.mdlc280xdspchiplib_patch.mdlc281xdspchiplib_patch.mdlOpen MATLABAt the command prompt, type: rehash toolboxcacheBrowse to the following directory:[matlabroot]\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000blks Open the three following libraries by typing their names at the command prompt:c280xdspchiplibc281xdspchiplibc2400dspchiplib   From the library dialog, unlock each of the libraries by clicking on Edit > Unlock Library Delete the PWM block found in each opened libraryOpen the three patch libraries that you just copied by typing their names at the command prompt:c280xdspchiplib_patchc281xdspchiplib_patchc2400dspchiplib_patch   Copy the PWM block found in each of the patched libraries to the corresponding library Right-click on the copied PWM block; under Link Options click Disable LinkSave and close the libraries At the command prompt, type: rehash toolboxcacheManually update your models with the new block from the DSP chip libraries

Summary: analyze75info and analyze75read functions fail when the specified file is not in the current directory or on the MATLAB path
Description: When the analyze75info  and analyze75read  functions are provided with the complete path and filename of the header or image to be read, they return the following error if the specified file is not in the current working directory or on the MATLAB path: ??? Error using ==> medformats\private\isanalyze75>hdrFileOpenFile does not exist.

Summary: Trying to read in JCAMP-DX files with JCAMPREAD produces an error.
Description: When trying to read some JCAMP-DX files, JCAMPREAD fails, displaying an error message similar to the one below: ??? Trouble reading floating point number from file (row 2, field 1) ==> , 51.3, 25.704225, 52.3, 17 Error in ==> strread at 51 [varargout{1:nlhs}]=dataread('string',varargin{:}); Error in ==> jcampread>peakRead at 224 [xi yi] = strread(data,'%f, %f','delimiter',';'); Error in ==> jcampread>ntupleRead at 459 dataBlock = peakRead(fid,DataSpec); Error in ==> jcampread at 166 dataBlocks = ntupleRead(fid);
Workaround: Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\jcampread.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\jcampread.m.old. Copy the attached file to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\jcampread.m

Summary: TABULATE reference page corrected to describe inputs with integer values
Description: Since R14, the tabulate  function has accepted text, integer, and non-integer inputs. When the input consists of positive integers, the output includes counts for all integers between 1 and the maximum input value. The count is 0 for an integer in this range that does not appear in the input. When the input consists of large  positive integers, the output includes a large number of 0 counts. This behavior has been maintained for backwards compatibility. Before R2006a, the MATLAB-file help incorrectly stated that the output included only non-zero counts. In R2006a, the help text was corrected, but the reference page in the documentation was not.
Workaround: To avoid 0 counts, convert positive integer input x to a column vector and use tabulate(num2str(x)).

Summary: Build information method packNGo fails when invoked after closing model
Description: If the RTW.BuildInfo  method packNGo  is invoked outside of a post code generation command, and the model to which it applies is closed, an error will occur. For example, the following MATLAB command sequence produces the message "??? Error using ==> get_param Invalid Simulink object handle ": >> open rtwdemo_buildinfo>> rtwbuild(gcs)>> bdclose all>> cd rtwdemo_buildinfo_grt_rtw>> load buildInfo.mat>> packNGo(buildInfo, {'fileName', 'testFlat.zip'})
Workaround: To work around this bug, invoke packNGo using a post code generation command. For example, issue a command similar to the following and then rebuild the model:set_param('rtwdemo_buildinfo', 'PostCodeGenCommand', 'packNGo(buildInfo);')

Summary: Workers reregister as idle during task evaluation
Description: MDCE workers reregister as idle during task evaluation, even though there are no error messages in the log files.
Workaround: To work around this problem, Stop and uninstall the mdce service on every computer in the cluster. Make sure that no job managers or workers are running; verify with nodestatus. Exit the MATLAB client as well.Delete the log and checkpoint directories on the cluster computers. By default, these are under C:\Temp\MDCE on Windows, and under /var/lib/mdce and /varlog/mdce on UNIXOn every client and cluser computer, rename the file matlabroot/java/jar/toolbox/distcomp.jarto distcomp.jar_OLDUnzip the attached attachment_318491_20060801.zip which contains the files distcomp.jar_2.0 and distcomp.jar_2.0.1.If you are running DCT and MDCE version 2.0, save the distcomp.jar_2.0 file to matlabroot/java/jar/toolbox/ and rename it as distcomp.jar.If you are running DCT and MDCE version 2.0.1, save the distcomp.jar_2.0.1 file to matlabroot/java/jar/toolbox/ and rename it as distcomp.jar.Start the mdce service, job managers, and workers on the cluster computers.Start a MATLAB client and use the findResource command to find the job manager and confirm that all the workers have successfully registered with it.

Summary: Why does MATLAB crash after calling IMAQRESET in a GUI callback?
Description: I have a GUI which displays a preview image from my camera. I have a callback which stops the preview and resets the Image Acquisition Toolbox.  My callback has the following code:     stoppreview(handles.vid);    imaqreset; No error is displayed and no error log is created.
Workaround: It is not safe to call imaqreset from within a callback. To clean up the videoinput object, use the delete function instead:stoppreview(handles.vid);delete(handles.vid);

Summary: Code generation for TI C672x processors is broken
Description: Code generation for C672x processor-based platforms is broken. C672x platforms require DSP/BIOS version 5.20 (or newer) DSP/BIOS. In the R2006a release, Target for TI C6000 supports Code Composer Studio 3.1 with DSP/BIOS 4.90 only.  Therefore, code generation with DSP/BIOS fails. In addition, C672x processors are based on a new C67+ core that requires a new separate chip support library (CSL) installation. The new CSL API differs from the CSL API for earlier C67xx platforms. As a result of the API changes, non-DSP/BIOS code generation fails.

Summary: Hysteresis MAT files saved in SI units do not work in Transformer blocks
Description: If you used the Hysteresis design tool of Powergui and saved a Hysteresis characteristic in SI units in a MAT file, and then try to use it to define a hysteresis in one of the Transformer blocks of powerlib, you get an error similar to the following: Error in 'power_transfohyst/150 MVA 289 // 133 kV': Initialization commands cannot be evaluated.  MATLAB error message: Output argument "sfun_InitialFlux" (and maybe others) not assigned during call to "C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2006a\toolbox\physmod\powersys\powersys\private\inithysteresis.p (inithysteresis)".
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached version of the inithysteresis.p file. Here are the steps: Quit MATLAB.Rename the following file:$MATLAB\toolbox\physmod\powersys\powersys\private\inithysteresis.p(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabrootat the MATLAB Command Prompt.) Rename the file to inithysteresis.p.oldDownload the attached file. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2. Restart MATLAB. After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB Command Prompt: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Simulink hangs when simulating a model that references a library block containing a Signal and Scope Manager generator or viewer.
Description: Simulink 6.3 (R14SP3) will fail (hang) and issues the following warning if SSMgr generator or viewer connections are made when the model is a library:Caught unexpected exception of unknown type However, Simulink will not hang when connecting a SSMgr viewer or generator from a non-library model into a library block.
Workaround: Do not use Signal & Scope Manager (SSMgr) connections in libaries.

Summary: DAQCard-AI-16E-4 is not supported with Real-Time Windows Target 2.6.1 (R2006a)
Description: In Real-Time Windows Target 2.6.1 (R2006a), the driver for DAQCard-AI-16E-4 is not available. If I clone any block from the Real-Time Windows Target and click 'Install new hardware' from the Dialog Parameters of the block, I do not see the driver for the card. The information at:     https://www.mathworks.com/products/rtwt/supportedio5835.html indicates that the driver should be available.
Workaround: This bug exists only in Real-Time Windows Target 2.6.1 (R2006a). To workaround the bug, download the attached driver file to the following location:    $MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\rtwin\drv\National_Instrumentswhere $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory obtained by typing    matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. After downloading, type:    rehash toolboxat the MATLAB command prompt. The driver will then be available

Summary: dicomwrite errors with message about missing VR
Description: A certain combination of DICOM metadata attributes can cause the dicomwrite  function to produce this error: ??? Error using ==> images\private\dicom_add_attrAttribute (0028,0108) requires a VR argument.
Workaround: When writing DICOM files with "explicit value representation" (explicit VR), the dicomwrite function must choose some VR values based on other metadata values.  In this case dicomwrite is missing the rules to choose the VR.Update the following two lines in $toolbox/images/medformats/private/dicom_modules.m        0, '0028', '0108', '3',  {}, {}, {}        0, '0028', '0109', '3',  {}, {}, {}with these two lines        0, '0028', '0108', '3',  {'(0028,0103)', {0, 'US'}, {1, 'SS'}}, {}, {}        0, '0028', '0109', '3',  {'(0028,0103)', {0, 'US'}, {1, 'SS'}}, {}, {}

Summary: Generated MATLAB-file in FDATool reorders when the Reordering option is set to none
Description: If you use Filter Design and Analysis Tool (FDATool) to scale a second-order sections (SOS) filter, and then you generate a MATLAB-file for the filter, the generated code reorders the filter sections. The reorder process appears in the MATLAB-file even when you set the Reordering  option to none  in the Reordering and Scaling of Second-Order Sections dialog box before you generate the MATLAB-file for the filter.

Summary: Printing figure windows to certain paper formats can cause IllegalArgumentException
Description: Printing a figure window to certain paper formats (such as A4) can fail when printing via File -> Print ; the following Java exception displayed in the MATLAB Command Window:java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: X dimension > Y dimensionat javax.print.attribute.standard.MediaSize.(Unknown Source)at com.mathworks.page.export.printdlg.Printdlg.initPrintDialogSettings(Printdlg.java:241)at com.mathworks.page.export.printdlg.Printdlg.setVisible(Printdlg.java:446)
Workaround: You can use any of the following workaounds:Use the print command from the MATLAB command prompt. For more information regarding this function, enter  doc printat the MATLAB Command Prompt.Change the PaperType property of the figure to a value that does not cause the error, for example, use the command:set(gcf,'PaperType','USletter')

Summary: Calling datestr with a pivot year always returns the current year
Description: When you call datestr  with a format string (or equivalent format number) argument, MATLAB incorrectly interprets that argument to be the format of the input, not the output.  This results in various inconsistent behaviors, depending on the exact format and date strings used.
Workaround: For example, instead of calling     datestr('Mar-03-43','yyyy', 1900)use    datestr(datenum('Mar-03-43', 1900), 'yyyy')

Summary: The Multimeter block can not display certain output types
Description: There is a bug in SimPowerSystem 2.3 (R13) in the way that the Multimeter block uses output type parameter.  When you are doing a phasor simulation and select an Output Type  other than Complex  or Magnitude  from the SimPowerSystems > Measurements > Multimeter block dialog box, you get errors. If Real-Imag  is selected, the following error message is displayed and the selection reverts to Complex  when you open the dialog box the next time: %%%BEGIN ERROR%%% Error using ==> set_param Invalid setting in MultimeterPSB block (mask) 'Multimeter' for parameter 'Outputtype'. %%%END ERROR%%% If Magnitude-Angle  is selected, you receive the following error messages when you update or run the model: %%%BEGIN ERROR%%% Error in port widths or dimensions. Output port 1 of block 'ˇ­/Multimeter/source' has 24 elements. This port does not accept the dimensions (or orientation) specified by the output signal Error in port widths or dimensions. Input port 1 of block 'ˇ­/Multimeter/selection' has 312 elements. This port does not accept the dimensions (or orientation) specified by the input signal %%%END ERROR%%%
Workaround: To work around this issue in SimPowerSystems 3.0 (R13SP1) and later versions, set the Output Type to Magnitude or Complex.If you wish to output the values in Real-Imagor Magnitude-Angle form, you can use the Current Measurement, Voltage Measurement, or Impedence Measurement blocks located in the SimPowerSystems > Measurements library.To work around this issue in SimPowerSystems 2.3 (R13), you can download a revised version of the buildmultimeterinfo.m MATLAB that fixes the problem. Here are the required steps:1. Quit MATLAB.2. Rename the following MATLAB-file:$MATLAB\toolbox\powersys\powersys\private\buildmultimeterinfo.m(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine)Rename the file to buildmultimeterinfo.m.old or whatever you wish.3. Download the following file from the bottom of this web pagebuildmultimeterinfo.mand place it in the same directory mentioned in step 2.4. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Stateflow Symbol Wizard UI has clipped text in Japanese.
Description: The text for the UncheckAll  button in the Stateflow Symbol Wizard UI is clipped on Japanese machines.

Summary: Cannot copy a line containing a UIContextmenu and paste it into its orignal axes
Description: If you copy a line with an attached UIContextmenu and paste it into the axes it came from, you get the following error:??? Index exceeds matrix dimensions. Error in ==> scribeccp>pasteLine at 413     uic = struct2handle(serialized.ContextMenu, ancestor(obj(1), 'figure')); Error in ==> scribeccp>ccpPaste at 318         obj = pasteLine(ss, selAxes); Error in ==> scribeccp at 49     ccpPaste(scribeax); Error in ==> D:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006a\toolbox\matlab\graph2d\plotedit.p>scribe_paste_cb at 1619 scribeccp(fig, 'paste') ??? Error while evaluating uimenu Callback

Summary: Application hangs when using deployprint in a GUI created with GUIDE
Description: Complex (multiwindow) GUIs generated by the MATLAB Compiler on Windows XP may hang when printing one or more of their windows. This is caused by a bug in deployprint , which was being passed the incorrect figure number.
Workaround: There is no workaround for this problem.

Summary: Cannot see text on some controls (Macintosh only)
Description: Under some circumstances, you cannot read text in a table using the popup menu in a cell.  If an item is selected from the popup menu, the text color in the popup menu turns white and is very faint or cannot be seen at all. Similarly, under some circumstances, only the selected item in the list of species in the reaction builder dialog is visible.  You can select other species in the list, but anything not selected appears white on a white background.
Workaround: Download and unzip the attached archive.  Move the 'com' folder from the unzipped archive to $MATLABROOT/java/patch/.

Summary: Problems with changing current working directory using a shared library.
Description: The code used to initialize a compiler-created shared library may not be able to find the associated CTF file, even if the file is in the same directory as the DLL/Shared Library file and the shared library has been correctly loaded.  If the directory containing the CTF is not on the library search path (PATH on Windows, LD_LIBRARY_PATH on Solaris/Linux, DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH on Mac OS) then the CTF will not be found and extracted.
Workaround: Make sure that the directory containing the CTF is on the library search path at the time when the compiler shared library initialization routines are called.

Summary: Signal data type conversions: temporary variable of type double generated when single-precision signal is converted to type integer
Description: Real-Time Workshop generates a temporary variable of type double in generated code for conversions of single-precision signals to type integer. In some cases, the double-precision temporary variable is not needed and can be replaced by a single-precision variable.
Workaround: Do both of the following: Select the Saturate on integer overflow block parameter for the block that has the source signal being converted Clear the Optimization > Remove code from floating-point to integer conversions that wrap out-of-range values option on the Configuration Parameters dialog box for the containing model

Summary: Demos do not run properly on big-endian hardware when target is not connected
Description: When you try to run a demo on target hardware that uses big-endian mode, the demo fails if the target is not connected before you run the demo.Link for CCS cannot determine the endianness of big-endian hardware if you create a CCS object before you connect the target hardware, which the demos try to do. This causes the demos to fail with an error message.

Summary: Cannot use fixdt to set data type of a bus element
Description: You cannot use the fixdt  function to set the DataType  property of an element of a Simulink.Bus  object.
Workaround: Use a Simulink.NumericType object to set the data type of the Simulink.Bus element.

Summary: Bug in arrayfun with complex outputs.
Description: When the output of arrayfun  can be complex, but the first instance of the output is real, and the UniformOutput  flag is either true  or unset, arrayfun  fails with a SegV error. For example, the following code produces such an error:   in = [.5, -.5]   out = arrayfun(@(x)(sqrt(x)), in)
Workaround: You can work around this problem by doing one of the following:Make sure the first output is going to be complex.   in = [-.5, .5]   out = arrayfun(@(x)(sqrt(x)), in)Make the output a cell array.   in = [.5, -.5];   out = arrayfun(@(x)(sqrt(x)), in, 'UniformOutput', false);

Summary: Conditionally assigned variables sometimes appear uninitialized in nested functions
Description: In certain cases, a scalar variable that is initialized conditionally may show up as uninitialized in a nested function.  This will only occur if the parent function is being called recursively.  In the following example, the variable t  may be uninitialized in the call to function nest : function test  foo(1);  foo(0);endfunction foo(y)    if y        t=0;        r=nest;    else        foo(1);    end    function z=nest            z=t;    endend
Workaround: Type the following command at the MATLAB prompt:   feature jitallow fast_jit_call off;

Summary: Bad Bookmarks in RTF and WORD documents
Description: Report Generator may generate bad bookmarks for WORD and RTF outputs: [Error! Bookmark not defined.]
Workaround: Download the attached files execute.p, loop_makeSection.p, then follow these steps:    Quit MATLAB.   Rename the following files:  $MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rptgen/@rpt_looper/execute.p$MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rptgen/@rpt_looper/loop_makeSection.p (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB Command Prompt).  To: execute.p.old and  loop_makeSection.p.old.    Download the attached files. Place the new files in the directory mentioned above.   Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Tasks fail with "JVM appears hung" messages in mdce-service.log
Description: Tasks created with the Distributed Computing Toolbox might finish with an ErrorMessage indicating that the task was cancelled due to worker re-registration.If the task failures coincide with messages like the following in the mdce-service.log file on the worker computer, it is likely that the MATLAB Distributed Computing Engine is incorrectly attempting to restart some of its processes.ERROR  | wrapper  | 2005/10/26 21:40:42 | JVM appears hung: Timed out waiting for signal from JVM.ERROR  | wrapper  | 2005/10/26 21:40:42 | Java Virtual Machine did not exit on request, terminatedSTATUS | wrapper  | 2005/10/26 21:40:49 | Launching a JVM...INFO   | jvm 2    | 2005/10/26 21:41:07 | Wrapper (Version 3.0.5)
Workaround: Ensure that all the mdce services are stopped on all the machines, and that no MATLAB processes or MATLAB-related Java processes are running.Copy the file MATLABROOT/toolbox/distcomp/config/wrapper-phoenix.configtoMATLABROOT/toolbox/distcomp/config/wrapper-phoenix.config.orgInsert the following line at the bottom of the first file(wrapper-phoenix.config):  wrapper.ping.timeout=0Delete all checkpoint directories from your machines to ensure that the restart is clean.Restart the cluster from scratch using mdce start, startjobmanager, etc.

Summary: geotiffinfo causes a segmentation violation after interacting with libTIFF functions.
Description: When function geotiffinfo  is called twice after a function that requires only the libTIFF library, for example imwrite  or print  with TIFF options specified, then geotiffinfo  causes a segmentation violation on Linux platforms.
Workaround: After calling either imwrite or print with TIFF options specified and before calling function geotiffinfo, run the command clear mexFor a more permanent workaround, edit the function geotiffinfo.m.  In your MATLAB root directory, locate the file toolbox/map/mapformats/geotiffinfo.m and add the following line:  clear 'imagesci/private/wtifc' after:  clear 'imagesci/private/rtifc' or back up this file and replace it with version in the attachment.

Summary: For generated C6416 DSK projects the memory model option is set to ml3
Description: When you generate projects for your C6416 DSK, the memory model option is always set to ml3 . Project generation does not apply the settings in Simulation -> Configuration Parameters  correctly.

Summary: Inconsistency When Writing Formats That Don't End on Byte Boundary
Description: Writing out formats that do not end on a byte boundary can cause inconsistency in flushing.  For example, writing out 5 12-bit values and then rewinding and trying to read them can cause a spurious value to be stored at the end of the array.

Summary: Unexpected shape and size of point x in output function for fgoalattain and fminimax
Description: In the output function for fgoalattain  and fminimax , the point x  of decision variables1. Contains one extra element appended to the end of the column vector, and 2. Is always a column vector, irrespective of the shape of x  that you expect (e.g., a row vector or a matrix).
Workaround: You can work around these issues inside your output function by1. Removing the extra element from the column vector x, and2. Reshaping the expected shape of x to a column vector.function stop = myOutputFunction(x,optimValues,state)% First correct vector x:x = x(1:end-1); % remove extra elementx = reshape(x,nRows,nCols); % reshape x to a nRows-by-nCols array% Insert the remainder of your output function here

Summary: "Task name specified conflicts" error after 10,000 acquisitions with two NIDAQmx objects.
Description: When using National Instruments hardware that uses the NI-DAQmx interface, repeated data output on one device in the presence of a second object (such as an analog input object) results in the following error:Error/Warning messages (if any): ??? NIDAQmx error : Task name specified conflicts with an existing task name. Task Name: MWDAT0012 Status Code: -200089  This occurs after 10,000 calls to the putsample function. The following code demonstrates this issue:samplerate = 1000; triggerrepeat = 140;   ai = analoginput('nidaq','Dev1'); aichans = addchannel(ai,0); set(ai,'SampleRate',samplerate);  set(ai,'InputType','Differential','TriggerRepeat',triggerrepeat);  ao = analogoutput('nidaq','Dev1'); aochans = addchannel(ao,0); set(ao,'SampleRate',samplerate);   start(ai); for cyclenum = [1:10001],     putsample(ao,[1]); end
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached version of the DLL, MWNIDAQMX.DLL, using these steps:    Quit MATLAB.  Rename the following DLL: $MATLAB\toolbox\daq\daq\private\MWNIDAQMX.DLL (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing   matlabroot  at the MATLAB Command Prompt.) Rename the file to MWNIDAQMX.DLL.old.  Download the attached file, MWNIDAQMX.DLL. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2.  Restart MATLAB.  After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB Command Prompt:   rehash toolboxcache

Summary: COM object interfaces not visible when instantiating the object using actxserver command in MATLAB 7.2 (R2006a)
Description: You can instantiate a COM server in MATLAB using actxserver  commandh = actxserver('ProgramID') However, no output is returned when you query the object to return the interfaces using the commandh.Interfaces

Summary: Installing another product from the Physical Modeling family affects the SimHydraulics installation
Description: If you install another product from the Physical Modeling family, for example, SimMechanics, after installing SimHydraulics, you can no longer see the SimHydraulics library inside the Physical Networks library or run SimHydraulics demos. You will see the following error message:Can't load 'C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2006a\bin\win32\ne_mli.dll The reason is that other Physical Modeling products do not have the latest version of this .dll file, which is shipped with the R2006a+ Web release of SimHydraulics.This problem is also present if you install SimHydraulics and SimMechanics simultaneously, because these products are installed in alphabetical order.
Workaround: To work around this issue, first install all the other products you need, except SimHydraulics. Then install SimHydraulics separately.

Summary: Missing Input Argument in Generated MATLAB-File Function
Description: In versions of the Curve Fitting Toolbox before v.1.1.6 (R2006b), the MATLAB-file function generated from cftool  using File  > Generate MATLAB file  would sometimes fail to include an input argument for the response variable. The problem occurred when working with smoothed data for which the source data was no longer present.
Workaround: Edit the generated MATLAB file function to include an input argument for the response variable.

Summary: Segmentation violation occurs during code generation when using many global Data Store blocks
Description: A segmentation violation can occur when generating code with Real-Time Workshop if all of the following conditions are true: ?     The model uses more than 2000 global Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks with unique data store names.     The Data Store Read and Data Store Write names are specified as Simulink signal objects in the base workspace.     The model does not contain Data Store Memory blocks corresponding to the data store names.
Workaround: To avoid a segmentation violation:?    Do not use more than 2000 global Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks without corresponding Data Store Memory blocks.    When not sharing data using model referencing, insert a Data Store Memory block into the model for each data store name specified in the base workspace.

Summary: The arrow size cannot be changed for wormhole transitions
Description: When a transition has multiple wires (it is a wormhole transition), changing the ArrowSize  property does not work. The visual appearance of the arrow size remains the same.

Summary: bufferm errors out in cpuni
Description: Some input polygons cause bufferm  to error out in private function cpuni .
Workaround: In the newer version of bufferm.m in the attachment, the use of cpuni and other private functions for set operations on polygons are replaced by calls to polybool.  The new version is also an order of magnitude faster than the original.

Summary: dicomread does not read YCbCr data correctly
Description: dicomread  produces incorrect results when converting YCbCr samples to RGB values for certain images, for example, color images that use run-length encoded (RLE) compression.  While the color might appear to have the correct tone, the luminance (Y channel) can be incorrect.
Workaround: dicomread uses two different file parsers: a fast parser that exhibits this incorrect YCbCr to RGB translation and a slower parser that does not.  To get the correct YCbCr color conversion, use the following command:X = dicomread(filename, 'raw', 0);

Summary: SOFTSCOPE fails to start when using saved settings.
Description: I have set up some measurements and saved my settings. I then start SOFTSCOPE with these settings using the command:softscope('t1watch.si')   I then receive the error:??? Error using ==> softscope Java exception occurred: java.lang.NullPointerException at com.mathworks.toolbox.daq.scope.Scope.setObject(Scope.java:1085)
Workaround: This issue is fixed in R2006b. If you are using a previous version, there is no known work-around.

Summary: Roundoff errors in event times cause incorrect sequence of events or warnings
Description: Simulations in which you expect events to be simultaneous but not causally related to each other might experience roundoff errors in event times. As a result, you might observe unexpected sequencing or spurious messages about timing engine warnings. For example, you might expect a priority-1 event and a priority-2 event to occur simultaneously at T=8.8, but if the priority-2 event is actually scheduled for T=8.7999... due to roundoff error, then the application processes it first despite the higher value of the priority. Versions 1.0 and 1.1 consider events on the event calendar to be simultaneous only if they occur at the same  value of the simulation clock, yet computers' use of floating-point arithmetic involves a finite set of numbers with finite precision. If roundoff error is very small, then the event logging feature and scope blocks might not reveal enough precision to confirm whether events are simultaneous or only close. To help diagnose this problem in some models, you can use the Discrete Event Signal to Workspace block to collect relevant data in MATLAB and examine the times at which signals experience updates.

Summary: System error indicating file does not exist when using -a flag with mcc
Description: When you include an additional file in a compilation using the MCC -a  flag, MATLAB Compiler sometimes removes the first letter of the file name specified.  As a result the system attempts to find a file with an incorrect name and generates an error such as the following:System error text: 'No such file or directory'  For example when you issue the following commandmcc -m mymfile.m -a myotherfile the software changes myotherfile  to yotherfile , which does not exist.
Workaround: To work around this problem, prepend all file names specified with the -a option with ./ on UNIX, or .\ on Windows. For example, the following command does not generate the error:mcc -m mymfile.m -a ./myotherfile

Summary: Large constants in function file cause segmentation violations
Description: When an MATLAB-file creates certain large constants in a function file, e.g., via nan(100,100,100,100) , MATLAB fails to recover properly from an out-of-memory error.
Workaround: One possible workaround is to substitue a variable for one or more of the constants. For example: k = 100;nan(k,k,k,k)

Summary: Memory model options in TIC6000 Compiler Options do not map to the memory options available in TI C6000 compilers
Description: Starting with CCS 3.1 (code generation tools 5.1.0 and above), C6000 compilers generate trampolines  for function calls. Trampolines modify function calls at link time to reach destinations that would normally be too far away to reach with near calls. With trampolines, you no longer need to specify memory model options to generate far calls. With the addition of trampolines in function call generation, the following memory model options in TIC6000 Compiler Options  are deprecated:Option Description  Near_Calls_and_Data  All data and function calls default to near  Far_Aggregate_Data (-ml0)   Aggregate data (structs and arrays) default to far  Far_Calls (-ml1)   All function calls default to far  Far_Calls_and_Aggregate_Data (-ml2)   All aggregate data and function calls default to far  Far_Calls_and_Data (-ml3)   All data and function calls default to far   The memory model options above are replaced by the following choices:Option Description  Near_data (--mem_model:data=near)   Data accesses default to near   Far_data (--mem_model:data=far )   Data accesses default to far   Far_aggregate (--mem_model:data=far_aggregates)   Accesses to aggregate data default to far. Accesses to nonaggregate data default to near. This is the default compiler behavior.   To maintain backward compatibility, TIC6000 Code Generation tools still support the earlier -mln  options. Code you generated by specifying the old memory model options works as before.About Trampolines When a function call is more than +/- 1M instructions away from its destination, the linker generates an indirect branch (a trampoline) to that destination, and redirects the function call to point to the trampoline. Now, function calls branch to the trampoline, and then the trampoline branches to the final destination.

Summary: Publishing to Word produces an error if Notebook used in that session
Description: When you select File > Publish To > Word Document  in the Editor/Debugger, the following error appears if you also used Notebook in the session:  ??? Error using ==> codetools\private\mxdom2wordAttempt to reference field of non-structure array.Error in ==> publish at 195        mxdom2word(dom,outputAbsoluteFilename);Error in ==> mdbpublish at 43outputPath = publish(file, options);
Workaround: If you use Notebook and then want to publish to Word, close MATLAB and restart it before publishing.Alternatively, you can change a MATLAB MATLAB-file to prevent the problem. In matlabroot\matlab\codetools\private\mxdom2word.m, change the linedoc = documents.Add;to documents.Add;doc = documents.Item(documents.Count);Save the file. You might need to run clear functions for the change to take effect in the current session.

Summary: memmapfile cannot map large files on some Linux file systems
Description: On some Linux file systems (including ext3), the memmapfile  class fails to map files two gigabytes and larger. An error occurs the first time that you access the Data  field: ??? Cannot map file "/tmp/aBigFile": File too large.
Workaround: Installation instructions       Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.       Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.       Exit MATLAB.       Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.                     Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file, type the following in a shell:unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>                            Extract the files from the .zip file by typing the following in a shell:                            cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>              Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.
