Description: rmpath  cannot remove directories with leading or trailing spaces. For example: >> addpath('D:\Temp  ')>> pathMATLABPATHD:\TempD:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006a\toolbox\matlab\generalD:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006a\toolbox\matlab\opsD:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006a\toolbox\matlab\langD:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006a\toolbox\matlab\elmatD:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006a\toolbox\matlab\elfun                 .                 .                 .>> rmpath('D:\Temp  ')Warning: "D:\Temp  ;" not found in path.> In rmpath at 62>> pathMATLABPATHD:\TempD:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006a\toolbox\matlab\generalD:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006a\toolbox\matlab\opsD:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006a\toolbox\matlab\langD:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006a\toolbox\matlab\elmatD:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006a\toolbox\matlab\elfun                 .                 .                 .

Summary: Stateflow Note's font angle property doesn't work
Description: The Font.Angle  property for Stateflow Note objects returns the font's bold state rather than the angle.

Summary: datestr is off by one millisecond with the HH:MM:SS.FFF format
Description: Because of rounding error, the output of the millisecond format may be off by 1 millisecond.
Workaround: There are no workarounds for this bug.

Summary: GUIDE GUIs ignore the design time visibility setting of the GUI figure
Description: When you create a GUI in GUIDE, if you set the figure Visible  property to off  using the Property Inspector, this design time setting takes no effect when you run the GUI. It still shows on screen.
Workaround: The following workarounds work in MATLAB versions greater than or equal to 6.5, including the version 7.3 that contains the fix. Use one of the following approaches to control initial GUI visibility.If you want a GUI to be invisible when you start it, set its Visible property to off when you start it. For example, you would start a GUI called untitled like this:untitled('Visible','off');If an existing GUI no longer shows up and you want to make it visible when you start it, set its Visible property to on when you start it. For example:untitled('Visible','on');In the GUI MATLAB file opening function, set the GUI figure Visible property to on or off as appropriate.

Summary: Solvers ode45, ode23, and ode23s can miss integrator reset events when using integrator state ports
Description: Simulation results for models where the continuous intergrator reset signal is driven by the state of a continous integrator can be incorrect due to missing reset zero crossing events. This bug occurs only when using the following solvers: ode45, ode23, and ode23s.
Workaround: Change the solver to one of the following: ode113, ode15s, ode23t, or ode23tb.

Summary: Debug and Data Manipulation Tutorial demo stops during execution
Description: The demo program Debug and Data Manipulation Tutorial (ccstutorial ) stops at the following line:run(cc,'runtohalt',30); This happens because the program issues a restart(cc)  command just before the run  command. The command should be reload(cc)  rather than restart .

Summary: An atomic subsystem that contains a Level-2 MATLAB File S-Function can cause Simulink Accelerator to produce inconsistent results
Description: Simulink Accelerator can produce results that differ from those of normal simulation if the model being accelerated has the following characteristics: The model contains multiple instances of an atomic system. The atomic subsystem contains a Level-2 MATLAB-File S-Function. The S-function has no corresponding TLC file.
Workaround: Set the Real-Time Workshop system code option in the atomic subsystem's parameter dialog box to Inline or Function.

Summary: Problems with filter options in resizem
Description: Mapping Toolbox function resizem  has an unintended dependency on function hamming  in the Signal Processing Toolbox. When resampling at a lower point density and using bilinear or bicubic interpolation, resizem  low-pass filters its input to avoid anti-aliasing and calls the hamming  function in the process. resizem  fails to properly parse its 4-th argument when given input of the form:resizem(..., method, n) where n  is a scalar used to specify an n -by-n  window.
Workaround: In the version of resizem.m in the attachment, the Hamming window is computed by a subfunction of resizem itself and the input parsing problem has been fixed.

Summary: PROBESETLOOKUP returns empty identifier for some chips
Description: The function PROBESETLOOKUP returns empty identifiers for certain chips. In particular the new Human Genome chips including HG-U133A_2.
Workaround: Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\probesetlookup.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\probesetlookup.m.old. Copy the attached file to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\probesetlookup.m

Summary: Command line error when plotting timeseries with multiple columns and multiple events
Description: If a timeseries  object contains data with two or more columns and has two or more events, calling the plot method will result in the following command line error: ??? In an assignment A(I) = B, the number of elements in B and I must be the same. Error in ==> timeseries.plot at 33 plot(utgetobj(h), varargin{:});  The time series and events show up correctly on the plot, however.

Rename the following file to plot_old.m: $MATLAB\toolbox\matlab\timeseries\@tsdata\@timeseries\plot.m where $MATLAB is your MATLAB root directory (returned by the MATLABROOT function). 2. Move the file plot.m in the attached zip file into the same directory. 3. Re-launch MATLAB. 4. Update your toolbox directories by entering rehash toolboxcache at the MATLAB command prompt.

Summary: MATLAB 7.1 (R14SP3) fails to open some figure files saved in MATLAB 7.2 (R2006a)
Description: If I create a figure in MATLAB 7.2 (R2006a), rotate the axes using the rotate3d  tool and save the figure, I cannot open the saved figure in MATLAB 7.1 (R14SP3). When I attempt to do so, I receive the following error:  ??? Attempt to reference field of non-structure array. Error in ==> rotate3d>destroyRotaObj at 609     if ishandle(rdata.textBoxText) Error in ==> rotate3d>setState at 122         destroyRotaObj(rotaObj); Error in ==> rotate3d at 58             setState(arg,arg2,rotatestyle); Error in ==> hgload>figload_reset at 272 rotate3d(fig,'off'); Error in ==> hgload at 158             figload_reset(h(k));
Workaround: A bug in MATLAB 7.2 (R2006a) corrupts figures saved after the axes has been rotated using the ROTATE3D tool. Place the new file in the $MATLAB\toolbox\matlab\graph3d directory mentioned in step 2.Restart MATLABAfter restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB Command Prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Why do I get an error when I launch a model coverage report?
Description: When using a Simulink Element, if the Enable model coverage  checkbox is enabled, an error occurs when the Launch Model Coverage Report  link in the Run Status pane is clicked. The error occurs at the MATLAB command line and reads:  ??? Reference to non-existent element of a cell array.
Workaround: This error may be caused by one of two things: The Simulink Element's Inherit coverage metric settings from the model option is enabled, but the Simulink model does not have its Enable Coverage Reporting checkbox selected, which is available under the Simulink Tools > Coverage Settings... menu; or The Simulink model has no coverage information to report, which can be determined by running your model outside of SystemTest and ensuring coverage information is being reported.To avoid this error, make sure the Simulink model's coverage reporting option is turned on, and that coverage information is being reported.

Summary: Wrong version number reported for mcc -? (reports as 4.3 when should be 4.4)
Description: When you type mcc -?  within MATLAB or from system prompt, the compiler version is incorrectly displayed as version 4.3, as shown:>> mcc -?MCC Invoke MATLAB to C/C++ Compiler (Version 4.3)            INCORRECTMCC [-options] fun [fun2 ...]                                               ...  The correct version message display should be:MCC Invoke MATLAB to C/C++ Compiler (Version 4.4)  CORRECT

Summary: Error encountered when saving a SISO Design Tool session with multiple stored designs.
Description: While saving a SISO Design Tool session, an error is encountered when there exists multiple stored designs.
Workaround: To correct this error, copy the attached file into the directory listed below and overwrite the original file.  (matlabroot)/toolbox/control/ctrlguis/@controlnodes/@DesignSnapshotFolder

Summary: Application failure because of problems with array indexing
Description: Some applications that rely on indexing with MWCharArray, MWCellArray, and MWLogicalArray classes might fail. This problem occurs because of improper indexing when these specific MWArray classes set the value of an array element using the set value indexers. The problem does not occur when you use get value indexers. For example, if you use set value indexers as shown in the following code, the specified array element is not set by MATLAB Builder for .NET, and there is no indication of this error when the application is run. cellArray[1,2,1]= mwArray????????          problem      Code such as the following example using  get value indexers does not have the problem and will work as expected. mwArray= cellArray[1,2,1]????????          not a problem
Workaround: To work around this bug:Download a new .NET Builder data conversion assembly (MWArray.dll) from the Web.  The new assembly is attached to this CBR.Copy the new assembly to matlabroot/bin/win32 on machines that have MATLAB installed, or to mcrinstalldir/v74/runtime/win32 on machines that have only the MATLAB Component Runtime (MCR) installed.Start a Visual Studio or DOS window, cd to the directory containing the new assembly, and run the following command from an account having admin privileges:gacutil /I MWArray.dllIf the problem persists after you complete these steps, rebuild the .NET Builder component before running the client application that had the problem.

Summary: Misinterpretation of some tokens by REGEXP is possible
Description: When the symbol \1 appears in a regular expression, MATLAB interprets it as a reference to the first token found in the string. For example, an expression like '(ABC)\1' looks for a generated token 'ABC' that is immediately followed by a repeat of that same token (represented by the symbol \1). This would match 'ABCABC' in a string. In the example below, '(ack)\w\1' stands for the characters 'ack' followed by a single character and followed by 'ack' again. This finds a match in the word 'backpack'.    regexp('My backpack is heavy', '(ack)\w\1')   ans =        5 This all works as expected.  The bug reported here is that the next example also finds a match, but in this case, the match is erroneous:    regexp('My backp1 is heavy', '(ack)\w\1')   ans =        5 When regexp attempts to match the '1' (following the characters 'backp') in the string with the token reference '\1' in the expression, it fails (as expected). But then it tries again, this time reinterpreting the '\1' expression as the character '1' rather than as the token reference it truly is. This time there is a match and regexp unexpectedly returns the string index of 5. This behavior is not restricted to token references of '\1' as can be seen here:    regexp('My backp77 is heavy', '(ack)\w\77')   ans =        5
Workaround: As a workaround, in cases such as these, use named tokens instead of numeric tokens. For example, you can replace    regexp('My backp1 is heavy', '(ack)\w\1')with   regexp('My backp1 is heavy', '(?&ltstr&gtack)\w\k&ltstr&gt')Using named tokens, the first example succeeds and the second fails.  This is the desired result.   regexp('My backpack is heavy', '(?&ltstr&gtack)\w\k&ltstr&gt')   ans =        5   regexp('My backp1 is heavy', '(?&ltstr&gtack)\w\k&ltstr&gt')   ans =        []

Summary: Incorrect handling of continuous sample time specified for Rate Transition blocks
Description: The Rate Transition block allows users to specify a continuous sample time, e.g., by entering 0  or [0, 0]  as the value of the Output port sample time  parameter on its block parameter dialog box. However, the Rate Transition block is discrete; therefore, the block is incapable of generating data with a continuous sample time.
Workaround: If you are using a previous version, please read the following:When using a Rate Transition block to transfer data from the output of a block operating at a discrete rate to the input of another block operating at a continuous rate, specify the value of its Output port sample time parameter as [0 1] such that it updates values only at major time steps.

Summary: ConnectionCallback property not saved
Description: Saving a model does not save changes to the ConnectionCallback properties of the ports that it contains.

Summary: Models that contain National Instruments A/D blocks produce incorrect output when compiled with Open Watcom
Description: If you use the Open Watcom 1.3 compiler to compile a model that contains a National Instruments Analog Input block, the following error occurs: The output of a Analog Input block remains constant even if you connect an external analog source to the National Instruments board. In addition, the execution time for the block becomes much longer (on the order of 10-15 milliseconds).
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can either:Use a supported compiler other than Open Watcom to build the model.Follow the described procedure:Quit MATLABRename the following file: $MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\adnipcie.c(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typingmatlabrootat the MATLAB Command Prompt.)Rename the file to adnipcie.c.old.Rename the following TMF file:$MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\xpc_watc.tmfRename the file to xpc_watc.tmf.old.Unzip the attached file. Place the new files in the same directories mentioned in steps 2 and 3. Restart MATLAB.In the directory in which you are building your model, delete the old $MODEL_xpc_rtw directory (where $MODEL is the name of your model).Rebuild the model.

Summary: Relative pathnames in addpath may confuse which and Profiler
Description: Using relative pathnames when adding directories to the MATLAB search path has been seen to cause errors that can affect both the which function and the MATLAB Profiler. This occurs only when the relative pathname contains the .. symbol. An example of adding this type of relative pathname is as follows:    addpath ..\..\dir1\dir2 This bug exists in MATLAB version 7.2, and is fixed in version 7.3. Effect on the which Function If you call the which function on an MATLAB-file that resides in a directory you have added to the MATLAB path using a relative filename, MATLAB may be unable to locate the file. This is true whether you added the directory to the path in this session or in a previous one. Whether or not the command fails depends on how much of the pathname you specify when calling which. For example, if you add a directory called my_mfiles to the MATLAB search path using the command    addpath ..\..\testdir\my_mfiles and then call which on a function MATLAB  file find_average.m that resides in the my_mfiles directory,     which ..\..\testdir\my_mfiles\find_average MATLAB may be unable to locate the file. However, if you only use that portion of the pathname that was specified as absolute in the addpath command, then which succeeds in finding the file. The following commands operate successfully:    which find_average   which my_mfiles\find_average   which testdir\my_mfiles\find_average while these commands may fail:    which matlab\testdir\my_mfiles\find_average   which ..\testdir\my_mfiles\find_average   which ..\..\testdir\my_mfiles\find_average Effect on the MATLAB Profiler If you run the MATLAB Profiler on a function that was added to the search path using a relative pathname, MATLAB may be unable to open the file and thus throws an error. For example, if function find_average resides in directory my_mfiles, and you added my_mfiles to the MATLAB search path with the command    addpath ..\..\my_mfiles You might get an error like the following from MATLAB if you try to profile the find_average function:    ??? Error using ==> find_average   Cannot find function "..\..\my_mfiles\find_average.m".
Workaround: Do not use relative pathnames that include the .. symbol when adding directories to the search path.

Summary: Demos configured incorrectly for code generation
Description: Many SimMechanics demo models are incorrectly configured for RTW code generation. In these models, the Real-Time Workshop  node of Configuration Parameters  shows the System target file  set to grt.tlc , but the Description  states Rapid Simulation Target . Attempting to generate code for these models causes an error.
Workaround: To correctly configure these demos for code generation, go to the Real-Time Workshop node of Configuration Parameters and manually select the desired System target file (for example, grt.tlc). This step correctly configures the code-generation settings, and the Description field will then match the System target file.

Summary: Publishing to Word errors when normal.dot template contains "output" or "MATLAB code" styles
Description: When you publish a MATLAB file to Microsoft Word and the normal.dot  template contains "output" or "MATLAB code" styles, an error similar to the following appears:  Error in ==> codetools\private\mxdom2word at 40style = wordApplication.ActiveDocument.Styles.Add('output');
Workaround: Modify the MATLAB-file in matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\codetools\private\mxdom2word.m by changing these lines:     style = wordApplication.ActiveDocument.Styles.Add('MATLAB code');to    try        style = wordApplication.ActiveDocument.Styles.Add('MATLAB code');    endand    style = wordApplication.ActiveDocument.Styles.Add('output');to     try        style = wordApplication.ActiveDocument.Styles.Add('output');    end

Summary: dicomwrite returns Index exceeds matrix dimensions error for certain attribute sequences
Description: The dicomwrite  function fails if you pass it a metadata structure that contains a sequence of attributes and one of the nested attributes has an item field with no fields beneath it ˇŞ for example, if the value of metadata.ReferringPhysicianSequence.Item_1  is empty.

Summary: Model Coverage generates errors if Simulink Verification and Validation license is unavailable
Description: Suppose a model is characterized by all of the following: A Stateflow chart is present. Signal logging is enabled for fixed-point signals. Model Coverage is enabled.  That is, the RecordCoverage  parameter is set to on  or, equivalently, the Enable Coverage Reporting  option is selected on the Coverage Settings dialog box.  If a Simulink Verification and Validation license is unavailable when simulating such a model, Simulink reports an error that begins as follows: Simulink Verification and Validation License check out failed. ??? One or more output arguments not assigned during call to "cv". Error in ==> simcoverage\private\report_create_structured_data at 107         [cmplx_ismodule,cmplx_shallow,cmplx_deep] = cv('MetricGet',cvId,cycloEnum, ...
Workaround: Disable Model Coverage for the model by entering the following command at the MATLAB prompt:set_param('modelName','RecordCoverage','off')where modelName is the name of your model.

Summary: In Hysteresis Transformer Design Tool, you cannot save model to a path with spaces
Description: The Hysteresis Transformer Design Tool cannot save hysteresis model to a path with spaces.For example, if you try to save to D:\Work\test spaces\hysteresis.mat, you get the error:Error using ==> saveVariable 'spaces\hysteresis.mat' not found.
Workaround: Save your hysteresis model in a directory with no spaces in the path.

Summary: Context-sensitive help links broken for some C6000 blocks
Description: For some of the blocks in the DM642 EVM Board Support Library (dm642evmlib ) of the Embedded Target for TI C6000, help links to the doc are broken. In addition, for UDP Send and UDP Receive blocks, the help links point to the wrong product help. This includes both the Help button in the block's dialog box (where applicable) and the Help item in the context menu that appears when you right-click on the block icon in its library. Here is a complete list of blocks with broken links to help:  Raw Capture (also called Video Port)GPIO Read GPIO Write IP ConfigUDP SendUDP ReceiveAudio DAC  Audio ADC
Workaround: To view the help topics for these blocks:  Open the Help browser by selecting Help > Full Product Family Help from the MATLAB toolbar. In the Help Navigator, scroll to the Embedded Target for TI C6000? DSP and double-click to open it. Click BlocksˇŞBy Category or BlocksˇŞAlphabetical List. If you click BlocksˇŞBy Category, click the appropriate category link in the content pane, and then click the block name. If you click BlocksˇŞAlphabetical List,in the content pane click the block name.

Summary: Assignment block can cause incorrect simulation results
Description: A model containing an Assignment block can give wrong simulation results if it meets all the following conditions: The Assignment block's Output (Y)  option is set to Initialize using input (U1) . The Assignment block resides in a For or While Iterator subsystem. The Assignment block's output is connected to a block located in the same atomic subsystem. Reuse Block Outputs  is enabled in the Optimization  pane of the model's Configuration Parameters  dialog box.
Workaround: Either of the following workarounds avoid this bug:Disable Reuse Block Outputs in the Optimization pane of the model's Configuration Parameters dialog box.Embed the Assignment block in an atomic subsystem.

Summary: BoundaryCondition property of species ignored by stochastic solvers
Description: The BoundaryCondition  property of a species object is ignored by stochastic solvers during simulation. Therefore, when a species object has ConstantAmount  property set to false  and BoundaryCondition  property set to true , the stochastic solvers ignore the BoundaryCondition  property and thus the species amount varies according to the rate of the reactions that contain the species.
Workaround: If the species amount is not determined by a rule, you can set the ConstantAmount property of the species to true by setting the property at the command line, or by selecting the ConstantAmount checkbox in the SimBiology desktop. If the species amount is determined by a rule, the stochastic solvers ignore rules and there is currently no workaround for such models.

Summary: Models that reference library blocks with open functions fail to load
Description: Attempting to load a model that references library blocks having open functions causes a memory segmentation violation of the form: ------------------------------------------------------------------------        Segmentation violation detected at Wed Apr 19 17:15:00 2006 ------------------------------------------------------------------------ Configuration:   MATLAB Version:   7.2.0.232 (R2006a)   MATLAB License:   111673   Operating System: Microsoft Windows XP   Window System:    Version 5.1 (Build 2600: Service Pack 2)   Processor ID:     x86 Family 15 Model 2 Stepping 9, GenuineIntel   Virtual Machine:  Java 1.5.0 with Sun Microsystems Inc. Java HotSpot(TM) Client VM mixed mode   Default Charset:  windows-1252 Register State:   EAX = 00000000  EBX = 00000000   ECX = 0fd48080  EDX = 0fd7f920   ESI = 0fd49400  EDI = 0248d220   EBP = 00d0bc88  ESP = 00d0bc60   EIP = 7a1244a5  FLG = 00210202 Stack Trace:   [0] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl OpenGraphSubWindows(struct slGraph_tag *)(0x0248d220, 0x111628d0, 0, 0) + 69 bytes   [1] libmwsimulink.dll:struct slErrMsg_tag * __cdecl OpenSystemOpenBlockDiagram(struct OpenSystemRec *)(0x00d0bd8a "yourModel.mdl", 0x0eb17a30, 0, 1) + 113 bytes
Workaround: Execute load_system and then find_system before opening the model, for example:>> load_system yourModel>> find_system yourModel>> yourModel

Summary: surfacem does not accept vector inputs for latitude and longitude
Description: surfacem  is supposed to accept input of the form surfacem(lat, lon, Z)  where Z  is a matrix, lat  is a vector with length equal to the number of rows in Z , and lon  is a vector with length equal to the number of columns in Z , but it errors out instead.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use meshgrid to expand vectors lat and lon to matrices before calling surfacem. For example,[lon,lat] = meshgrid(lon,lat);Alternatively, after backing up the file MATLABROOT/toolbox/map/mapdisp/surfacem.m, replace it with the version in the attachment.

Summary: Cell array inputs to the union function with the same first element returns incorrect indices
Description: Cell array inputs to the union  function that 1. Have the same first element 2. Are the same size may return incorrect indices. For example:A = {'a','b','c'};B= {'a','z','y'};[C,IA,IB] = union(A,B)IA =     1     3IB =     2     3     1A(IA)    'a'    'c'B(IB)    'z'    'y'    'a' In this case, the indices IA and IB can be incorrect.

Summary: Seg. Fault on Concatenation of Empty Arrays
Description: MATLAB 7.2 has been observed to generate a segmentation fault during the concatenation of three or more arrays if at least two of these arrays were empty and also had conflicting dimensions.
Workaround: If you need to concatenate arrays with unmatching dimensions, do so with a maximum of two arrays at a time. That is, if you need to concatenate arrays A, B, and C, and at least two of these matrices do not match in dimension, then concatenate array A and B first, and then concatenate the result of that operation with array C.

Summary: Function gtopo30 may return an inaccurate referencing vector
Description: The referencing vector returned by function gtopo30 , when called with latitude and longitude limits, is calculated solely from the given limits.  This is incorrect; the referencing vector should be adjusted to correspond to actual cell boundaries within the input GTOPO30 data grid.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the version of the gtopo30.m function in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. MATLABROOT/toolbox/map/mapformats/gtopo30.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, gtopo30.m.OLD.Copy the version of gtopo30.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: The Stateflow sfprint command's printEntireChart option works incorrectly
Description: When a portion of a chart is visible in the Stateflow editor window, sfprint(gcb, 'jpg', 'myfile2', 1)  prints the visible portion of the chart, whereas sfprint(gcb, 'jpg', 'myfile2', 0)  prints the entire chart. The reverse should happen.

Summary: Error reading Gene Expression Omnibus Dataset files
Description: The function GEOSOFTREAD sometimes returns an error when trying to read Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO) Data Set (GDS) files.
Workaround: Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\geosoftread.m to$MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\geosoftread.m.oldCopy the attached file to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\geosoftread.m

Summary: Publishing to Word after publishing to HTML removes PNG files used in the published HTML file
Description: If you publish an MATLAB file that includes LaTeX equations or MATLAB plots to HTML, and then publish the MATLAB file to Word, when you view the published HTML file, the equations and plots are no longer there.
Workaround: Publish the Word document in a different directory than the directory in which you published the HTML file.

Summary: S-functions: generated multirate single-tasking S-functions produce incorrect results when used in models configured for a GRT target
Description: Execution of generated code for a model under the following conditions produces incorrect results:  You convert a multirate single-tasking model to an S-function with the S-function target  You use the generated S-function in another model, which is configured for a GRT target and has a base sample time that is faster than the base rate of the S-function   Execution results of the model containing the S-function are incorrect because the S-function output is called multiple times. The S-function output should be called only once at a specific rate.
Workaround: Make sure the the model and the S-function that it contains use the same base rate.

Summary: mcc -m -R -nodisplay creates an application that warns about missing display
Description: On UNIX, an application created by mcc  warns about a missing display when -R -nodisplay  is passed as an argument to mcc .
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: MDCE workers do not communicate on the ports specified by BASEPORT
Description: If MDCE workers are restarted, they no longer communicate on the ports specified by BASEPORT.
Workaround: To work around this problem, Stop and uninstall the mdce service on every computer in the cluster. Make sure that no job managers or workers are running; verify with nodestatus. Exit the MATLAB client as well. Delete the log and checkpoint directories on the cluster computers. By default, these are under C:\Temp\MDCE on Windows, and under /var/lib/mdce and /varlog/mdce on UNIX On every client and cluser computer, rename the file matlabroot/java/jar/toolbox/distcomp.jar  to distcomp.jar_OLDIf you are running DCT and MDCE version 2.0, save the attached distcomp.jar_2.0 file to matlabroot/java/jar/toolbox/ and rename it as distcomp.jar. If you are running DCT and MDCE version 2.0.1, save the attached distcomp.jar_2.0.1 file to matlabroot/java/jar/toolbox/ and rename it as distcomp.jar. Start the mdce service, job managers, and workers on the cluster computers. Start a MATLAB client and use the findResource command to find the job manager and confirm that all the workers have successfully registered with it.

Summary: imhandles fails if a figure contains multiple colorbars
Description: imhandles  fails if a figure that contains an image also includes two or more colorbars. For example, if you try to add the Pixel Information tool to a figure containing multiple colorbars, it fails. impixelinfo  calls imhandles . someData1 = imread('moon.tif');someData2 = imread('moon.tif');subplot(2,1,1), imagesc(someData1), colorbar subplot(2,1,2), imagesc(someData2), colorbar impixelinfo ??? Error using ==> eqMatrix dimensions must agree.Error in ==> imhandles at 54      hImage(hImage == colorbarHandle) = [];Error in ==> impixelinfo at 71imageHandles = imhandles(h);
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the version of the imhandles function in your current installation with the updated version attached.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file.matlabroot\toolbox\images\imuitools\imhandles.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, imhandles.m.OLD. Download the attached file, imhandles.m, storing it the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: A referenced model with enabled or triggered subsystems may generate incorrect code
Description: If a model references a submodel that contains an enabled or triggered subsystem and the subsystem meets the following conditions:  The enabled/triggered subsystem's RTW System Code  paramter is set to Function  The control input port of the subsystem is directly connected to either a root inport or root outport compiling the simulation target code may fail, preventing Simulink from simulating the top model.
Workaround: Do either of the following:Set the enabled/triggered subsystem's RTW System Code option to either Inline or Reusable Function.  Note Reusable function may not work if there are other systems inside the enabled/triggered subsystem that have been set to Function.Add a Signal Conversion block between the root inports and/or root outports and the control signal input.  Set the output to Contiguous copy and check Override optimizations and always copy signal.

Summary: M-Lint incorrectly reports syntax error for some uses of persisent
Description: M-Lint reports a syntax error for persistent a, a=0; but this syntax is valid in MATLAB.
Workaround: As a workaround, replace the comma by a semicolon, or move the initialization to the next line. M-Lint will not report the syntax as as error.

Summary: D2C/C2D functions do not work with some options
Description: When you have the Control System Toolbox installed, the System Identification Toolbox uses Control System Toolbox utilities for converting IDMODEL objects to LTI objects (ss, tf, zpk), performing d2c/c2d conversions, model order reduction, and viewing model responses using the LTI Viewer. However, since MATLAB R2006a, the System Identification Toolbox cannot find the Control System Toolbox utilities for D2C/C2D operations, when you select the conversion method 'prewarp', 'matched' or 'tustin'.
Workaround: When performing d2c or c2d conversions on idmodel objects using 'tustin', 'prewarp' or 'matched' methods, follow these steps:  Download the attached file, unzip and copy the extracted files into their respective locations, as specified below: toolbox\ident\ident\@idmodel\d2c.m toolbox\ident\ident\@idmodel\c2d.m toolbox\ident\idutils\iscstbinstalled.m  "clear classes" or restart MATLAB (if already running).

Summary: Indexed assignment into arrays of structures may lead to unexpected results
Description: Within a function, indexed assignment into an array of structures may lead to unexpected results. For example, the function shown here creates a structure and reads from that structure using indexed assignment. It returns an incorrect answer of 0, where the expected result is 0.5. function out = bug  a.b        = 0;  c(1).d(1)  = a;  a.b        = 0.5;  c(1).d(1)  = a;  out = c(1).d(1).b;>> bugans =     0
Workaround: To work around this bug, issue this command at the beginning of your MATLAB session:   feature jitallow non_scalar_structs off

Summary: Reading VMAP0 data on Windows with '\\' in filename
Description: On Windows, function vmap0data  replaces multiple backslash file separator characters ('\\') with a single backslash file separator character ('\') when constructing VMAP0 filenames. If the VMAP0 data files are on a network device identified by '\\' in the filename (as when VMAP0 data is copied from the Internet or from CDs to a network drive), the replacement prevents the function from finding the VMAP0 data files and returns []  after a read operation.
Workaround: Two workarounds exist to fix the bug. The first workaround requires that the parameter devicename in the function vmap0data include a mapped drive letter, i.e. D:\ or Y:\. The second workaround is to modify the function vmap0data. Remove line 353 in function vmap0data:tilepath = strrep(tilepath,[filesep filesep],filesep); % for the Browse libraryor replace MATLABROOT\toolbox\map\mapformats\vmap0data.m with the version in the attachment.

Summary: On Solaris builds only, incorrect HDL Cosimulation output can occur for some Verilog data types larger than 32 bits.
Description: On Solaris builds only, when bits are transferred between ModelSim and Simulink, the HDL Cosimulation block can output incorrect values for Verilog data types larger than 32 bits.
Workaround: To fix this bug, replace "matlab\toolbox\modelsim\sol2\simulinklink.so" with the attached file.

Summary: Editor/Debugger with M-Lint enabled terminates MATLAB for certain large or complex files
Description: MATLAB might terminate unexpectedly when you open certain types of large or complex MATLAB files in the Editor/Debugger when M-Lint is enabled. Examples of such files are those containing tens of thousands of lines, or with a very long series of if/else  statements.
Workaround: To work around the problem, disable the integrated M-Lint in the Editor/Debugger while you work on large or complex files. To do so, select File > Preferences > Editor/Debugger > Language, and clear the Enable integrated M-Lint check box. You can still run M-Lint for the file from Directory Reports in the Current Directory browser.Alternatively, reduce the size or complexity of the M-file by, for example, creating several smaller MATLAB files, or smaller if/else statements.

Summary: Problems using DIGITALIO with Measurement Computing USB-DIO96H/50 device.
Description: Customers attempting to use the USB-DIO96H/50 from Measurement Computing may encounter problems when calling digitalio .
Workaround: This has been identified as a bug in the configuration file associated with the Measurement Computing Interface. You can work around this issue by changing the configuration file for your USB-DIO96H/50.To change the configuration of the USB-DIO96H/50:Quit MATLAB.Change to the directory $MATLAB/toolbox/daq/daq/private, where $MATLAB is the directory where you installed MATLAB.Back up the file mwmcc.ini to another name such as mwmcc.ini.bak.Open mwmcc.ini and find the text [USB-DIO96H/50].In that section, find the line ID = 0x83, and change it to ID = 0x95.Save and close the file.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Creating initial population is incorrect with the ga function when my problem has bounds or linear constraints
Description: When using the ga  function with a bound and/or linear constraints problem and providing an initial population, one of two problems may occur:First, the CreationFcn  option in the gaoptimset  function is ignored when specified.Second, the ga  function is only using one feasible individual, and a copy of the feasible individual, in the initial population.
Workaround: If you would like to use different individuals as opposed to a copy of a feasible individual for linearly constrained problem, you must provide a creation function. Alternatively, in the gaoptimset function you can set the InitialPopulation option to be used by ga for linear constrained problems.

Summary: GUIDE documentation examples do not work
Description: The GUIDE examples "GUI with Multiple Axes" and "Simple GUI" do not work because their FIG-files, two_axes.fig  and simple_gui.fig  are corrupted.
Workaround: Replace the two_axes.fig and simple_gui.fig files with the attached files. These two files are located in $MATLAB/help/techdoc/creating_guis/examples, where $MATLAB is your MATLAB root directory.

Summary: Incorrect Colorbar for filled contour plots with user-specified contour lines
Description: When users specify a list of levels for a filled contour plot and the range of values in the list exceeds the range of the data, a colorbar added to the plot can have a scale that reflects the specified levels rather than the limits of the data being portrayed. The following example demonstrates this:y=[0:.05:1];x=[0:.05:1]';f=sin(pi*x)*cos(pi/5*(y+.2));umax=1;du=umax/10;figurecontourf(x,y,f',[-umax:du:umax])colorbar The colorbar does not reflect the data limits; it is capped by the minimum and maximum contour levels specified to contourf  (in this case, -1:1, while the actual data minimum is above 0), which may not be present on the plot or represent only a subrange of the data.
Workaround: To work around this issue, insert the following line of code between the contourf statement and the colorbar statement:set(gca,'clim',[0 1])

Summary: The MATLAB Compiler 4.4 (R2006a) incorrectly compiles programs that call certain plotting functions
Description: If I compile a function that contains calls to one or more of the following functions, polarcompassconeplotcontourisosurfacepiepie3rosestreamlinestreamribbonstreamtube I receive a run-time error such as this after I deploy the compiled executable: Error in ==> specgraph\private\mcoderegister at 8 Error in ==> pie at 127 Error in ==> pieTest at 2 ??? Undefined command/function 'makemcode'. MATLAB:UndefinedFunction
Workaround: A bug in MATLAB 7.2 (R2006a) prevents some plotting function calls from being compiled correctly. To work around this issue, compile your program with a copy of the file makemcode.m (included in the attachment), which must be resident in the same directory as the function you are compiling. Use the mcc command to compile MATLAB-file myfile.m, as follows:mcc -m myfile.m -a makemcode.m

Summary: Printing a figure on UNIX from the Print button causes an exception when default printer is unset
Description: Attempting to print a figure window from the toolbar Print  button when no default printer is set fails with a NullPointerException :java.lang.NullPointerExceptionat java.lang.String$CaseInsensitiveComparator.compare(Unknown Source)at java.lang.String$CaseInsensitiveComparator.compare(Unknown Source)at java.lang.String.compareToIgnoreCase(Unknown Source)at com.mathworks.page.export.printdlg.Printdlg.initPrintDialogSettings(Printdlg.java:326)at com.mathworks.page.export.printdlg.Printdlg.setVisible(Printdlg.java:446)
Workaround: You can use any of several workarounds:Print using the Print dialog, by selecting the File->Print menu item;Print the figure from the MATLAB Command Line using the print command; orSet a default printer in the UNIX print environment.

Summary: Signals in a referenced model not logged
Description: Simulink errors out when logging signals at the same level of a model hierarchy when signal names differ only by case. The error message is "Property name already in use." This bug occurs any time two names that differ only in case appear at the same level in the following Simulink signal logging objects:  Simulink.ModelDataLogs Simulink.SubsysDataLogs Simulink.StateflowDataLogs Simulink.ScopeDataLogs Simulink.TsArray
Workaround: Ensure that names of signals to be logged at any given level of a Simulink model hierarchy are unique. For example, if you want to log two signals in a subsystem, ensure that the signals have different names.

Summary: Signal storage reuse optimization interferes with algebraic loop occurrences minimization
Description: When the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences  option on the Subsystem block's parameter dialog box is selected in combination with the Signal storage reuse  optimization, the generated code may not define local variables that are necessary. This prevents accelerated simulation of such Simulink models. It also prevents successful code generation.
Workaround: The Signal storage reuse option in the Optimization section of the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box should not be selected.

Summary: Problems with modal dialog boxes used with MATLAB Builder for Excel
Description: When a MATLAB function that creates a modal GUI is compiled using the MATLAB Builder for Excel, the GUI does not repaint itself when called from a cell in an Excel spreadsheet. The visual appearance of this bug is that the window's previous positions are not erased when the GUI is moved. Continuous clicking and dragging will produce a trail of GUI windows.
Workaround: Do not use modal dialog functions from MATLAB code that is to be called from a cell in an Excel spreadsheet. This includes the following functions:uiwaituiresumequestdlguigetfile

Summary: Snapshot images omitted from reports in RTF or Word Document format
Description: When you generate a report in Rich Text Format (RTF) or Word Document format, some images in snapshot components (such as Axes Snapshot, Stateflow Snapshot, and System Snapshot) are omitted from the report.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached file. Here are the steps:Rename the following file:$MATLAB\sys\jade\docbook\print\db31.dsl(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB Command Prompt).Rename the file to db31.dsl.old.Download the attached file. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 1.

Summary: Cannot use workspace variables to initialize Stateflow data if SrcWorkspace is current or parent.
Description: When running a simulation from the command line in R2006a (using the sim  command), you cannot use workspace variables to initialize Stateflow data if you set SrcWorkspace  to current  or parent . For example: >> options = simset('SrcWorkspace', 'current');>> sim(modelName, 10, options);
Workaround: Set SrcWorkspace to base instead of current or parent before running simulation from the command line.

Summary: Invalid rate transition connections between asynchronous subsystems can cause incorrect results
Description: Simulink allows you to create invalid rate transition connections between asynchronous subsystems. Such invalid connections can lead to incorrect simulation results. To be valid, rate transition connections between asynchronous subsystems must meet the following conditions: If the input of a rate transition block is asychronous, the input port must be connected directly to an output of an asynchronous subsystem.  If the output of a rate transition block is asynchronous, the output port must be connected directly to an input of an asynchronous subsystem.
Workaround: Ensure that no more than one rate transiton block exists between the output of one asynchronous subsystem and the input of another asynchronous subsystem.

Summary: FDATool errors when generating MATLAB files
Description: For Butterworth filters with the Specify order option selected, using File->Generate MATLAB file produces an error.

Summary: */ in MATLAB comments in an Embedded MATLAB Function block cause compilation errors.
Description: In an Embedded MATLAB Function block, a MATLAB comment containing */  causes a compilation error, as in this example:% /* My C-style comment. */  This bug also affects Truth Table blocks, and Stateflow Embedded MATLAB Functions and Truth Tables.
Workaround: Do not embed */ inside a MATLAB comment.

Summary: Simulink reports an unexpected function-call subsystem data dependency violation
Description: Simulink reports a data dependency violation for two or more function-call subsystems involved in a loop even though the same function-call initiator block triggers the function-call subsystems. The problem occurs only if an Atomic, Enabled, Action, or Triggered Subsystem emits two or more function-call signals at its output ports. Furthermore, these signals must originate from multiple blocks as illustrated in the attached model, m308944.mdl .
Workaround: Several workarounds exist for this issue. Consider, for example, the attached model, m308944.mdl, which illustrates the problem.One approach for avoiding the error in this example is to add a Unit Delay block in the feedback connection between the function-call subsystems f and g.  Alternatively, consider moving the function-call subsystems ss/f and ss/g outside of the subsystem ss.

Summary: Stateflow chart outputs inheriting size/type cause assertion during model compilation
Description: When Stateflow output data inherits size/type, the data tries to back-inherit the size/type from the Simulink block to which it is connected. However, if the output data connects to a block such as Scope or Display ˇŞ or even to another output port that does not specify its input size/type ˇŞ an assertion failure occurs during Stateflow compilation.
Workaround: To work around the issue, do not use inherited size/type for Stateflow output data.

Summary: Atmospheric density and pressure profiles in Non-Standard Day blocks
Description: Non-Standard Day 310 and Non-Standard Day 210C blocks compute atmospheric density and pressure profiles using hydrostatic equations. This algorithm sometimes introduces minor but noticeable inaccuracies.

Summary: fscanf and sscanf do not skip trailing literal strings in format when size is specified
Description: This bug shows up in the fscanf  and sscanf  functions when you specify a format string in which one or more ordinary characters follow the last conversion specifier. It only occurs when you call these functions with all three input arguments: the input file or string, the format string, and the number of conversion specifiers to match before terminating the scan. The bug manifests itself somewhat differently in the two functions, yet has the same overall effect. With fscanf , the function leaves the file position indicator in the wrong location when the function completes. (The file position indicator is the value returned by ftell .) With sscanf , the function returns an incorrect value in the nextindex  output. In either case, the bug makes it difficult to resume scanning the string at the position where you left off. When MATLAB scans a string with fscanf  or sscanf , it matches the conversion specifiers (e.g., %f or %c), but skips over any ordinary characters (such as / or ,) that might follow these specifiers. For example, the format string '%f/' could be translated as, "match a floating point number, but skip over the / character that follows it". When you include a size  input argument in the call to fscanf  or sscanf , MATLAB matches only that many conversion specifiers and then stops. If size  is specified and there are any ordinary characters that follow the conversion specifier in the format string, MATLAB is supposed to scan only the number of matches specified by size , and skip over the ordinary characters that follow each match. This would leave the file position indicator pointing at the start of the next number in the string (if there are more numbers remaining). This is what has been observed to fail in the R2006a release. Example Using fscanf  The command shown below should read the following sequence from the file referred to by fid : a floating-point number, a comma (which is discarded), another floating-point number, another comma (also discarded), and so on. MATLAB should skip each comma as it comes to it. The final comma of the scan is not skipped however, due to this bug.     fscanf(fid, '%f,', 4) When the function completes and there are more numbers in the file that have not yet been scanned, the file position indicator should be set to the start of the next number in the string. Instead, the indicator points to the comma that precedes this number. Example Using sscanf  The following command reads 4 floating point numbers from the input string, skipping each comma that separates the numbers:     [A,count,errormsg,nextindex] = ...      sscanf('5.8,7.3,4.9,8.2,1.5', '%f,', 4) When the function completes and there are more numbers in the sequence that have not yet been scanned, the nextindex  output should be set to the index of the next number in the string (in this example, that would be 17, the index of the character '1'). Instead, nextindex  points to the comma that precedes this number (that is, nextindex  is set to 16). Note that this bug does not occur when size  is left unspecified (defaulting to Inf ), or is specified as Inf .
Workaround: One possible workaround is to add one extra call to fscanf or sscanf to skip the trailing text. For example, to implement a workaround for the fscanf example in the Description section above, use    fscanf(fid, '%f,', 4); fscanf(fid, ',');

Summary: Thirteen envelope data points in the Non-Standard Day 210C block not matching data points from MIL-STD-210C
Description: The following are the profile data points where the Non-Standard Day 210C block does not give same output as in the MIL-STD-210C specification:Low density at 5 km, Frequency: 10% -> density at 70 km Low density at 10 km, Frequency: 1% -> temperature at 78 km Low density at 30 km, Frequency 10% -> temperature at 72 km  High density at 5 km, Frequency: 1% -> density at 56 km High density at 20 km, Frequency: 10% -> density at 16 km High density at 30 km, Frequency: 10% -> density at 2 km High density at 30 km, Frequency: 10% -> density at 18 km High density at 30 km, Frequency 10% -> density at 42 km   High temperature at 10 km, Frequency 10% -> temperature at 34 km  High temperature at 20 km, Frequency 10% -> temperature at 44 km   High temperature at 20 km, Frequency: 10% -> density at 48 km High temperature at 20 km, Frequency 10% -> temperature at 62 km   High temperature at 20 km, Frequency: 10% -> temperature at 78 km

Summary: dicomuid produces unique identifiers (UIDs) with leading zero components
Description: On UNIX platforms, dicomuid  can produce unique identifiers (UIDs) with components that begin with zeros, such as '1.2.03.4'. These leading zeros are illegal and can cause problems with other DICOM-compliant applications and hardware.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the version of the dicom_generate_uid.m file in your current installation with the updated version contained in the attached file.Rename the dicom_generate_uid.m file in the following directory, giving it a name such as dicom_generate_uid.m.old: matlabroot/toolbox/images/medformats/privatewhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.Download the attached file, extract dicom_generate_uid.m, storing it the directory specified in the previous step.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Slowdown using Plot Tools and annotating figures when the Array Editor is open
Description: When using the plotting tools (Plot Browser, Figure Palette, and Property Editor), or adding annotations (such as text boxes or arrows) to figures, significant slowdowns can occur when the Array Editor is also open.
Workaround: Close the Array Editor when working with the plotting tools.

Summary: Calling clabel after setting 'Ydir' to 'Reverse' causes the y-axis to invert
Description: When reversing the direction of the y-axis using set  and then calling clabel , the whole plot, not just the axis labels, flips. Reproduction steps: [c,h]=contour(peaks); set(gca, 'YDir', 'reverse'); clabel(c, h)
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached view.m file. Here are the steps: 1. Quit MATLAB2. Rename the following MATLAB file:$MATLAB\toolbox\matlab\graph3d\view.mto$MATLAB\toolbox\matlab\graph3d\view.m.old(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabrootat the MATLAB Command Prompt.)3. Download the attached file. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2. 4. Restart MATLAB 5. After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB Command Prompt: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: When the Integer-To-Bit Converter block is configured to output boolean data it can produce wrong results.
Description: When the Integer-To-Bit Converter block is configured to output boolean data, and the input data values exceed 255, the block outputs incorrect values.
Workaround: If the input data values to the block exceed 255, set the output data type to uint16, int16, uint32, int32, single, or double.

Summary: S-function auto generation: Generate code only option inhibits auto generation
Description: If the Generate code only  option on the Real-Time Workshop (General) pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog is selected for a model when you select Generate S-function  from the Real-Time Workshop submenu of the Tools menu or subsystem block's context menu, the build process terminates prematurely and Real-Time Workshop does not generate the S-function.
Workaround: Clear the Generate code only checkbox.

Summary: Unable to change the length of a 1-D table in the Calibration Manager window
Description: 1-D Tables that do not have a shared normalizer (commonly referred to as "functions") cannot have their length changed in the Calibration Manager tool within CAGE.  Applying a length change appears to work correctly but the table does not change in length. 1-D Tables that have shared normalizers are not affected by this issue.
Workaround: A patch is available that will fix this error in versions 2.1, 2.1.1, 2.1.2, 3.0 and 3.0.1 of the toolbox. To install this fix:Rename the file $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcexpr\@cglookupone\set.m to $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcexpr\@cglookupone\set.m.old.Download the file set.m that is listed in the Attachments section and save it to $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcexpr\@cglookupone\set.m.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Digital hardware triggered acquisitions on National Instruments NIDAQmx hardware can drop points.
Description: When using National Instruments hardware and the NIDAQmx driver, if I configure the Data Acquisition Toolbox to sample a certain number of data points triggered by a digital hardware trigger, I sometimes get fewer samples than I requested.ai = analoginput('nidaq','Dev1');addchannel(ai,0);ai.SamplesPerTrigger = 1000;ai.TriggerType = 'hwDigital';start(ai);% Trigger the hardware signal on PFI0, and wait 1 secondai.SamplesAvailable ans =  768 Expected ai.SamplesAvailable to return 1000.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached version of the DLL, MWNIDAQMX.DLL. Here are the steps:Quit MATLAB.Rename the following DLL: $MATLAB\toolbox\daq\daq\private\MWNIDAQMX.DLL (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB Command Prompt.)Rename the file to MWNIDAQMX.DLL.old.Download and extract the attached file, MWNIDAQMX.DLL. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Subfunctions not hyperlinked when using help functionname
Description: Subfunctions are not hyperlinked in the "See Also" line of the help functionname  output.

Summary: LCC implementation of atanh returns positive result for negative input
Description: C code that calls atanh and is compiled with LCC produces the absolute value of the correct answer, and 0.0 for out-of-range inputs.This bug potentially affects the following products/features if they are configured to use LCC and call atanh:  C MEX functions Stateflow using custom C code integration S-function builder Custom S-functions Use of the Trigonometric Function block in Real-Time Workshop
Workaround: Do not use atanh, or use a different MEX-supported C compiler.

Summary: Using CPU Timer block in model causes an error
Description: Embedded Target for TI C6000 reserves CPU timer 1 for clocking the base rate task. If your project is not using DSP/BIOS, remaining (unallocated) CPU timers in the platform are available for use. In most platforms the only remaining timer is CPU timer 0. However, the CPU Timer block from c6000dspcorelib  incorrectly reports the following error when you try to generate code by configuring CPU timer 0:Error using ==> beforemakeHookpoint_DSPtargetBoth Timer 0 and Timer 1 have been already allocated for use by the system or by Timer blocks.

Summary: Scalar times sparse matrix yields performance degradation
Description: When you multiply a sparse matrix by a scalar, the operation time is dependent upon the order of m*n , but it should only depend on the order of the number of nonzeros.The most egregious form of the problem is seen asA = sparse(10000,10000); 2*A; A  contains nothing but zeros and therefore is the ultimate sparse matrix. In this case, scaling by a finite number should be almost immediate.
Workaround: The example below shows two workarounds for multiplying a sparse matrix by a scalar in a timely manner.Suppose you wish to compute B = s*A, where A is sparse and s is a scalar, you could compute either of the following:B = spfun(@(x) s.*x, A);m = size(A,1); B = sparse(1:m, 1:m, s)*A;

Summary: Array Editor: copying cell arrays doesn't always copy all cells
Description: Copying data from a cell array in the Array Editor sometimes doesn't copy all of the selected cells onto the clipboard. Instead, only a subsection of the selected data is copied.

Summary: A loop involving a Function-Call Subsystem and a Merge block may cause a memory error or crash
Description: Simulink may report a memory allocation error while updating a model that contains a data dependency loop involving a Function-Call Subsystem and a Merge block. Also, Simulink may crash while simulating a model that contains a loop involving a Function-call Subsystem and a Merge block.
Workaround: Use a Memory block to resolve the loop involving the Function-Call Subsystem as illustrated in the attached models, mdlCase1.mdl and mdlCase2.mdl.

Summary: COM methods, properties, or events of type VARIANT cause errors
Description: Calling COM methods or properties of the VARIANT  type can cause MATLAB to stop responding. Receiving events of the VARIANT  type can cause MATLAB to stop responding or cause the event argument to return incorrect results.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached version of the file comcli.dll. Here are the steps:Quit MATLAB.Rename the following file: $MATLAB\bin\win32\comcli.dll(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB Command Prompt). Rename the file to comcli.dll.old.Download the attached file, comcli.dll. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: gaussfir uses old, incorrect formula for alpha
Description: gaussfir  was implemented using the formula for alpha given in the first edition of Wireless Communications: Principles and Practice by Theodore Rappaport, Prentice Hall 1996. This formula is incorrect and has been changed in the second edition of this book.
Workaround: Before calling gaussfir, you should multiply the 3 dB Bandwidth-Symbol time product by 2 to produce the correct answer. For example, if the desired BT value is 0.3, you should use  b = gaussfir(.6,...);

Summary: Error when designing a compensator with a bus signal
Description: When tuning blocks in a compensator with a bus signal that passes through a Zero-Order Hold, Unit Delay, Memory, or Rate Transition block, Simulink Compensator Design Task may generate an error like the following:Error using ==> get_param Invalid Simulink object name: modelname/BlockName/BlockName_1.
Workaround: If you are using Simulink Control Design 2.0 (R2006a),  Get the value of matlabroot, the directory in which MATLAB is installed on your system, by entering matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. Quit MATLAB.  In the directory matlabroot/toolbox/slcontrol/slcontrol/@LinearizationObjects/@linearizationutil/ Rename utDFSfactorization.m to utDFSfactorization.m.old  Rename utFTPnt2Pnt.m to utFTPnt2Pnt.m.old  Download the attached files, utDFSfactorization.m and utFTPnt2Pnt.m, to the same directory mentioned in step 3.   Restart MATLAB.

Summary: interrupt qualifier breaks DSP/BIOS Hardware Interrupt block interrupt service routine
Description: The DSP/BIOS HWI block generates interrupt service routines (ISR) that include the interrupt  keyword with the interrupt function qualifier. However, since the DSP/BIOS HWI block uses the DSP/BIOS interrupt dispatcher, incorporating the interrupt  keyword breaks your generated code.
Workaround: Download the attached ZIP archive tic6000_patch_306513.zip and TIC6000 patch installer tic6000_patch.m. Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch:  tic6000_patch('tic6000_patch_306513.zip', 'install')  Installing patch tic6000_patch_306513.zip fixes the ISRby changing the interrupt function qualifier from interrupt to void.

Summary: RTW build error with Communications Blockset blocks if $MATLABROOT contains white space
Description: On Windows, an RTW build for a model which contains Communications Blockset blocks will fail, if $MATLABROOT (folder where MATLAB is installed) contains white space.
Workaround: This was due to an incorrect folder specification in the file $MATLABROOT/toolbox/commblks/commmex/commblks_rtwmakeinfo_common.m.An updated version of the said file is included in the attached .zip file to avoid this error. Please update this file in the $MATLABROOT/toolbox/commblks/commmex/ folder.

Summary: Computer may restart when using some Measurement Computing PCI-DAS boards.
Description: There is a bug in the driver for the Measurement Computing PCI-DAS-16xx and PCI-DAS-10xx boards that may cause the computer to restart under some circumstances during loading of the driver.
Workaround: Use a driver from the previous release (R14sp3) or the next release (R2006b).  The bug exists in R2006a only, and the drivers in the three releases are compatible.

Summary: Signal names for Stateflow ports are not resolved correctly when added to the xPC Target Scope block
Description: This issue is caused by a bug in the tlc  file (scBlock.tlc ) that corresponds to the Scope (xPC) block.
Workaround: To work around this issue and resolve signal names for Stateflow ports:   Quit MATLAB   Rename the following file $MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\tlc_c\scblock.tlc  (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing   matlabroot   at the MATLAB command prompt.)  Rename the file to scblock.tlc.old.Download the attached file. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in Step 2.  Restart MATLAB  After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:  rehash toolbox

Summary: Simulating the Zero Pad block using the Simulink Accelerator can give incorrect results
Description: Models using the Zero Pad block can yield incorrect results when using the Simulink Accelerator.Incorrect results can occur under the following conditions:There is a post-compile virtual block before the Zero Pad block The input and output of the Zero Pad block have the same data type and complexity The input size of the Zero Pad block is smaller than its output size The input size of the virtual block is same as the output size of the Zero Pad block Accelerator mode is being used  When these conditions are met, Simulink allows the input and output ports of the Zero Pad block to share the same memory, and no code is generated for the block. The Zero Pad block then yields incorrect results.When there is a test point on the input signal of the virtual block, Simulink does not allow the output of the Zero Pad block to overwrite its input port memory, and you get the correct results.
Workaround: To work around this problem, go to Simulation > Configuration Parameters in your model and clear the Reuse block outputs check box in the Opimization pane. The model now gives the correct results in both Normal and Accelerator mode.

Summary: set_param can clear previous error information
Description: Calling set_param  can clear the values returned by the functions lasterror  and lasterr .
Workaround: Store the values returned by lasterr or lasterror before calling set_param.

Summary: Receive error: This device is not recognized by the toolbox when using National Instruments PCI-DIO-96
Description: The toolbox is listed as supporting the National Instruments PCI-DIO-96, but when I try to use it I get the following error:  ??? Error using ==>  digitalio.digitalio Error using ==> digitalio.digitalio>localCreateDigitalIOObject  This device is not recognized by the toolbox. However, many times, supportfor new hardware  can be added. Visit The Mathworks web site and search on thename of your device for more  information.     As shown in the following example:  >>  p=daqhwinfo('nidaq') p = AdaptorDllName: [1x74 char] AdaptorDllVersion: '2.8.1  (R2006a)' AdaptorName: 'nidaq' BoardNames: {'PCI-6115' 'PCIe-6259' 'PCI-DIO-96'}  InstalledBoardIds: {'Dev2' 'Dev3' 'Dev4'} ObjectConstructorName: {3x3 cell} >>  dio=digitalio('nidaq','Dev4') ??? Error using ==> digitalio.digitalio Error using  ==> digitalio.digitalio>localCreateDigitalIOObject This device is not recognized by the  toolbox. However, many times, support for new hardware can be added. Visit The Mathworks web  site and search on the name of your device for more information.
Workaround: The work-around is to change the configuration file for your device.To change the configuration file of the device:Quit MATLAB.Change to the directory $MATLAB/toolbox/daq/daq/private, where $MATLAB is the directory where you installed MATLAB.Back up the file mwnidaqmx.ini to a another name such as mwnidaqmx.bak.Open mwnidaqmx.ini and find the entry for your device, [PCI-DIO96].Change the first line from "[PCI-DIO96]" to "[PCI-DIO-96]" (add a dash before the 96).Save and close the file.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Acoustic noise cancellation demo does not build with GRT system target file
Description: Acoustic Noise Cancellation demo (*dskANC.mdl ) does not build for the following hardware platforms when you set the system target file to ti_c6000.tlc : C6416 DSK (c6416dskANC.mdl )C6711 DSK (c6711dskANC.mdl )C6713 DSK (c6713dskANC.mdl ) The Downsample block in the model causes the build failure.
Workaround: Download the attached ZIP archive tic6000_patch_305913.zip and TIC6000 patch installer tic6000_patch.m. Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch:  tic6000_patch('tic6000_patch_305913.zip', 'install')  Installing the patch tic6000_patch_305913.zip fixes the problem with the demo by removing the downsample block from the model.

Summary: Turning off block reduction causes the linearize command to error out
Description: An error may occur when you linearize a Simulink model with the BlockReduction  option off . The following code illustrates the issue: >> f14 >> opt = linoptions; >> opt.BlockReduction = 'off'; >> sys = linearize('f14',operpoint('f14'),opt); ??? Output argument "Jreduced" (and maybe others) not assigned during call to "D:\Work\Acontrol\matlab\toolbox\slcontrol\slcontrol\@LinearizationObjects\@linearizationutil\minjacobian.m (minjacobian)".

Summary: Why does DAQHWINFO return empty values when used in a MATLAB Compiler application?
Description: When a function using DAQHWINFO is compiled using the MATLAB Compiler, it returns empty values when the generated executable is run. For example, suppose the following function is created:function getdaq     daqhwinfo   Using the MATLAB Compiler, an executable called GETDAQ.EXE is generated. If this executable is run on a machine where MATLAB has not been installed, the following output is produced:ans =           ToolboxName: 'Data Acquisition Toolbox'        ToolboxVersion: ' '         MATLABVersion:  ' '     InstalledAdaptors: ' '  The executable does not recognize any available Data Acquisition Toolbox adaptors.
Workaround: To work around this issue, register the hardware driver adaptors individually using daqregister. Safeguard against errors by using a try - catch statement sequence:  function getdaq     try         daqregister('nidaq') %nidaq is an example.     catch     end     daqhwinfo Repeat this for all the adaptors.

Summary: Cannot use Extended Symbolic Math Toolbox due to lack of license for Symbolic Math Toolbox
Description: For standalone installations on Windows systems, if you install the Extended Symbolic Math Toolbox, but not the Symbolic Math Toolbox, you will not be able to use the Extended Symbolic Math Toolbox. When you try to use the Extended Symbolic Math Toolbox, you will get an error similar to:License Manager Error -5.Cannot find a license for Symbolic_Toolbox.Make sure your license file is correct.No such feature exists. This is a problem with the installer, not with the toolbox, and only occurs when installing from the CD. Concurrent licenses and Network Named User installations are not affected by this issue.
Workaround: To fix this problem, download the R2006a+ Extended Symbolic Math Toolbox and the installer from the MathWorks Web site, and reinstall the product. The installer has been updated in R2006a+ to install the license for the Symbolic Math Toolbox when installing the Extended Symbolic Math Toolbox.

Summary: Compiled GUI crashes if the visibility is set to off on a controlling figure window
Description: Setting the visibility of a figure window to off and then attempting to display a second figure window from the same method causes a compiled application to error out. See the following example of code that causes the problem:     set(gcf, 'visible', 'off');      fig2;  While this code works fine for non-compiled code, when compiled the application reports the following warning.     Warning: Objects of uitools.uimodemanager class exist - not clearing              this class or any of its super-classes
Workaround: The workaround for this bug is to change     set(gcf,'visible','off');     fig2; to the following:    h= gcf;     fig2;     set(h,'visible','off');

Summary: Error when saving a figure containing the camera toolbar
Description: When saving a figure which has a visible camera toolbar as a FIG-file, the following error is printed to the command-window.Reference to non-existent field 'wcb' The error only occurs when the camera toolbar is visible.
Workaround: Prior to saving the figure, turn off the camera toolbar.

Summary: Embedded Target for TIC2000 does not allow subsytem code generation
Description: If you attempt to generate code from a subsystem, Simulink will generate the following error:Error using ==> DSPTgtPkg.eZdspF2812TgtPrefs.validateModel_tic2000targetThe model may not contain more than one target preference block. If you attempt to generate code for a specific subsystem containing a Target Preference block, Simulink will hang after displaying the following initialization message: A Target Preference Block for Embedded Target for TIC2000 has been added to the model. Do you want to initialize the configuration parameters pertinent to this target to this default settings?
Workaround: Embedded Target for TIC2000 does not support code generation for or from a subsystem in this release.To generate code, the TIC2000 Target Preference block must be at the root of the Simulink model and code generation must be instantiated from the root of the model.

Summary: Random Source block fails RSIM target in certain mode
Description: A valid RSIM executable can not be generated from a model using the Random Source block when both of the following conditions are true:The Inline parameters  check box on the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameters  dialog box is selectedThe Repeatability  parameter on the Random Source block dialog box is set to Not Repeatable .
Workaround: To work around this problem, clear the Inline parameters check box on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Subsystems: Real-Time Workshop fails to compile multitasking models that include multirate enabled subsystems
Description: If the following conditions apply, Real-Time Workshop fails to compile the model due to an undeclared task identifier:  The model is configured for multitasking - the Type  solver configuration parameter option is set to Fixed-step  and Tasking mode for periodic sample times  is set to MultiTasking . The model includes a multirate enabled subsystem.
Workaround: If possible, avoid using a multirate enabled subsystem in a multitasking enviroment. Code generated from enabled subsystems can exhibit nondeterministic behavior and data integrity issues in a preemptive, multitasking environment if both of the following conditions exist:  The enabled subsystem uses multiple sample rates. Options on the Solver pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box are set as follows:   Type is set to Fixed-step.  Tasking mode for periodic sample times is set to Auto or Multitasking.   The issues that can result are Issue 1: For multirate enabled subsystems in a multitasking environment, where the rates execute preemptively, nondeterministic behavior can occur due to timing variations associated with enabling and disabling subsystem code. Issue 2: For enabled subsystems and function-call subsystems that include an asynchronous subsystem, the potential exists for data corruption when data read or write operations are preempted. This can occur when any of the following are true:  States when enabling option for the subsystem's Enable block is set to reset. Output when disabled option for one or more of the subsystem's Outport blocks is set to reset. A block in the subsystem uses an Enable or Disable method that gains access to global data.    If you must use a multirate enabled subsystem, do the following:Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.Quit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\subsystemlib.tlc to matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw\subsystemlib.tlc.old.Download the attached file subsystemlib.tlc and place it in the directory matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\mw.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: C-API interface: Real-Time Workshop generates incorrect parameter dimensions
Description: If a model uses two parameters with different dimensions, Real-Time Workshop can generate a C-API interface that represents the parameters with incorrect dimensions. For example, if parameter x1   has a dimension of 1x71 and parameter x2   has a dimension of 17x1, The generated array rtDimensionMap  in model _capi.c  lists only one of the dimensions Both parameters in the array rtModelParameters  in model _capi.c  incorrectly share the same dimension
Workaround: This is a limitation with Real-Time Workshop R14 (Version 6.0) through R14SP2 (Version 6.2). There are no known workarounds.

Summary: close function might fail if a function or class called "result" is on the MATLAB path
Description: If the close  function is called in a MATLAB session while a function or class named "result" is on the MATLAB path, the close operation can fail with the following error:??? Function 'not' is not defined for values of class 'result'.Error in ==> close>request_close at 180if ~resultError in ==> close at 95status = request_close(h);
Workaround: Remove the function or class with the name "result" from the MATLAB path or rename it.

Summary: Build failure when building a .NET component from dotnettool
Description: You get the following error when attempting to build a component using dotnettool: Error using ==> basicui>buildBuild error.
Workaround: The workaround is to build using the option to generate verbose output.Open the Project Settings dialog box.Click Show Verbose Output in the Compiler Options section.Rebuild the project.

Summary: With debugging disabled, for loops in Embedded MATLAB Function blocks may iterate incorrectly
Description: In an Embedded MATLAB Function block, the iteration count in for  loops may be one less than expected under the following conditions:The for  loop step size is a value other than 1 and -1. The class of the for  loop expression is an integer. Debugging is disabled for the block.  For example, if debugging is disabled, the following for  loop executes once instead of twice:  for k = int32(1):int32(2):int32(3)    y = y + 1;  end In this case, the step size is 2, and the class of the expression is int32 . The for  loop executes correctly if you remove the explicit class specification:  for k = 1:2:3     y = y + 1;  end In this case, the index variable k  will be an integer in the generated code, so the explicit class specification is not required.
Workaround: To work around this problem, use one of the following techniques:Avoid explicit integer-class expressions in the for loop. For example, use  for k = 1:2:3     y = y + 1;  endinstead of  for k = int32(1):int32(2):int32(3)    y = y + 1;  endEnable debugging for the Embedded MATLAB Function block.
