Note that this is the default condition.Issue the following MATLAB command before building or simulating the model:  sf('Feature','For-loop raising','off');

Summary: Incorrect sample time error
Description: Simulink displays an error similar to the following: Cannot determine a sample time for block 'sampletime_test/Subsystem1/Subsystem/Gain1'. Either specify the sample time of this block (a suggested rate is [1, 0], the greatest common divisor of the input rates) or specify sample times for the blocks that drive this block's inputs.  during update diagram if the following is true about the model: The block in the error has a -1 (inherited) sample time. The signal driving one or more of the inputs of the block has its sample time set by a virtual block (a subsystem inport or a signal specification block). The sample time of the signal upstream from the virtual block is faster (i.e. a lower number) than the sample time defined by the virtual block.  or The block in the error has a -1 (inherited) sample time. The block is inside a periodic atomic subsystem. The sample time of the signal upstream from the subsystem is faster (i.e. a lower number) than the sample time defined by the subsystem  block.
Workaround: Either specify the sample time directly on the block or insert a rate transition block that changes the sample time of the signal instead of the virtual block.

Summary: Unnecessary Fixed-Point Toolbox license check out for models with Stateflow charts
Description: A Fixed-Point Toolbox license is checked out for models that contain a Stateflow chart, even when no fixed-point features are used.
Workaround: To work around this problem,1. Quit MATLAB2. Rename the following file based on your platform by appending a .old extension:Windows $MATLAB\toolbox\fixedpoint\fixedpoint\@embedded\schema.pAll other platforms$MATLAB/toolbox/fixedpoint/fixedpoint/@embedded/schema.p$MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. 3. Download and unzip the attached zip file to schema.p from the Attachments section below.Place it in the same directory mentioned in Step 2.4. Restart MATLABNote:This workaround has a side effect regarding the order in which fimath and fi objects can be created.To use the add, sub, and mpy fimath functions to operate on fi objects, create a fi object before creating a fimath.You might see an error like the following:  F = fimath;a = fi(pi);F.add(a,a);"?? No appropriate method or public field add for class embedded.fimath" Work around such an error by executing the following command at the MATLAB prompt:methods(F)

Summary: Incorrect CCS version specified for DM642 EVM target
Description: User documentation for the Embedded Target for TIC6000 Version 2.3.1 states that CCS Version 2.20 is required for working with the DM642 EVM target. That is not correct. The correct CCS version is 3.0.
Workaround: Here are the correct CCS versions for using the DM642 EVM target: Embedded Target for TIC6000 Version 2.3.1 (R14SP2): CCS 3.0 Embedded Target for TIC6000 Version 2.4 (R14SP3): CCS 3.0 Embedded Target for TIC6000 Version 3.0 (R2006a): CCS 3.1

Summary: Error on ADDCHANNEL with National Instruments USB-9221 device
Description: When you attempt to addchannel  to an analog input object representing a National Instruments USB-9221 device, you receive: ??? Error using ==> daqdevice.addchannel NIDAQmx error : Specified property is not supported by the device or is not applicable to the task. Property: DAQmx_AIConv_Rate Task Name: MWDAT0002 Status Code: -200452  Example code: ai=analoginput('nidaq','Dev1'); addchannel(ai,0); ??? Error using ==> daqdevice.addchannel NIDAQmx error : Specified property is not supported by the device or is not applicable to the task. Property: DAQmx_AIConv_Rate Task Name: MWDAT0002 Status Code: -200452
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached version of the DLL, MWNIDAQMX.DLL. Here are the steps:Quit MATLAB.Rename the following DLL: $MATLAB\toolbox\daq\daq\private\MWNIDAQMX.DLL (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB Command Prompt.)Rename the file to MWNIDAQMX.DLL.old.Download and extract the attached file, MWNIDAQMX.DLL. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Matrix Size Limitations
Description: A MATLAB Programming release note on the topic of "Larger Data Sets with 64-Bit Windows XP" is inaccurate.  The release note states MATLAB support for Windows XP 64-bit edition enables you to handle much larger data sets. There remains a 2 GB limit on each variable, but you can store many more such variables in memory at one time.
Workaround: The 2G limit is actually on the number of elements in an array, as stated in another release note on "System Requirements":Matrix Size Limitations.   Historically, MATLAB matrices have been limited in size to those that would fit in a 32-bit address space. Some of those limitations have been lifted, but some remain. Specifically, the data type used to index into an mxArray is still a 32-bit signed integer. This limits the number of elements in any one array to INT_MAX-1, or 2147483646 (approximately 2*10^9). With this limit, you can create matrices up to 16 GB (for doubles). You can create as many of these as your machine has memory to support.

Summary: Why does the Coreco adaptor ignore offsets defined in the device file?
Description: I have defined a horizontal or vertical offset in a device file created by Coreco's Camera Configurator. However, when I pass this device file to the videoinput  command, the offsets that I specified are ignored. This leads to a defective image with, for example, black bars on the top or left side of the image. This happens with both the PC2-Vision and PC-Camlink boards.
Workaround: There is a patch available for this issue. This patch will only work with versions R2006a or later. To use the patch, download the attached files and follow these steps:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup of <MATLAB>/toolbox/imaq/imaqadaptors/win32/mwcorecoimaq.dll and <MATLAB>/toolbox/imaq/imaqadaptors/win32/corecoIFC.dll.Extract the files in the attached zip file to <MATLAB>/toolbox/imaq/imaqadaptors/win32/Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Simulink.Bus.save command does not save the bus Description property
Description: The Simulink.Bus.save  command does not save the bus Description  property.
Workaround: In your MATLAB installation area, do the follwing:Exit MATLABBack up matlabroot/toolbox/simulink/simulink/@Simulink/@Bus/save.m to save.m.orig.Back up matlabroot/toolbox/simulink/simulink/@Simulink/@Bus/cellToObject.mto cellToObject.m.orig.Copy the attached file attachment_304471_20060530.zip to a temp dir. This file contains save.m and cellToObject.m. Unzip the file.Copy save.m and cellToObject.m files to matlabroot/toolbox/simulink/simulink/@Simulink/@Bus.Start MATLAB and run rehash toolboxcache.

Summary: Body Spring & Damper fails in algebraic loops and with Joint Stiction Actuator
Description: The Body Spring & Damper block does not work when placed in a model that requires an algebraic loop for simulation. For example, use of the Joint Stiction Actuator triggers an algebraic loop, and if you use both blocks together in the same model, this error occurs:Input data dependency violation due to action subsystems. See Subsystem Examples in the Simulink library for valid and invalid examples of action subsystems.
Workaround: When simulating with algebraic loops (for example, if you use the Joint Stiction Actuator), work around this bug by implementing the spring and damper force law using SimMechanics sensors and actuators and pure Simulink blocks. You need to sense the displacements and relative velocities of the two bodies and actuate them with the appropriate action-reaction force pair.

Summary: Successive calls to imshow and Image Processing Toolbox modular GUI tools get slower with each call
Description: When imshow  and impixelinfo  are called successively.  with each call, the performance gets progressively worse until eventually MATLAB crashes.  This same behavior occurs when calling imshow  with the other Image Processing Toolbox modular GUI tools, including:  imageinfo imcontrast  imdisplayrange  impixelinfoval  impixelinfo imoverview impixelregion    The following example illustrates this behavior. >>load mri; >>tic;for i=1:20 imshow(D(:,:,:,i),[]); end;toc; Elapsed time is 4.211042 seconds. >> tic;for i=1:20 imshow(D(:,:,i),[]); impixelinfo; end;toc; Elapsed time is 6.024808 seconds. >> tic;for i=1:20 imshow(D(:,:,i),[]); impixelinfo; end;toc; Elapsed time is 7.115280 seconds.

Summary: MCC fails when compiling a MEX-file when there's a MATLAB file with the same name
Description: MCC may not be able to properly validate some MEX-files when a MATLAB file with the same name is found in the same location. In addition, MCC's attempt to validate such a MEX-file may have side effects such as stopping output to stderr from working, so the user may not see an error message. The effect is that the MEX-file will not be included in the resulting CTF archive.
Workaround: Running mex to regenerate the MEX-file may fix the problem. Otherwise, you must temporarily move the MATLAB file with the same name to a different location to perform the compilation.

Summary: Error when adding a variable in the Data Editor: "[A,B] Incompatible Sweepset Object Sizes"
Description: In the Data Editor window of the Model Browser, trying to add a variable in the Variable Editor dialog may result in the following error being displayed in the MATLAB Command Window.  This will only occur if the data contains certain combinations of other items such as filters:??? Error using ==> sweepset.horzcat[A,B] Incompatible Sweepset Object SizesWarning: Error occurred while evaluating listener callback.> In D:\MATLAB\MATLAB71\toolbox\mbc\mbcguitools\@xregGui\@uddDispatcher\send.p>send at 13  In D:\MATLAB\MATLAB71\toolbox\mbc\mbcguitools\@xregGui\@uipushtool\pNotifyButtonUp.p>pNotifyButtonUp at 18  In D:\MATLAB\MATLAB71\toolbox\mbc\mbcguitools\@xregGui\@javaToolbar\pButtonUp.p>pButtonUp at 18  In D:\MATLAB\MATLAB71\toolbox\mbc\mbcguitools\@xregGui\@javaToolbar\javaToolbar.p>i_buttonup at 56
Workaround: A patch is available that will fix this error in Version 3.0. To install this fix:Rename the file $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbctools\@sweepsetfilter\setAllows.m to $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbctools\@sweepsetfilter\setAllows.m.old.Download the file setAllows.m that is listed in the Attachments section and save it to $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbctools\@sweepsetfilter\setAllows.m.Rename the file $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbctools\@sweepsetfilter\private\getFlags.m to $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbctools\@sweepsetfilter\private\getFlags.m.old.Download the file getFlags.m that is listed in the Attachments section and save it to $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbctools\@sweepsetfilter\private\getFlags.m.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Joystick Input block cannot detect presence of DirectX API
Description: Joystick Input block cannot detect presence of DirectX API, which causes the joystick force-feedback functionality to work improperly. This issue does not affect other joystick functions.

Summary: Error message when you generate code from Simulink model containing Byte Pack or Byte Reversal blocks from the Host Communication library
Description: When you try to generate code from a Simulink model that contains Byte Pack or Byte Reversal blocks from the Host Communication library, you get the following error message:Error: File: \toolbox\shared\etargets\etargets\tlc_c\reverseendian.tlc Line: 7 Column: 30The %implements directive specifies xpcreverseendian as the type where reverseendian was expectedError: File: \toolbox\shared\etargets\etargets\tlc_c\any2byte.tlc Line: 7 Column: 25The %implements directive specifies xpcany2byte as the type where any2byte was expected
Workaround: Download the attached ZIP archive tic6000_patch_302096.zip and the TIC6000 patch installer tic6000_patch.m. Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch to fix the problem:  tic6000_patch('tic6000_patch_302096.zip', 'install') Installing this patch updates the any2byte.tlc and reverseendian.tlc files with the correct %implements directives.

Summary: Error when executing a model with large number of measurement blocks
Description: When you try to simulate a model that contains a large number (around 400) of Voltage Measurement and Current Measurement blocks, you get the following error:??? Error using ==> set_param Input bounds are out of range.
Workaround: To work around this problem, download and install the files in attachment.matlab/toolbox/physmod/powersys/powersys/power_utile.mdlmatlab/toolbox/physmod/powersys/powersys/private/spsbuild.p

Summary: Magnitude response estimate analysis in FDATool and FVTool does not account for quantization in fixed-point filter arithmetic
Description: When you use the magnitude response estimate analysis in FDATool or FVTool (Analysis > Magnitude Response Estimate  from the menu bar), quantization in the arithmetic operations in fixed-point filters is not being taken into account.For fixed-point arithmetic filters, FVTool/FDATool estimates the magnitude response using the fixed-point filter coefficients, but does not include quantization errors that arise from roundoff in multiplications and/or additions internal to the filter.

Summary: imopen produces incorrect results on some binary images with decomposed structuring elements
Description: For some binary images, with certain decomposed structuring elements, the imopen function produces incorrect results for the bottom 32 rows. For example: bw = imread('circbw.tif');se = strel('disk', 5);bw_open = imopen(bw, se); bw_open has some incorrect output pixels in the bottom rows.
Workaround: Copy the file matlabroot\toolbox\images\images\private\morphop.m to matlabroot\toolbox\images\images\private\morphop.original, where matlabroot is the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.Replace the original file morphop.m with the version contained in the attached file.At the MATLAB command prompt, type:rehash toolbox

Summary: Model Advisor launch fails if model name contains character "z"
Description: If you try to launch Model Advisor for a model whose name contains the character "z", such as the demo model sldemo_zeroxing , an error similar to the following is displayed: ??? Error using ==> get_param Invalid Simulink object name: sldemo_]roxing.
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2006a, rename the model such that its name does not contain the character "z".

Summary: Error encountered when selecting compensator in the compensator editor panel of the SISO Design Tool
Description: When selecting compensators in the 'Compensator Editor' tab of the SISO Design Tool it is possible to encounter an 'Index exceeds matrix dimensions' error displayed in the command window. This bug does not affect the controller design workflow or results. This case is encountered under the following scenario: Suppose two compensators C1 and C2 are present in the 'Compensator' combo box located in the 'Compensator Editor' tab in SISO Design Tool and each compensator has a different number of poles or zeros. For example, C1 has 1 pole and C2 has 3 poles. Select C2 in the 'Compensator' combo box and then select the third pole in the 'Dynamics' table. Then select C1 in the 'Compensator' combo box. An 'Index exceeds matrix dimensions' error shows up in MATLAB command window.
Workaround: Download the attachment and unzip its content to a temporary directory. Replace MATLABROOT/toolbox/control/ctrlguis/@sisogui/@pzeditor/refresh_pz_editor.m.??

Summary: Segmentation violation when pinning annotation objects
Description: MATLAB produces a segmentation violation when the user attempts to pin any annotation object to an axes whose units  is anything other than 'normalized' .

Summary: National Instruments PCI-6254, PCI-6259, PXI-6254, and PXI-6259 report an incorrect number of digital lines.
Description: When creating a digital I/O object on the National Instruments PCI-6254, PCI-6259, PXI-6254, and PXI-6259, the toolbox reports that the device only has 8 lines on port 0, when in fact it has 32.
Workaround: You can get access to the first 31 lines of these devices by changing the configuration files. Line 32 is unavaliable at this time.To get access to the first 31 lines: Quit MATLAB. Find the following ini file:$MATLAB\toolbox\daq\daq\private\mwnidaqmx.ini(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB Command Prompt.)Make a back-up copy of this file to mwnidaqmx.ini.old. Open the file in a text editor, and find the entry for your device.  Each device is listed by the device name, e.g. [PCI-6254].Replace the line starting with digitalPortSize with the line digitalPortSize  = 31,8,8(Effectively, you are changing the value from 8 to 31.)Save the file and restart MATLAB.

Summary: exifread function causes segmentation violation or hang when reading malformed EXIF data
Description: When reading EXIF tags from a JPG or TIFF file, the exifread  function issues a warning message (displayed below) if the EXIF data is malformed, but still attempts to read the data anyway. In some instances, this can result in the termination of the MATLAB application.Warning: Illegally sized directoryWarning: Illegal number format 32 for tag 0002Warning: Illegal number format 24 for tag 0002Warning: Illegal value pointer for tag 0002Warning: Illegal value pointer for tag 0003 ...
Workaround: It may be possible to read the EXIF data from a file by using a different application which is more tolerant of incorrectly written files. You can then use the application to write the data back to a file as properly written EXIF data, which can then be read by MATLAB.

Summary: Draw Markers block might output incorrect results at port B
Description: When the Input image type parameter is set to RGB, the Draw Markers block might output incorrect results at the B port. When this issue occurs, all the pixels in the blue plane of the RGB image appear black.
Workaround: To correct this problem, follow these steps:1) Navigate to$matlabroot\toolbox\vipblks\vipmex\tlc_c2) Save the existing svipdrawmarkers.tlc as svipdrawmarkers_old.tlc3) Unzip attachment_303244_20060309.zip into a temporary directory. Replace svipdrawmarkers.tlc with one of the extracted files based on the following criteria:If you are using Video and Image Processing Blockset Version 2.0 or 2.1, save svipdrawmarkers_20.tlc as svipdrawmarkers.tlc in the directory above.If you are using Video and Image Processing Blockset Version 1.2, save svipdrawmarkers_12.tlc as svipdrawmarkers.tlc in the directory above.If you are using Video and Image Processing Blockset Version 1.1, save svipdrawmarkers_11.tlc as svipdrawmarkers.tlc in the directory above.

Summary: Model block: model containing Model block that includes noninlined S-function can fail to simulate if Real-Time Workshop license is not present
Description: A model can fail to simulate if all of the following conditions exist:  The model contains a Model block.  The Model block contains at least one noninlined S-function.  A valid Real-Time Workshop license is not installed on the system on which you run the simulation.   When the simulation fails, the following error message might appear: ### Updating model reference SIM target for model: temp_model Error: File: utillib.tlc Line: 2180 Column: 32 Undefined identifier Subsystem

Summary: Why do I get the run-time error "Can't Start MATLAB engine" when I mex engine applications using Borland C++ Compiler 5.6?
Description: If you create an engine application using a non-MSVC compiler, at run time you may get an error"Can't Start MATLAB Engine"

Summary: Simulink Verification and Validation license is checked out when copying Stateflow Chart or Embedded MATLAB Function blocks
Description: Suppose your MATLAB installation includes Simulink Verification and Validation. In this case, when you copy either of the following blocks in Simulink:     Stateflow Chart     Embedded MATLAB Function  the license for Simulink Verification and Validation is checked out, despite the fact that the block might not contain any requirement links. Note that if you use a floating license for Simulink Verification and Validation and all available licenses are checked out when you copy either of these blocks, MATLAB will display an error stating License checkout failed .
Workaround: You can work around this issue by disabling the feature that copies a block's requirement links when you copy the block. To do so, enter the following at the MATLAB prompt:slfeature('MdlDuplicateRequirementsOnCopy',0)

Summary: Japanese version: MATLAB 7.2 terminates after upgrading from V7.1 and shortcuts are not maintained
Description: On Japanese Windows versions, after upgrading from MATLAB 7.1 (R14SP3), MATLAB 7.2 (R2006a) terminates upon startup and the following exception appears: org.apache.xerces.dom.ParentNode.internalRemoveChild(ParentNode.java:574)org.apache.xerces.dom.ParentNode.removeChild(ParentNode.java:516)com.mathworks.mlwidgets.shortcuts.ShortcutUtils.getShortcutsFile(ShortcutUtils.java:641)... If you then restart MATLAB 7.2, you will not have any of the  shortcuts from MATLAB 7.1.
Workaround: There are three workarounds to choose from, depending on whether or not you have already run MATLAB 7.2 and whether or not you want to maintain shortcuts from MATLAB 7.1.No Need to Migrate ShortcutsIf you did not have or do not need to migrate MATLAB shortcuts from Version 7.1 (R14SP3), you do not need to do anything else. If you already ran MATLAB 7.2 and got the exception, MATLAB 7.2 should work fine when you restart it. If you have not yet run MATLAB 7.2, MATLAB will produce an exception and terminate the first time you run it, but will work fine after you restart it.Have Not Yet Run MATLAB 7.2 and Want to Migrate ShortcutsIf you want to migrate shortcuts from MATLAB 7.1 (R14SP3) and have not yet run MATLAB 7.2 (R2006a), install the patch as described here:In the matlabroot/java directory, create this subdirectory structure: /patch/com/mathworks/mlwidgets/shortcuts/resources/Download the attached resource file and save it to the resources directory you just created.Start MATLAB 7.2.Already Ran MATLAB 7.2 and Want to Migrate ShortcutsIf you have already run MATLAB 7.2 and you want to migrate shortcuts from MATLAB 7.1 (R14SP3), perform these steps:Run  MATLAB 7.2.Type prefdir in the Command Window to determine where MATLAB 7.2 stores preferences.In Windows Explorer, navigate to the directory where MATLAB 7.2 stores preferences.Exit MATLAB.In Windows Explorer, navigate up one directory from where MATLAB 7.2 stores preferences.Open the R14SP3 subdirectory and copy the shortcuts_ja.xml file.Navigate up one directory and then open the R2006a subdirectory. Paste the shortcuts_ja.xml file into that directory (R2006a).In the matlabroot/java directory, create this subdirectory structure: /patch/com/mathworks/mlwidgets/shortcuts/resources/. Download the attached resource file and save it to the resources directory you just created.Start MATLAB 7.2.

Summary: Invalid code generated for ERT S-function when nonvirtual bus crosses boundary
Description: Invalid code can be generated if you create an ERT S-function wrapper for a model or subsystem that has a nonvirtual bus crossing its boundary.
Workaround: If you are running R14sp3, you can work around the problem by replacing the file matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/ertsfcnbody.tlc in your MATLAB installation tree with a patched version, as follows: Determine your MATLAB root directory, matlabroot, by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt. Quit MATLAB. In the directory matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw, rename the file ertsfcnbody.tlc to ertsfcnbody.tlc.old. Click the attachment link below and download ertsfcnbody.tlc.R14sp3_patch_for_302886.  Rename the file to ertsfcnbody.tlc and place it in the same directory as the file you renamed. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: .NET Builder component throws an exception when used in ASP.NET or any unmanaged application
Description: Currently, a .NET component built using MATLAB Builder for .NET throws a null value exception when used in ASP.NET or when referenced from any unmanaged application.  This occurs because of a bug in the MWArray static constructor.
Workaround: This bug has been fixed for the next release. In the meantime, you can work around the bug by downloading a new .NET Builder data conversion assembly (MWArray.dll) from the Web. The new assembly is attached to this CBR. Copy the new assembly to matlabroot/bin/win32 on machines that have MATLAB installed, or to mcrinstalldir/v74/runtime/win32 on machines that have only the MATLAB Component Runtime (MCR) installed.  Start a Visual Studio DOS window, cd to the directory containing the new assembly, and run the following command from an account having admin privileges:  gacutil /i MWArray.dll. Note: In addition to the above procedure, you might have to rebuild the .NET Builder component before running it in ASP.NET

Summary: CCS generates an RTDX library error when running video surveillance demo
Description: When you run the Video Surveillance demo, CCS reports an error message that says:RTDX target application does not match emulation protocol! Loaded program was created with an RTDX library which does not match the target device.  When you click OK  on the error message dialog, CCS downloads the executable code to the target normally, but the running process cannot establish RTDX communication with the target and the demo times out waiting for an RTDX message from the target.
Workaround: Reset the board using the Reset button on the target board or by power cycling the board, and then try downloading and running the demo again.

Summary: Segv occurs when loading model with different block and system names
Description: A segv occurs when Simulink loads a model with different block and system names.

Summary: Simulink may crash when updating or simulating a model with an InitFcn or StartFcn callback
Description: Simulink may crash with a segmentation violation when updating or simulating a model if the following conditions are all true:     The InitFcn or the StartFcn callback of the model is set.     The model has at least one Inport with a bus object specified.     The InitFcn or StartFcn callback code manipulates a mask or block parameter.
Workaround: No workaround is available.

Summary: LoadLibrary error when installing COM component created by MXLTOOL or DOTNETTOOL
Description: Customers attempting to install COM components created with MATLAB Builder for Excel and MATLAB Builder for .NET get the following error message:LoadLibrary() failed - The specified module could not be found. This error occurs because of a bug in the component packaging process in MATLAB Builder for Excel and MATLAB Builder for .NET.  The packaging process creates a component installer, implemented as a self-extracting executable, that customers run on their target machine to get access to the COM components. The component installer fails to include MCRRegCOMComponent.exe , a utility file, which registers the component. Because of this bug, the utility is missing, the component is not registered, the module cannot be found, and the installation process generates the error. NOTE: This bug affects COM components generated by MATLAB Builder for Excel and MATLAB Builder for .NET. It does not affect.NET components created by MATLAB Builder for .NET.
Workaround: To work around this issue, the creator of the COM component has to build a new package that contains the missing utility, as follows:Contact The MathWorks and download the MCRRegCOMComponent.exe to the machine where the COM component is being created.Open the project used to create the COM component.In the MATLAB Builder dialog, click Component->Package Component.In the Package Files dialog, click Add File.In the Add files to package dialog, navigate to, select, and open the utilitythat you downloaded in the first step.Add other files needed for the package and include the MCR and its location asneeded.Click Create.The packaging process creates a component installer, which is an .exe file in the/distrib area of your project directory.Copy the component installer to the target machine and run it from a DOScommand prompt.NOTE: The component installer pauses after running the MCR installer. Press the Enter keyto make sure the registration process is completed.

Summary: Unsupported angular and torque units displayed as block dialog options
Description: In some of the block dialogs of the new version of SimMechanics, you see certain angular and torque units that are unsupported. These include:rev  (revolution) for angle J  (Joule) for torque Btu  (British thermal units) for torque ft*lb  (foot-pound) for torque  If you attempt to use these units when updating or simulating the model, SimMechanics issues an error message.
Workaround: Open the block dialog containing the unsupported unit and change your unit selection to supported units: (N*m or lb*ft) and (deg or rad).

Summary: Lane Departure Warning System demo produces an error in Draw Lines mode
Description: If you set the Switch block to Draw Lines mode, the Lane Departure Warning System demo produces an error.
Workaround: If you are running the Windows version of the demo model, follow these steps:1) Open vipldws_win.mdl.2) Type the following text at the MATLAB command line:set_param('vipldws_win/Lane Departure Detection/Draw Lines/DrawShapes','shape','Lines')If you are running the platform independent version of the demo model, follow these steps:1) Open vipldws_all.mdl.2) Type the following text at the MATLAB command lineset_param('vipldws_all/Lane Departure Detection/Draw Lines/DrawShapes','shape','Lines')

Summary: Error when you do a short-circuit of an RLC element
Description: If you short-circuit Series or Parallel RLC Branch blocks, you get an Index exceeds matrix dimensions  error.
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the powersolve.p file in your installation with the version provided in the attachment below. Here are the steps: 1. Quit MATLAB.2. Rename the following P-file:$MATLAB\toolbox\physmod\powersys\powersys\private\powersolve.p(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB Command Prompt.)Rename the file to powersolve.p.old.3. Download the attached file. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in step 2.4. Restart MATLAB.5. After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB Command Prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Draw Shapes block might produce errors when drawing filled polygons
Description: The Draw Shapes block produces an error if these three conditions are met:1. You set the Shape parameter to Polygons.2. You select the Fill shapes check box.3. The input to the Pts port is a matrix whose number of rows is less than or equal to 4.
Workaround: Set the Shape parameter to Lines.

Summary: Incorrect figure window brought to front when running a GUIDE GUI or opening a FIG-file
Description: When you use the openfig  command to open an existing FIG-file or start a GUIDE GUI by invoking the name of the GUI MATLAB-file, the figure window that comes to the front is not the one for the FIG-file or the GUIDE GUI.
Workaround: Replace the existing openfig.m file with the attached file following these instructions: Rename the existing openfig.m file to a name such as openfig_old.m. The openfig.m file is located in matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\graphics\, where matlabroot is the location at which you installed MATLAB.Download the attached openfig.m file and place it in this same location.This workaround is for MATLAB Version 7.3 (R2006a) only. Please do not use it in other versions.

Summary: Dialogs not rendering and missing units: Sensors, Actuators, and Body Spring & Damper
Description: You cannot specify parameters on the following blocks in 'lb ' or derived units:Joint Stiction Actuator Joint Actuator Body Actuator Joint Sensor Constraint & Driver Sensor Body Spring & Damper  If they were previously created using one of these units, the block dialogs might not render and instead might issue the following error:Failed to create dialog schema. Contact technical support.: Error using ==> PMDialogs.PmGuiObj.realizeChildrenPMDialogs.DynGroupPanel.realizeChildren
Workaround: Download the attached file to the following directory and restart MATLAB:$matlabroot/toolbox/physmod/pm_util/pm_util/Overwrite your existing file of the same name with the attached file.If you have installed SimHydraulics 1.0, do not apply this fix.If you install SimDriveline or SimPowerSystems after applying this fix, you need to reapply the fix.

Summary: Looking under mask of Check Discrete Gradient block may cause Simulink to hang
Description: If you look under the mask of a Check Discrete Gradient block, the contents of its subsystem window flicker. Subsequent attempts to move or modify those flickering objects may cause Simulink to become unresponsive and hang.
Workaround: If you look under the mask of a Check Discrete Gradient block and observe flickering objects, immediately close the affected window.

Summary: UDP Target to Host Communication demo has a broken link/incorrect block
Description: The UDP Target to Host Communication demo uses the Pack block from the xpclib/UDP  block library in xPC Target. If you do not have xPC Target installed, you encounter a broken link error when you run the demo model.
Workaround: Follow these steps to update the demo.Download the attached ZIP archive patch_302096_R2006b.zip and the patch installer tic6000_patch.m. To install the patch, Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt:  tic6000_patch('tic6000_patch_302096.zip', 'install') Installing patch tic6000_patch_302096.zip fixes the demo by updating the demo model to use the Byte Pack block from Embedded Target for TI C6000 DSP instead of the xPC Target Pack block.

Summary: Saving figure as TIFF file with the Save dialog causes an error
Description: Trying to save a figure as a TIFF using the figure Save dialog from the File  menu or the figure toolbar causes an error:  Error using ==> strcmp Too many input arguments. The figure is not saved. For the rest of the MATLAB? session, any attempts to save a figure using the File  menu or the toolbar fail immediately.
Workaround: To save a figure as a TIFF image or as a TIFF image without compression, use either of these two workarounds: Workaround 1:  Download the new version of filemenufcn.m, included in the attached .zip file, and use it to replace the copy saved in (matlabroot)/toolbox/matlab/uitools. Quit and restart MATLAB.Workaround 2: Use the saveas command from the command window.The saveas command takes an optional third parameter to specify file format. Use 'tiff' for TIFF images and 'tiffnocompression' for TIFF no compression images.

Summary: Space Vector Generator block gives wrong output if inputs are 0
Description: The Space Vector Generator block in the C28x Digital Motor Control Library gives incorrect output values when the input values are equal to 0 . Input values of 0  represent a typical startup condition, where the control algorithm is not applying any torque to the motor. This bug originated in code provided by Texas Instruments in the DMC library. The fix appears in Version 3.20 of the SVgendq.c file and will be included in Embedded Target for TI C2000 in the indicated release. Note :??This bug appears in R2006a, but not in R14SP3 or earlier releases.
Workaround: To fix the problem, install the two attached files as follows:  Close MATLAB.  Navigate to, and make a backup copy of each of the two following files:      [installation-root]\matlab\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000blks\tidmcsvgendq.mexw32    [installation-root]\matlab\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000blks\tlc_c\tidmcsvgendq.tlc    Download the two attached files into their respective directories listed above.   Run MATLAB and type rehash toolboxcache in the MATLAB command window.

Summary: Model blocks: incorrect code might be generated for model containing Model blocks
Description: If all of the following conditions apply to a model, Real-Time Workshop might generate code that will not compile: Contains Model blocks Is configured to use the ERT target  Has the Inline parameters  option on the Optimization  pane turned off Has the Generate reusable code  option on the Real-Time Workshop > Interface  pane turned on

Summary: MATLAB Builder for .NET does not install .NET Framework on deployment machine
Description: In the documentation for MATLAB Builder for .NET, in the 2nd chapter, under the section "How Does Component Deployment Work?", it is mentioned that:  ********************************* If the .NET framework and the MCR are not already installed, and if the Install MCR option was selected, the component installer does the following: 1 Installs the .NET framework  *********************************  This is not true. We do not package .NET Framework with MCRInstaller.exe. This was an initial proposal which was dropped later on. Users are supposed to have the appropriate version of the .NET Framework installed on their machine if they want to use components created using MATLAB Builder for .NET.
Workaround: The documentation has been corrected.Note that customers do have to install .NET Framework on deployment machines.

Summary: Simpson 4-Speed transmission template issues warnings
Description: Opening a model built with SimDriveline before Version 1.2 (R2006a) that contains a Simpson 4-Speed transmission template calls a function that no longer exists and produces command line warnings such as these:Warning: When displaying: "drive_vehicle_brake/Simpson 4-Speed",the expression "Icon.x9" could not be evaluated.Check that the parameter entries and variables used inthe Initialization command are valid MATLAB expressions.> In general\private\openmdl at 13  In open at 141  In uiopen at 181
Workaround: To work around this problem, either:Delete the plotting commands and the callback in the Simpson 4-Speed block mask Initialization tab that calls drive_simpsongearblock.Download the attached file to the following directory and restart MATLAB:$matlabroot/toolbox/physmod/drive/drive/

Summary: hdf5read and hdf5write do not handle variable length strings correctly
Description: HDF5 provides several mechanisms for storing strings. The hdf5read and hdf5write functions cannot handle one of these types: variable-length strings. This issue does not affect fixed-length strings. For example: data = hdf5read('myfile.h5', 'data_field')hdf5.h5string:       Name: ''     Length: 4    Padding: 'nullterm'       Data: 'p???['
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the following MATLAB DLLs and MATLAB-files with the updated versions attached. To do this, you can eitherDownload the zip file and extract all of the files into one directory and add this directory to the head of your MATLAB search path, for example: addpath('c:\myfiles\hdf5\');If you use this method, you will need to run this command once per MATLAB session before using any HDF5 functions.Rename the versions of these files in your existing MATLAB installation and then download the updated versions into the same directories. To do this, perform this procedure.Exit MATLAB.Make backup copies of the following files. For example, rename the existing files, adding "old" to the file extension. In the following, MATLABROOT represents your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot command. In MATLABROOT/bin/win32/libhdf5.dlllibhdf5.mapIn MATLABROOT/toolbox/matlab/imagesci/hdf5info.mhdf5read.mhdf5write.mIn MATLABROOT/toolbox/matlab/imagesci/private/hdf5infoc.mexw32hdf5infoc.mexw32.csfhdf5readc.mexw32hdf5readc.mexw32.csfhdf5writec.mexw32hdf5writec.mexw32.csfDownload the zip file and extract all of the files, then store them in the directories specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Bus Selector dialog may generate an error for signal names containing spaces
Description: The Bus Selector dialog may generate an error for signal names containing spaces. If a signal name is prefixed or postfixed with spaces, the Bus Selector dialog fails to recognize it as a valid signal when selected. For example, selecting a signal called 'mySignal' works correctly, but a signal called 'mySignal ' fails to resolve and generates a '???' in the selected signals list.
Workaround: Do not use spaces in signal names.

Summary: Changing of Default Editor to the MATLAB Editor gives error
Description: Changing the Virtual Reality Toolbox default editor to the MATLAB editor gives an error in the command window and the MATLAB Editor does not open. This error occurs when MATLAB is installed in a directory with spaces on the Windows platforms.
Workaround: To work around the issue of having spaces in matlabroot, use double quotes around the full path to meditor.exe.vrsetpref('Editor','"%matlabroot\bin\win32\meditor.exe" %file')

Summary: Signal objects: initial value for mpt.Signal objects ignored if storage class is Auto or SimulinkGlobal
Description: Prior to R2006a (V4.4), Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder used the InitialValue  property of an mpt.Signal  object to initialize memory for global data stores in generated code regardless of the object's storage class. In R2006a (V4.4), the InitialValue  property for an mpt.Signal  object was moved into the standard Simulink.Signal  object and is now used for both simulation and code generation.  As a result of this change, Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder ignores an mpt.Signal  object's initial value if the storage class is set to Auto  or SimulinkGlobal .
Workaround: Set the storage class of the mpt.Signal object to a value other than Auto or SimulinkGlobal.

Summary: Incorrect serial bit rate setting in demo models c166_fuelsys and c166_async will cause c166_profile('serial') command to fail
Description: The serial bit rate setting in the demo models c166_fuelsys and c166_async is incompatible with the c166_profile('serial') command and will cause the command to fail with the following error. ??? Error using ==> exprofile_getdata_serial>localBailoutTimeout occurred. No execution profiling data was received fromthe target. You should check the following:    1. The serial port on the target is connected to the serial      port on the host machine.   2. The application on the target is running.   3. The target and host serial ports must both be configured      for the same bit rate. The host-side default bit-rate      is 57600. The target-side bit-rate is typically established      via the serial settings within the Resource Configuration block.   4. The application on the target is properly configured to      provide execution profiling data. If you are performing execution profiling over a long period of time it may be necessary to increase the timeout value.
Workaround: You should re-configure the demo model to set the serial bit rate to 57600, as recomended in point 3 of the error message. Then you can execute the profiling command correctly.  Locate and double-click the C166 Resource Configuration Block in the model. Find the Bit_rate_ideal setting, which is located under c166drivers/Asynchronous//Synchronous Serial Interface, and set it to 57600.

Summary: Using National Instruments devices with the NIDAQmx driver, the analog output sample rate reverts to default when channels added.
Description: When using National Instruments devices with the NIDAQmx driver, I can set the analog output sample rate but when I add a channel to the analog output object, the sample rate reverts back to the default.>> ao = analogoutput('nidaq','Dev2');>> ao.SampleRateans =        1000>> ao.SampleRate = 5000;>> ao.SampleRateans =        5000>> addchannel(ao,0)   Index:  ChannelName:  HwChannel:  OutputRange:  UnitsRange:  Units:      1       ''            0           [-10 10]      [-10 10]     'Volts'  >> ao.SampleRateans =        1000
Workaround: You can avoid this problem by adding all the channels before setting the SampleRate.>> ao = analogoutput('nidaq','Dev2');>> ao.SampleRateans =        1000>> addchannel(ao,0)   Index:  ChannelName:  HwChannel:  OutputRange:  UnitsRange:  Units:      1       ''            0           [-10 10]      [-10 10]     'Volts'  >> ao.SampleRate = 5000;>> ao.SampleRateans =        5000

Summary: R14SP3 models error in R2006a when the Window Function block is saved with Specify word length selected for the Window parameter
Description: R14SP3 models that contain a Window Function block with the Window  parameter set to Specify word length  will error when simulated in R2006a.
Workaround: To work around this problem, double-click the Window Function block in your model to open the dialog and then click OK to close the dialog. This only needs to be done once per Window Function block; the model will then be in a clean state for subsequent simulations.

Summary: Many adaptive equalizer blocks do not work properly with sample-based signals
Description: The following adaptive equalizer blocks do not work properly when they receive sample-based signals:LMS Linear EqualizerRLS Linear EqualizerSign LMS Linear EqualizerNormalized LMS Linear EqualizerVariable Step LMS Linear EqualizerLMS Decision Feedback EqualizerRLS Decision Feedback EqualizerSign LMS Decision Feedback EqualizerNormalized LMS Decision Feedback EqualizerVariable Step LMS Decision Feedback Equalizer
Workaround: Use frame-based signals with these equalizers.

Summary: Wavelet 1-D GUI errors for screen resolutions higher than 1280 x 1024
Description: If your screen resolution is higher than 1280 x 1024 and you click the Statistics button of the Wavelet 1-D GUI, an error occurs.
Workaround: To fix the Wavelet 1-D GUI Statistics option for high screen resolutions, download the wtbutils.m file attached below into the matlabroot\toolbox\wavelet\wavelet directory,  where matlabroot is the path where your MATLAB is located.
