Summary: mex writes temporary files in wrong directory when using Visual C++
Description: When you build a MEX-file on Windows using the mex  command and Microsoft Visual C++, mex  writes temporary files in the wrong directory. The mex  command should write the files in the directory that is the value of the TEMP  environment variable (the %TEMP%  directory). Instead, mex  writes the files in the directory that contains the %TEMP%  directory. For example, if the value of the TEMP  environment variable is C:\Temp , mex  writes temporary files in the directory C:\ . The files have names like Temp_lib nnnnn .exp , where nnnnn  is a string of digits. The mex  command fails to delete the temporary files when finished with them.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download and unzip the attached zip file. Here are the steps:Download the attached file, mex.zip.Place mex.zip in the directory $MATLAB\bin (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB Command Prompt).Start MATLAB.At the MATLAB Command Prompt, typecd(matlabroot)cd('bin')movefile('mex.pl', 'mex.pl.old')unzip('mex.zip')This workaround causes mex to write temporary files in the %TEMP% directory. However, mex still does not delete the files, and you may need to delete such files yourself from the %TEMP% directory periodically.

Summary: Code generation compiles with errors if "floating point numbers" option is not selected
Description: If the "Support floating-point numbers" option in the Interface section of the Simulink's Configuration Parameters is not checked, the compilation of the generated code may produce errors.
Workaround: Close MATLABCopy the attached file to the following folder in your MATLAB installation: MATLABROOT/toolbox/shared/etargets/etargets/@DSPTgtPkg/@BuildOptionsStart MATLAB

Summary: Code generation slower for models with conditionally executed subsystems whose outports use wide signals or have nonempty initial conditions
Description: Code generation can slow down significantly for models containing conditionally executed subsystems whose outports have Wide signals (e.g. dozens of elements) entering them Non-empty initial conditions
Workaround: A patched version of one of Real Time Workshop's TLC files is attached as outport.tlc.R2006a_patch_for_g301776. Save it as rtw/c/tlc/blocks/outport.tlc in an R2006a distribution.

Summary: Using the print function on a maximized figure that contains a uicontrol can result in an error in UIJ_AreThereWindowShowsPending
Description: Using the print  function on a maximized figure can cause MATLAB to generate an error.  Below is a code sample demonstrating the error case. >>figure>>uicontrol [Maximize the figure.]>> print??? Error using ==> printError using ==> graphics\private\prepareError using ==> drawnowUIJ_AreThereWindowShowsPending - timeout waiting for window to show up.
Workaround: The error does not occur if the figure is not maximized.  If you require a large figure, use the figure Position property to make the figure as large as it would be if it were maximized.

Summary: Cascaded discrete event subsystems and cascaded function-call-generating blocks issue incorrect error
Description: Some configurations of blocks that represent discrete event subsystems or that generate function calls might cause the model to issue an incorrect error message. Relevant blocks include Discrete Event Inport block, inside the Discrete Event Subsystem block Signal-Based Event to Function-Call Event block Signal-Based Function-Call Event Generator block  Affected configurations have both of these properties: Blocks from the list above are cascaded, that is, an output from one block is an input to a subsequent block The Specify event priority...  parameter is selected for the first as well as subsequent blocks in the cascade  The incorrect error message states that a function-call initiator attempted to redisable or reenable a function-call subsystem. The attached image shows cascaded blocks and indicates which items can specify an event priority for the associated event without causing the error message. An item is either a block in the image or a Discrete Event Inport block that corresponds to an input port on a discrete event subsystem in the image.
Workaround: You can avoid the error by clearing the Specify event priority... check box for any block in the list above if the block receives a signal from a block in the list. Note that clearing the check box means that the event does not appear on the event calendar and has no explicit priority relative to other events that might be scheduled for the same time.

Summary: C281x CAP block generates an error during code generation
Description: While trying to generate code from a model containing a C281x CAP block, Simulink will error out with the following error message: Undefined function or variable 'useModule'
Workaround: You can work around this problem by manually changing the S-function parameters for the C281x DSP Chip Library.Make the C281x DSP Chip Library writeable. To do this:          In Windows Explorer, browse to        [installation-root]matlab\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000blks      Right click on c281xdspchiplib.mdl and select Properties.      In the Properties dialog box, clear the Read-only checkbox and click OK to close the dialog box.      Create a backup copy of c281xdspchiplib.mdl by copying and pasting the file with a different name of your choice.   Start MATLAB.In the MATLAB command window, type c281xdspchiplib to open the C281x DSP Chip Library.In the model window for the library select Edit > Unlock Library.Right-click on the C281x CAP block and select Look Under Mask.Replace the S-function parameters string with the following string:usemodule,interruptstatus,flagstatus,datasend,sampletime,softwarereset,adctrigger,datatype,unit1status,unit1edgeselect,unit1timebase,unit4timebase,unit1scaling,unit4scaling,postinterruptcap1,unit2status,unit2edgeselect,unit2timebase,unit5timebase,unit2scaling,unit5scaling,postinterruptcap2,unit3status,unit3edgeselect,unit3timebase,unit6timebase,unit3scaling,unit6scaling,postinterruptcap3Click OK and save the library.Restart MATLAB. The problem should then be gone.

Summary: Why can't I find the imaqadaptorkit.vcproj file?
Description: In the Image Acquisition Toolbox Adaptor Kit?user's guide, I am instructed to open the file $MATLAB\toolbox\imaq\imaqadaptors\kit\imaqadaptorkit.vcproj. This file does not seem to be present in my MATLAB installation.
Workaround: Extract the file in the attached zip file to the $MATLAB\toolbox\imaq\imaqadaptors\kit\ directory.

Summary: MWArrayAPI.chm listed as MWArray.chm in .NET Builder doc
Description: Documentation has incorrect name for file that contains the API for MWArray.
Workaround: The correct name for the location of the API documentaton for MWArray is as follows:matlabroot/help/toolbox/dotnetbuilder/MWArrayAPI/MWArrayAPI.chm

Summary: Why does my DCAM format 7 camera not work with the Image Acquisition Toolbox?
Description: I have a camera that is compliant with the version 1.3 of the IIDC (DCAM) specification. This camera supports format 7 video modes. Although the camera is detected by imaqhwinfo , when I try to create an object using videoinput  I receive an error.
Workaround: To work around this bug, please follow these directions:Exit MATLAB.Make a back-up copy of the file: /toolbox/imaq/imaqadaptors/win32/mwdcamimaq.dll.Download the attached file and extract the dll in it to the directory: /toolbox/imaq/imaqadaptors/win32.Start MATLAB.Please note that this fix will only work with version 1.10 of the Image Acquisition Toolbox.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB SVD function gives wrong answers with some complex matrices. Affects NORM and PINV.
Description: The SVD function in an Embedded MATLAB Function block sometimes gives the wrong answers for complex input matrices. Since NORM and PINV rely on SVD, they are also affected. Real input matrices are not affected.
Workaround: For release R2006a (version 6.4), follow these steps:Replace the file matlab/toolbox/eml/lib/matlab/svd.m with the attached svd.m file.Restart MATLAB.The attached svd.m file is designed to work with release R2006a, and should not be applied on top of any other releases.

Summary: Cannot manipulate linear plot in Lookup Table Editor
Description: When you try to drag a point in the linear plot in the Lookup Table Editor, the following error occurs: Entry is not a valid finite number.

Summary: rtwdemo_buildinfo: support files for demo missing
Description: When loading the demo model rtwdemo_buildinfo , a warning similar to the following appears in the MATLAB Command Window: Warning: Name is nonexistent or not a directory:D:\matlab\toolbox\rtw\rtwdemos\buildinfo_demo Real-Time Workshop is missing a directory that contains files necessary to use the demo rtwdemo_buildinfo  .
Workaround: If you are using Real-Time Workshop 6.4 (R2006a), Get the value of matlabroot, the directory in which MATLAB is installed on your system, by entering matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Quit MATLAB. Download one of the following attached compressed files:For Windows systems, download buildinfo_demo.zipFor Solaris, Linux, or Mac system, download buildinfo_demo.tar.gz  Extract the compressed file in matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtwdemos/.The extraction creates the directory buildinfo_demo.Restart MATLAB.Open the demo rtwdemo_buildinfo and follow the instructions provided.

Summary: save ignores -V6 flag with -append and character data
Description: The save  command in V7.0 (R14) and later has a -V6  flag that instructs it to save data in a format that can be read by older versions of MATLAB (version 6.0 and later). The -V6  flag is incorrectly ignored when all of the following conditions are met:save  is called with the -append  flag The variable being saved already exists in the MAT-file The variable being saved contains character data  In this case, MATLAB saves a MAT-file that contains UNICODE character data, and older versions of MATLAB (version 6.0 and later) cannot load this data.
Workaround: One workaround is to use the -V4 flag.  This workaround might not be feasible if you are also trying to save MATLAB objects or other data structures that are not supported in the older V4 MAT file format.An alternate workaround is to avoid the use of -append by loading and then resaving the data to a new MAT-file.

Summary: Integer division is sometimes incorrectly performed in floating point for Stateflow, Truth Table, and Embedded MATLAB Function blocks.
Description: Inside an Embedded MATLAB Function block, Stateflow block, or Truth Table block, an incorrect optimization sometimes converts an integer division into a floating-point division, yielding unexpected results.  This bug can occur in Real-Time Workshop and in simulation when debugging is turned off.This bug occurs when the following two conditions exist:The inputs to the division are integer constants. The result is written into a floating-point variable (single or double) or is otherwise involved in a larger floating-point calculation.  The following examples trigger this bug:xDouble = 22 / 7;  or  z = sin(22 / 7); These kinds of expressions may arise internally due to interactions with other optimizations, even when the expressions are not present in the original model.
Workaround: Turn off this optimization from the MATLAB command line. For releases prior to R2006a, type the following command:>> sf('Feature','Shrink Types',0)In R2006a, you need to issue this additional command:>> sf('Feature','Shrink Types Late',0)You must issue these commands every time you restart MATLAB.  You can automatically disable this optimization in your startup.m file.

Summary: mcc command hangs or terminates with a segmentation violation on the Mac.
Description: The mcc  command will sometimes hang or crash when first starting up on the Mac.
Workaround: Try running mcc again, or try quitting some other running programs and then re-running mcc.  This failure is sporadic.

Summary: Running profile('info') More Than Once Provides Incorrect Results
Description: If you run profile on...profile off and then runprofile('info') the results are correct. But if you then run profile('info')  again, the results are incorrect. Following is an example: function x=simple(x)  x = x + 1;profile on; simple(2); profile off;x = profile('info'); x.FunctionTable(1).ExecutedLinesans =    2.0000    1.0000    0.0000x = profile('info'); x.FunctionTable(1).ExecutedLinesans =    2.0000    2.0000    0.0000x = profile('info'); x.FunctionTable(1).ExecutedLinesans =    2.0000    3.0000    0.0000
Workaround: You can use any of these workarounds:Use profsave instead of profile('info').Use p = profile('info') and access p rather than rerunning profile('info')Clear the results using profile clear, and rerun profile on ... profile off; profile('info')

Summary: Model Advisor runs Real-Time Workshop checks when Real-Time Workshop is not installed
Description: Clicking Run Advisor  in the Model Advisor runs the following Real-Time Workshop-related checks even when Real-Time Workshop is not installed and the checks are invisible: Check solver for code generation Identify questionable blocks within the specified system Check for model reference configuration mismatch Check the hardware implementation Identify questionable software environment specifications Identify questionable code instrumentation (data I/O) Check for blocks that have constraints on tunable parameters Identify questionable subsystem settings  These checks run in addition to Simulink-related checks  selected in the left pane of the Model Advisor. The Real-Time Workshop checks cannot be disabled and report results that are not of importance for models intended to be used only for simulation. The Real-Time Workshop checks can also contain inoperable links, for example, to specific Configuration Parameters  dialog panes.
Workaround: Replace the following files with the corresponding attachmented files, then restart MATLAB:matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\simulink\@Simulink\@ModelAdvisor\createDiagnoseStartPage.pmatlabroot\toolbox\simulink\simulink\@Simulink\@ModelAdvisor\createLeftPane.p

Summary: License error message when running the DM642 EVM Picture-in-Picture Display, Audio and Video Pass-Through, or UDP Target to Host Communication demos
Description: When you try to run the DM642 EVM Picture-in-Picture Display, Audio and Video Pass-Through, or UDP Target to Host Communication demos, you get the following error message in MATLAB that says you do not have a license for Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder:Error building Real-Time Workshop target for block diagram 'dm642evmPIP'. MATLAB error message: Error using ==> RTW.makertw.Enter Unable to locate a license for Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder. The current target is: D:\Applications\MATLAB\R2006a\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic6000\tic6000\ti_c6000_ert.tlc  These demos use the ERT system target file ti_c6000_ert.tlc , which requires Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder. Without the license you cannot run the demos.
Workaround: Download the attached ZIP archive tic6000_patch_302096.zip and the TIC6000 patch installer tic6000_patch.m. After you download the files, start MATLAB and enter the following command at the MATLAB prompt to install the patch. tic6000_patch('tic6000_patch_302096.zip', 'install') This patch updates the affected demo models to switch automatically between ERT and GRT system target files depending on whether Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder is available.If Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder is available, the demo uses the ERT target file. If not, it uses the GRT target file.

Summary: Using API to delete signals and groups from Signal Builder block causes error
Description: When you use the signalbuilder  command to delete signals and groups, an error is generated at the command prompt and nothing is deleted from the block.
Workaround: Use the Signal Builder GUI to delete signals and groups.

Summary: LoadLibrary error when installing COM component created by MXLTOOL or DOTNETTOOL
Description: Customers attempting to install COM components created with MATLAB Builder for Excel and MATLAB Builder for .NET get the following error message:LoadLibrary() failed - The specified module could not be found. This error occurs because of a bug in the component packaging process in MATLAB Builder for Excel and MATLAB Builder for .NET.  The packaging process creates a component installer, implemented as a self-extracting executable, that customers run on their target machine to get access to the COM components. The component installer fails to include MCRRegCOMComponent.exe ,  a utility file, which registers the component. Because of this bug, the utility is missing, the component is not registered, the module cannot be found, and the installation process generates the error. NOTE: This bug affects COM components generated by MATLAB Builder for Excel MATLAB Builder for .NET. It does not affect .NET components created by MATLAB Builder for .NET.
Workaround: To work around this issue, the creator of the COM component has to build a new package that contains the missing utility, as follows:Download the  attached file MCRRegCOMComponent.exe to the machine where the COM component is being created.Open the project used to create the COM component.In the MATLAB Builder dialog, click Component->Package Component.In the Package Files dialog, click Add File.In the Add files to package dialog, navigate to, select, and open the utility that you downloaded in the first step.Add other files needed for the package and include the MCR and its location as needed.Click Create.The packaging process creates a component installer, which is an .exe file in the /distrib area of your project directory. Copy the component installer to the target machine and run it from a DOS command prompt.NOTE: The component installer pauses after running the MCR installer. Press the Enter key to make sure the registration process is completed.

Summary: Profiling command profile_mpc555 fails when using WindRiver Diab compiler version 5.3.0
Description: When using the WindRiver Diab compiler version 5.3.0 the profiling command profile_mpc555('can')  or profile_mpc555('serial')  fails with the following error message when processing the data from the target: ???  In an assignment  A(I) = B, the number of elements in B and I must be the same.  This is due to a change in the translation of the C source code into assembly instructions by the WindRiver Diab compiler version 5.3.0. This causes incorrect results to be stored on the target which are subsequently transmitted to MATLAB.
Workaround: Download the attached file profile.c.Execute the following command in matlabcd(fullfile(matlabroot, '\toolbox\rtw\targets\common\profile\execution')).This will take you to the directory where your exisiting profile.c lives.Rename the exisiting profile.c to profile.old.Copy the downloaded profile.c to the current directory.

Summary: Dialogs not rendering and missing units: Body, Joint Spring & Damper, Joint Initial Condition Actuator
Description: You cannot specify parameters on these blocks in units ofmm  (millimeters) mm/s  (millimeters per second) ft-lb  (foot-pounds)  If they were previously created using one of these units, the block dialogs might not render and instead might issue the following errors:Failed to create dialog schema. Contact technical support.: The 'ft-lb' enumerated value is invalid.  Failed to create dialog schema. Contact technical support.: The 'mm' enumerated value is invalid.  Failed to create dialog schema. Contact technical support.: The 'mm/s' enumerated value is invalid.
Workaround: Download the attached files to the following locations, overwriting your existing files of the same name. Then restart MATLAB.$matlabroot/toolbox/physmod/pm_util/pm_util/pm_define_unitenums.m$matlabroot/toolbox/physmod/mech/mech/@MECH/@MechJointSpringDamperStateHelper/CopyJointSpringDamperStateObj.p$matlabroot/toolbox/physmod/mech/mech/private/mech_buildJointSpringDamper.pIf you install SimDriveline, SimPowerSystems, or SimHydraulics after applying this workaround, you need to reapply the workaround.If you applied this workaround before May 16, 2006, you must reapply it using the new files. You should also delete any previous versions of pm_define_unitenums.p (the P-code version of the attached file) if you find it in its directory.

Summary: MATLAB Compiler fails if you use MCC with -d and %#external
Description: MATLAB Compiler fails if you use MCC with the -d  option to compile an MATLAB file that contains a %#external  pragma. The failure occurs at the mbuild stage of the process, generating an error message similar to the following example: > mcc -m function_name.m -d output_dirfatal error C1083: Cannot open include file:'output_dir\function_name_external.h': No such file or directory
Workaround: To work around the problem do either of the following:Avoid using the -d option. Instead, copy the output of MCC to the desired directory after the output is generatedUse the -M -I compiler options as well as -d.For example,-M -Ifull-path-to-cwd where full-path-to-cwd is the absolute path of the directory where MCC is being invoked. In this case, use the following command instead of the command shown in the first example:D:\mydirectory\mysubdir> mcc -m function_name.m -d output_dir -M -ID:\mydirectory\mysubdir

Summary: Maximum variable size in MATLAB
Description: Why do I get the error message? Maximum variable size allowed by the program is exceeded. This message means that one of two things happened:    The number of elements in a matrix  is too big     The matrix size is bigger than a hardware specific maximum amount of contiguous addressable virtual memory  The first condition occurs when the requested number of elements for a matrix (M*N)  is equal to or bigger than the MATLAB internal constant MWSIZE_MAX . The value of the constant MWSIZE_MAX  is dependent on the platform and MATLAB version. See Workaround  for the specific MWSIZE_MAX  values. The second condition occurs when the size of the matrix (M*N*ElementSize)  exceeds the CPU virtual memory bits. On 32-bit machines, you can address up to 4GB (2^32-1), and on 64-bit machines,  you can address up to 16EB (2^64-1) bytes of memory. However, not all the memory is available to the process. On 32-bit machines excluding Windows, the virtual address space is 3GB. On 32-bit Windows machines, the virtual address space of processes and applications is limited to 2GB unless the /3GB switch is used in the Boot.ini. (See http://www.microsoft.com/whdc/system/platform/server/PAE/PAEmem.mspx) On the 64-bit machines that we currently support, the maximum virtual address space of processes and applications is 2^48 (256TB). The current Opteron chips have 40 bits of physical address space and 48 bits virtual.  (See http://www.amd.com/us-en/Processors/ProductInformation/0,,30_118_8796_8805,00.html) The current Intel EM64T chips have the same 40/48 bits parameters. (See http://www.intel.com/technology/intel64/4071_intel_xeon_rev3.pdf) Note:  On Win64 in R2006b, the ZEROS function and other functions    which require zero-initialized memory are limited to 2^29-1 elements (from the Command line) and 2^31-2 elements (in scripts and functions). This issue is fixed in R2007a. Note:  On Win64 in R2006b, the error Maximum variable size allowed by MATLAB...  is not reported when it should be. This issue is fixed in R2007a.
Workaround: Starting with MATLAB version R14sp1, The MathWorks has been making progress to remove MATLAB specific limitations on the number of elements in a matrix on 64-bit platforms.These workarounds help you avoid the error message Maximum variable size allowed by MATLAB...  based on your release (R14sp1 through R2006b) and platform (64-bit or 32-bit). MATLAB releases R14sp1, R14sp2, R14sp3 on 32-bit and 64-bit machinesMATLAB releases R2006a and R2006b on 32-bit machines?MWSIZE_MAX = 2^31-1M*N < MWSIZE_MAXM*N*element_size < 3GBNote: By default on Windows 32-bit, the virtual space is limited to 2GB unless the /3GB switch is used in the Boot.ini but we always check against 3MB.MATLAB release R2006a on 64-bit Linux machines?MWSIZE_MAX =  2^31-1M*N < MWSIZE_MAXM*N*element_size < 16PB (PB=2^50)Note:The maximum M*N*element_size value is wrong but harmless because the first check for M*N is too strict.MATLAB release R2006a on 64-bit Windows machines?MWSIZE_MAX =  2^31-1M*N < MWSIZE_MAXM*N*element_size <  3GBNote: In R2006a, the values for the maximum M*N*element_size on 64-bit Windows and Linux machines are different. So, MATLAB on 64-bit Linux can create 64-bit mxArrays.MATLAB release R2006b on 64-bit machinesMWSIZE_MAX = 2^48-1M*N*element_size < MWSIZE_MAX

Summary: Signal storage reuse optimization interferes with algebraic loop occurrences minimization
Description: In a mulitasking model, when a multirate subsystem's Minimize algebraic loop occurrences  option is selected, code generation may fail to insert code to minimize algebraic loops. This results in incorrect generated code results.
Workaround: In a mulitasking model, uncheck Minimize algebraic loop occurrences for a multitrate subsystem.

Summary: SISO Design Tool Response Optimization errors: "Undefined function or variable 'linoptions'"
Description: Runing SRO frequency domain optimization from the Control System Toolbox's SISO Design Tool when Simulink Control Design is not installed causes an error: "Undefined function or variable 'linoptions'"
Workaround: To fix this problem download and replace the files:matlab\toolbox\sloptim\sloptim\@sroengine\@FrequencySpec\getCurrentResponse.mmatlab\toolbox\sloptim\sloptim\@sroengine\@FrequencySpec\private\getSensitivity.m

Summary: Stateflow charts using qualified data/event/function names can get corrupted in certain situations.
Description: When a Stateflow chart uses qualified data, event, or function names, such as stateA.stateB.data , the chart can become corrupted in either of the following situations:If any state has one or more siblings with the same name, Stateflow may truncate any qualified name label in the chart that refers to that state. For example, suppose stateA  contains two substates called stateB  at the same level in the Stateflow hierarchy. Stateflow may truncate label strings that refer to stateA.stateB.data  after you perform any of the following operations:        Parse or build the chart     Edit the affected label strings     Right click and explore symbols referenced by the affected label strings     Add, delete, or rearrange transitions or states in the chart       When you use right-mouse drag and drop to copy a state that resides in the hierarchical path of a qualified name, Stateflow may truncate all label strings that contain that name. For example, suppose stateA  contains stateB . If you copy stateB  using drag and drop, Stateflow may truncate all label strings in the chart that refer to stateA.stateB .
Workaround: If your Stateflow chart uses qualified names, you can work around this issue by:Making sure no sibling states have the same nameNot using drag and drop to copy Stateflow objects. You can use Ctrl-C/Ctrl-V as long as you paste the copy in a different parent.

Summary: Using xPC Target external mode with inlined parameters corrupts parameter objects in MATLAB workspace
Description: Using inlined parameters with xPC Target Simulink external mode corrupts the MATLAB workspace parameter objects that are used for tunable parameters in the model. The corruption occurs after connecting to the target PC application.
Workaround: As a workaround, reset the values of the parameter object in the MATLAB workspace to the correct value after you connect to the target PC application.
Products: Simulink Real-Time, xPC Target Embedded Option

Summary: find_system with regular expressions can be slow
Description: Simulink's find_system command operates on many parameter-value pairs. Some of these pairs are used as settings for how find_system should operate, whereas others are used to specify for what find_system should be looking. In the case where find_system is using regular expressions, (the pair: 'RegExp', 'On') all string typed search constraints will use regular expressions to search. Often, the intent is for one of those constraints to be a regular expression, and for the others to be literal. Use of regular expressions for literal strings is less efficient than it has been in prior releases.
Workaround: If you have a search that uses multiple string search constraints, and some are regular expressions and some are literal, separate these into separate calls to find_system. For example:list = find_system(gcs, 'Regexp', 'On', 'BlockType', 'Gain', 'Name', '^f.*r$');Can be optimized as:list = find_system(gcs, BlockType', 'Gain'); list = find_system(list, 'Regexp', 'On', 'Name', '^f.*r$');

Summary: Segmentation fault when resizing or repainting a figure with ActiveX in a a deployed application
Description: If you deploy an application created with MCC, and if the application contains a figure containing an ActiveX control, and if the figure is resized or repainted, the application will terminate because of a segmentation violation. The problem occurs even though the application might execute without any problems in MATLAB.
Workaround: Extract the attached file (patch.zip)to the following location:MCRROOT/java/where MCRROOT is the location of the MCR used to run the compiled app.  This operation should create the following files:MCRROOT/java/patch/com/mathworks/hg/peer/ActiveXCanvas.classMCRROOT/java/patch/com/mathworks/hg/peer/ActiveXCanvas$1.classMCRROOT/java/patch/com/mathworks/hg/peer/ActiveXCanvas$2.classIf the user wishes to test compiled apps directly against a MATLAB installation, then that installation must be patched by extracting patch.zip to MATLABROOT/java/.

Summary: Context-sensitive help links broken for some C2000 blocks
Description: For some of the blocks in Embedded Target for TI C2000?, the context-sensitive help link to the doc is broken. This includes both the Help button in the block's dialog box (where applicable) and the Help item in the context menu that appears when you right-click on the block's icon in its library. The following blocks are affected: C2000 Target Preferences blocksBlocks in the C281x DSP Chip Support Library except From Memory and To MemoryHardware Interrupt and Idle Task blocks in the C280x DSP Chip Support Library Here is a complete alphabetical list of affected blocks: C280x Hardware InterruptC281x ADCC281x CAPC281x eCAN ReceiveC281x eCAN TransmitC281x GPIO Digital InputC281x GPIO Digital OutputC281x Hardware InterruptC281x PWMC281x QEPC281x SCI ReceiveC281x SCI TransmitC281x SPI ReceiveC281x SPI TransmitC281x TimerCustom C280x BoardCustom C281x BoardF2808 eZdspF2812 eZdspIdle TaskLF2407 eZdsp
Workaround: To view the help topics for these blocks:Open the Help browser in one of these ways:      Select Help > Full Product Family Help from the MATLAB main menu   Type doc in the MATLAB command window   In the Help Navigator, scroll to the Embedded Target for TI C2000? DSP node, and double-click to open it.Click on the BlocksˇŞBy Category or BlocksˇŞAlphabetical List node. If you click on BlocksˇŞBy Category, then in the content pane click on the appropriate category link, then click on the block name.If you click on BlocksˇŞAlphabetical List, then in the content pane click on the block name.

Summary: Some frequency transformations are not correct on dfilt.allpass, dfilt.wdfallpass, dfilt.cascadeallpass, dfilt.cascadewdfallpass
Description: In some cases, the frequency transformations for the following structures are wrong: dfilt.allpassdfilt.wdfallpassdfilt.cascadeallpassdfilt.cascadewdfallpass  Here are the cases where the transformations are not correct.The transformation requires that the transformed filter's numerator be a negated version of the denominator.The transformed filter should be complex.
Workaround: It is possible to work around the first case by cascading a gain of -1 with the resulting filter--dfilt.scalar(-1).Work around the complex case error by constructing a dfilt.df2 from the prototype allpass filter first, and then applying the transformation to this df2 filter.

Summary: Workspace browser shows signed representations of unsigned integers
Description: The Workspace browser's Value  column shows incorrect, signed values for unsigned integer values. For example, when executingx = uint8(254) the Command Window correctly shows that x  equals 254 , but the Workspace browser incorrectly shows a value of  -2 .
Workaround: To see the correct value of an unsigned integer, type the variable name in the Command Window and press Enter.

Summary: Code Composer Studio compiler warnings for Sort block
Description: When you use the Sort block in a model built for a TI C6000 target, the block triggers compiler warnings from Code Composer Studio when you generate and build code to your target. These warnings appear if you select the Inline run-time library functions  option in Real-Time Workshop Embedded Target  in the model configuration parameters.At build time, the compiler issues the following warning for each of the inlined sort C run-time files:"D:/MATLAB/MATLAB71/toolbox/rtw/dspblks/c/dspqsrt/sort_ins_idx_d_rt.c", line 13: warning: external/internal linkage conflict with previous declaration
Workaround: Eliminate the warning by modifying your local copy of the filetoolbox/dspblks/include/dspsrt_rt.hIn dspsrt_rt.h, make the following change to the lines at the top of the file. Change the second #define line:#ifdef DSPQSRT_EXPORTS#define DSPQSRT_EXPORT EXPORT_FCN#else#define DSPQSRT_EXPORT extern % This is the line to change.#endifto the following:#ifdef DSPQSRT_EXPORTS#define DSPQSRT_EXPORT EXPORT_FCN#else#define DSPQSRT_EXPORT MWDSP_IDECL % Replaced extern by MWDSP_IDECL.#endif

Summary: The xcorr function in an Embedded MATLAB Function block doesn't match MATLAB scaling in cross correlation with the coeff option
Description: In an Embedded MATLAB Function block, the xcorr  function scales differently from MATLAB in cross correlation with the coeff  option.  Example:  y = xcorr([1,2],[2,3],1,'coeff') In MATLAB: y = 0.3721 0.9923 0.4961 In an Embedded MATLAB Function block: y = 0.3750 1.0000 0.5000

Summary: Graphs showing RTDX feedback for speed and PWM on "DC Motor Control via RTDX" demo interface may not update.
Description: The first time you run the "DC Motor Control via RTDX" demo during a MATLAB session, the two graphs that show feedback for speed and PWM on the user interface may not update.This problem is due to an incorrect command being called when the RTDX channel is opened.
Workaround: Close MATLAB.Browse to the following directory:[matlabroot]\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000demosBackup these three files by renaming them:runc2812speedcontrolDC.mspeedControlDCLoop.mspeedcontrolDL.m Copy the three attached files (updated versions of the renamed files) into the \tic2000demos directory.Open MATLAB.Type rehash toolboxcache at the command prompt.

Summary: Period of polar algorithm in randn documentation is incorrect.
Description: The reference page and MATLAB-file help for randn  incorrectly state that the period of the generator based on the polar algorithm is (2^32-1)*(pi/8). The correct period is (2^31-1)*(pi/8).

Summary: Body Add CS and Custom Joint Add Primitive buttons disabled upon first opening either dialog
Description: When you first open any Body dialog, its Add CS  button is disabled.When you first open any Custom Joint dialog, its Add Primitive  button is disabled.
Workaround: Select any item in the table of Body coordinate systems (CSs) or the table of joint primitives, in the Body or Custom Joint dialog, respectively.Once you have made a selection, the Add CS or Add Primitive button, respectively, is enabled.

Summary: Current Directory browser: sorting by Last Modified column is sometimes slow
Description: In the Current Directory browser, sorting files by clicking the Last Modified  column can be slow.

Summary: NI PCI-6722 sampling frequency limited for more than four channels
Description: Attempts to add more than 4 channels to the PCI-6722 analog output device result in the maximum rate being set incorrectly: addchannel(ao,[1:6]);rates = propinfo(ao,'SampleRate')rates =  Type: 'double'  Constraint: 'bounded'  ConstraintValue: [0.6000 0]  DefaultValue: 1000  ReadOnly: 'whileRunning'  DeviceSpecific: 0  Note that the maximum sample rate is 0.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you will need to update two files:Quit MATLABRename the following dll and csf-file:$MATLAB\toolbox\daq\daq\private\mwnidaqmx.dll$MATLAB\toolbox\daq\daq\private\mwnidaqmx.dll.csf(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typingmatlabrootat the MATLAB Command Prompt.)Rename the files to mwnidaqmx.dll.old and mwnidaqmx.dll.csf.oldDownload and extract the attached file(s). Place the new file(s) in the same directory mentioned in step 2.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Signal Builder blocks created with API do not display requirements in Requirements pane
Description: When you use the signalbuilder  command to create Signal Builder blocks, the Requirements  pane of the Verification Manager in the Signal Builder dialog box fails to display requirements. In particular, the Requirements  pane does not allow you to link requirements documents to the Signal Builder block's test groups and their scheduled Model Verification blocks. If you attempt to link requirements to test groups using the Requirements  pane, note that Simulink associates those requirements with the Signal Builder block itself. Hence, you can view the requirements only from the block's context menu.
Workaround: After using the signalbuilder function to create a new Signal Builder block, select the block to make it current and then enter the following command at the MATLAB prompt:set_param(gcb,'MaskType','Sigbuilder block')This enables the block to accept and display requirements using the Requirements pane of the Verification Manager in its dialog box.

Summary: Warning: File or URL Not Found
Description: Generated reports that display in the MATLAB Web browser might contain links that do not work. Instead, the warningFile or URL Not Found  appears.  In R2006a, when you generate an HTML report, a warning appears in the Command Window alerting you to the possible problem and a workaround: Warning: Not using system browser.  Some links may not work.To change go to MATLAB -> Preferences -> Report Generator -> View command
Workaround: This problem does not occur if you display the generated reports in your system browser (for example, Internet Explorer). You can change the default Report Generator HTML viewer from the MATLAB Web browser to your system browser. To do soSelect File > Preferences > Report Generator > View command.By default, the Report Generator view command isweb(rptgen.file2urn('%<FileName>'))which uses the MATLAB Web Browser. Change the view command to the following to use your system browser:web(rptgen.file2urn('%<FileName>'), '-browser')If you prefer to use the MATLAB Web browser, you can prevent the problem by using the following view command, but it might not work in all circumstances:web('%<FileName>')

Summary: Simulation results sometimes incorrect when motion-actuating a joint containing three prismatic primitives
Description: If you motion-actuate the third prismatic primitive of a joint, and the joint is cut as part of a closed loop, the simulation yields wrong results. This applies to any joint that has three prismatic primitives, including 6-DoF and Custom Joint.
Workaround: If you are only actuating one or two prismatics on the same joint, and the joint has three prismatic primitives, be sure not to actuate the last primitive in the dialog. You can do so by moving the actuated prismatics up in the primitive list.If you are actuating all three prismatics, duplicate the joint. Then actuate two prismatics on one joint and one on the other, making sure you do not actuate the third dialog prismatic in either joint.

Summary: MATLAB can falsely report syntax errors on complicated expressions with { }
Description: MATLAB syntax treats pairs of curly braces ( { } ) in two different ways.  When constructing a cell array, whitespace can behave like a comma:    { a b }  is the same as { a, b }However, when indexing into a cell array, whitespace is ignored:    x{ a b } is illegal -- you must write x{ a, b }A coding error introduced in MATLAB 7.0 causes whitespace inside of certain indexing cell array expressions to be treated as comma.  In order for this bug to be triggered, there needs to be a space between the cell array and the index (for example,  x {a,b} ), the index needs to have whitespace that might become a comma if the { } were an array constructor, and the whole indexing expression needs to be inside of at least one set of parentheses, brackets, or braces.  The simplest example that triggers this bug is thus something like:    a( b { c (1,2) } )which gives a fallacious message about unbalanced parentheses.Since most MATLAB users do not leave spaces between an array and the following parenthesis or curly brace, this bug has been present for years without manifesting itself until recently.This bug is almost certain to cause some kind of syntax error message when it is triggered.  It will be fixed in R2006b.
Workaround: In a cell array indexing expression, remove any whitespace between the cell array name and the left curly brace ( { ).

Summary: Negative simulation time in a multirate model can result in a wrong answer or hang
Description: Simulating a multirate model that has a negative start time can miss the sample hits of certain tasks at negative simulation times resulting in wrong answers. Additionally, the simulation may get stuck at a negative simulation time. If it does get stuck, you can stop it by clicking Stop  on the model editor toolbar or Simulation  menu.
Workaround: Do not use negative simulation times. Instead, scale time to start at 0.

Summary: Subsystems: asynchronous subsystem in noninlined atomic subsystem can cause undeclared variables
Description: If all of the following conditions exist for a model, Real-Time Workshop fails to compile the code it generates for the model due to undeclared local variable errors: The model includes an asynchronous subsystem (uses an Async Interrupt block) that is in an atomic subsystem. The atomic subsystem is configured to generate a function instead of inline code, based on the setting of Real-Time Workshop system code  on the subsystem's parameters dialog box. The asynchronous subsystem declares local variables.   Real-Time Workshop fails to declare local variables in the code generated for asynchronous rate and the following compilation error occurs, where rtb_Gain  is the name of a local variable used in the asynchronous subsystem: model.c(67) : error C2065: 'rtb_Gain' : undeclared identifier
Workaround: For the asynchronous subsystem, set the subsystem parameter Real-Time Workshop system code to Inline.

Summary: Opening OPC Toolbox block library dialogs generates warning
Description: When you open an OPC Toolbox library block, the following warning appears at the MATLAB command line:Warning: The input to STR2FUNC "untitled/OPC Configuration" is not a valid function name. This will generate an error in a future release.
Workaround: As a workaround, you can turn off this warning by typing the following command at the MATLAB command line:warning off MATLAB:str2func:invalidFunctionNameNote: If you want to make this change for every MATLAB session you run, you should put the command into your local or system-wide startup file. For information on how to modify your local or system-wide startup files, typedoc matlabrc

Summary: S-Function Builder block corrupts its Outputs pane code
Description: Repeatedly opening and closing the S-Function Builder dialog box can result in the block corrupting the code you entered in its Outputs  pane. For example, several lines of the Outputs  pane code might disappear.
Workaround: Continue to open and close the S-Function Builder dialog box until the code in its Outputs pane appears intact.

Summary: Tiled printing issue on Windows
Description: You will get an error printing a model where all of the following is true:- Using tiled printing- Printing multiple subsystems at once- Printer name contains spaces- Printing on any version of Windows
Workaround: Download the attached simprintdlg.m file and use it to replace the corresponding file in your MATLAB installation at MATLABROOT\toolbox\simulink\simulink\simprintdlg.m. Make sure to delete simprintdlg.p in the same directory if it exists.

Summary: Interactive ActiveX control creation tool actxcontrolselect starts in a very small window
Description: The interactive ActiveX control creation tool displayed by the MATLAB command actxcontrolselect  might start with a very small window in which only the title bar is visible. This can happen when using the actxcontrolselect  command directly or when adding ActiveX controls in GUIDE.
Workaround: Download the attached file, actxcontrolselect.m and place it in the following directory to replace the existing file having the same name. You should rename or make a copy of the existing file first: MATLABROOT\toolbox\matlab\winfun\MATLABROOT is the directory where you installed MATLAB. You can run the matlabroot command in MATLAB to get the directory name.If the problem persists after downloading the file, then resize the resulting window to a proper size.The attached file is for MATLAB version R2006a only.

Summary: Slow response using the STOP command with National Instruments cards using hardware triggering.
Description: When I attempt to stop a National Instruments card with queued data and TriggerType='HwDigital' there is a delay of more than 10 seconds.Example Code:% sampling frequency of 1kHzao1=analogoutput('nidaq','Dev1');addchannel(ao1, 0);ao1.TriggerType = 'HwDigital';samples=zeros(100, 1);putdata(ao1,samples); start(ao1) tic stop(ao1) toc Elapsed time is 10.233110 seconds.

Summary: Tornado target: download to target does not work
Description: If you build a model with external mode support for a Tornado target and try to use external mode to download the resulting executable to the target system, the download fails and the following error message appears: Error building Real-Time Workshop target for block diagram model _68k_win32 . MATLAB error message: Undefined function or variable 'make'
Workaround: Generate the Tornado target executable and then load the executable onto the target system manually.

Summary: Error occurs when using AVIFILE/ADDFRAME and MOVIE2AVI on Windows XP 64-bit.
Description: MATLAB currently defaults to using Indeo codecs to compress video frames when using AVIFILE/ADDFRAME and MOVIE2AVI.If you attempt to use AVIFILE/ADDFRAME or MOVIE2AVI on a Windows XP 64-bit platform without specifying the compression type, you will see an error message indicating the codec was not found.Non-default settings must be explicitly passed in when using these commands on Windows XP 64 because Microsoft does not provide Indeo codecs on this platform.This issue does not affect 32-bit Windows XP installations.
Workaround: 1) Explicitly specify no compression when creating the AVIFILE or when calling MOVIE2AVI - for example:  aviobj = avifile('myvideo.avi', 'compression', 'none');  or  movie2avi(mov, 'myvideo.avi', 'compression', 'none');2) Specify a codec for a compression that is installed.  A list of installed codecs can be found by going to:Start->Programs->Accessories->System Tools->System Information->Components->Multimedia->Video codecsThe ones that are currently included with Windows XP 64 are:IYUV:  Intel YUV codec (c:\winnt\system32\iyuv_32.dll)MRLE:  Microsoft RLE codec (c:\winnt\system32\msrle32.dll) MSVC:  Microsoft Video 1 codec (c:\winnt\system32\msvidc32.dll) For example, to use the Intel YUV codec, use the four-CC code:  aviobj = avifile('myvideo.avi', 'compression', 'IYUV');Other codecs can be found at http://fourcc.org.Note there are restrictions with some codecs - for example, some codecs can only be used with grayscale images.3) Run the 32-bit version of MATLAB using the command line switch:matlab -win32

Summary: An error message appears at the command line when invoking data cursor mode on a SystemTest Test Results Viewer plot.
Description: The following error will occur when attempting to add a data tip (Data Cursor mode) to a plot created within the SystemTest Test Results Viewer. ??? Undefined function or variable 'newstr'. Error in ==> datacursormode>localUpdatePanel at 875  pText = text('Visible','off','String',newstr,'Units',get(hPanel,'Units'),'FontSize',... Warning: Error occurred while evaluating listener callback. > In graphics.datatip.updatestring at 62  In graphics.datatip.updatePositionAndString at 19  In graphics.datatip.update at 30  In datacursormode>localWindowButtonDownFcnPanel at 846  In datacursormode>localWindowButtonDownFcn at 802  In hgfeval at 54  In uitools.uimode.modeWindowButtonDownFcn at 54  In uitools.uimode.modeControl>localModeWindowButtonDownFcn at 71 ??? Undefined function or variable 'newstr'. Error in ==> uitools.uimode.modeControl>localModeWindowButtonDownFcn at 71 hThis.modeWindowButtonDownFcn(hFig,evd,hThis,newValue); ??? Error while evaluating figure WindowButtonDownFcn
Workaround: On line 35 of the MATLAB file:(matlabroot)/toolbox/systemtest/viewer/@hdsviewer/@plotEditor/newplot.mreplace the property-value pair "'HandleVisibility','off'" with "'HandleVisibility','callback'". Specifically replace line 35 of this file with:set(f,'WindowStyle','docked','HandleVisibility','callback','NumberTitle','off','visible','on');?

Summary: Joint Spring & Damper block causes simulation failure if dialog fields reference unassigned variables
Description: When you try to simulate or update a model containing a Joint Spring & Damper block, you can experience the following failures:A strange error message apears at the command line indicating an invalid Simulink handle but not the source of the error (R2006a Version 2.4). A segmentation violation occurs (R14SP3 Version 2.3).  This error can occur if any of the parameters referenced by the Joint Spring & Damper have not been assigned at the time of model update or simulation.
Workaround: The workaround contains two parts.A. To ensure that the correct error messages are displayed and no segmentation violations occur,Download the release-appropriate attached file. Rename the file by removing the underscore-release extension.Delete the existing file of the same name in the directory:$matlabroot/toolbox/physmod/mech/mech/Copy the renamed downloaded file to this directory. Restart MATLAB.B. To ensure that the referenced block parameter variables are assigned values, add in your model, under File > Model Properties > Callbacks > PostLoadFcn, a PostLoadFcn script that initializes the variables used to represent the Joint Spring & Damper block parameters.

Summary: M-Lint warnings about unused variables are misleading, such as for varargin
Description: M-Lint provides this message Input variable 'varargin' appears never to be used. Typically when you see this M-Lint message, you remove the variables that are never used. However in this case, varargin  represents a variable length input argument list for a function. If you remove varargin , MATLAB errors when the function runs. Instead, ignore the M-Lint message.
Workaround: To suppress a misleading M-Lint message, add the M-Lint tag %#ok at the end of the line in your MATLAB-file. M-Lint suppresses messages about any lines in a filename that have %#ok at the end.You can override the %#ok tags in a file by running the mlint function with the -notok option.

Summary: Dimensionless units not supported in SimBiology
Description: If you create a unit such as mole/item (for example: mole/cell), in SimBiology both mole and item are units of physical quantity of the type ˇ®amountˇŻ; this results in a dimensionless unit, which is not supported in SimBiology.

Summary: MATLAB API not accessable or engine applications fail on Windows x64 systems
Description: You may encounter any of the following issues on a Windows x64 system Access to the MATLAB Application Programmer's Interface (API) from other programming languages fail Engine applications fail Calls to actxserver('matlab.application') fail  These problems can indicate that MATLAB is not registered as an automation server. This can happen if certain Microsoft Visual Studio 2005 runtime libraries are not installed on your system when you run the MathWorks Installer. If these libraries are missing, the installation still succeeds; however, the installer finishes without registering MATLAB as an OLE Automation server, and does not issue a warning.
Workaround: Start MATLAB, specifying the regserver option with the matlab command. This command installs the required libraries and registers MATLAB as an OLE automation server.NOTE: You may need to have administrator privileges to do this.

Summary: C6711DSK Audio DAC block outputs wrong value when input amplitude is +1.0
Description: The C6711DSK ADC block outputs an incorrect DAC voltage when the amplitude of the input signal to the block has a numerical value of exactly +1.0 in normalized scaling mode. The correct DAC output voltage is the maximum allowed positive DAC voltage, but the block outputs the most negative allowed value. This occurs only when you select Normalize  for the scaling mode.
Workaround: Download the attached ZIP archive tic6000_patch_295925.zip and the TIC6000 patch installer tic6000_patch.m. After you download the patch and installer, start MATLAB and enter the following command at the MATLAB command prompt to install the patch.tic6000_patch('tic6000_patch_295925.zip', 'install')Patch tic6000_patch_295925.zip updates the file c6000_satmpy.tlc to use the correct saturation arithmetic.

Summary: RTDX-based demos fail on C54x simulator family when you use CCS 3.1
Description: When you use CCS 3.1 with this release, RTDX-related demos RTDX Tutorial (rtdxtutorial ) and LMS Adaptive Filtering (rtdxlmsdemo ), do not run properly on simulators for the C54x processor family. The demos fail because CCS 3.1 shipped with the wrong C54x simulator RTDX library.
Workaround: If you have the c54x RTDX simulator library from an earlier CCS release, such as CCS V2.2, you can modify and rebuild the demo project to use the earlier version of the library to overcome the failure.When the RTDX-based demo fails because of a build problem, go to CCS IDE and follow these steps to modify the project build options to use your earlier version RTDX libraries:In CCS IDE, select Project->Build Options to open the build options for the active project.Click Linker.In Include Libraries, look for one of the following libraries.  rtdxsim.lib  rtdxsim_ext.libReplace each occurrence with an earlier version of the same library, such as those shown here:  CCS_earlier_version\rtdxsim.lib  CCS_earlier_version\rtdxsim_ext.libwhere CCS_earlier_version is the path to the earlier version of the C54x RTDX simulator library.Usually, you find the simulator libraries in CCS_root\C5400\rtdx\lib, where CCS_root is your CCS installation directory.

Description: The fitsread  function fails when reading data from binary tables if the data type is a bit type with a multiple of 8 bits, such as 8-bit (TFORMn value of X8) and 16-bit (X16). The problem is caused by the fitsinfo  function which returns an incorrect data precision value when called by fitsread .

This error occurs because multibandread  calls the memmapfile  function, which can fail if the size of the file is greater than the largest block of contiguous memory that is available.

Summary: Get error "OCX registration error: The activeX controls for the Data Acquisition Toolbox failed to register." while installing.
Description: While installing MATLAB, I receive the message:"OCX registration error: The activeX controls for the Data Acquisition Toolbox failed to register. Please rerun the installation. "
Workaround: This error appears innocuous. We have found that you can continue the installation, and run the Data Acquisition Toolbox without any side effects.

Summary: From File and To File blocks: memory corruption assertion can occur during code generation for models that include multiple From File or To File blocks that specify long file names
Description: When you generate code for a model that includes multiple From File or To File blocks that specify long pathnames for the Filename  block parameter, Real-Time Workshop might report a memory corruption assertion. An example of a long file name is C:\aaaaaa\bbbbbbbb\cccccccccc\dddddd\eeeeeee\fff\gggggggggg.mat  The stack trace that results from the memory corruption assertion follows:  Assertion failed: Forced Assertion at line 732 of file "memmgr/memdebug.cpp". Invalid Memory Block Stack Trace:   [0] libmwbridge.so:ThrowAssertion  [1] libmwbridge.so:MATLABAssertFcn  :  [5] libmwsimulink.so:sluGetUniqueToFileNames  :
Workaround: Avoid specifying long pathnames for the From File and To File block Filename parameter.

Summary: Incorrect values from constraint block in exported model
Description: If you export a model from the Model-Browser to Simulink, then the constraint output from the exported block has incorrect values, i.e. when the evaluation point is inside the design bounds it has a large positive value, when it should have a negative value.
Workaround: A patch is available that will fix this error in v3.0. To install this fix: Rename the file $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcdesign\@xregdesign\slconstraints.m to $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcdesign\@xregdesign\slconstraints.m.old. Download the file attachment_294453_20060105.zip that is listed in the Attachments section.Unzip the contents of this file, slconstraints.m.Replace your installed version of the file $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcdesign\@xregdesign\slconstraints.m. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Error when exporting boundary model to Simulink
Description: If you export a constraint (boundary model) from the boundary editor to Simulink then you see an error like this: ??? Error using ==> add_block A new block named 'OneStage_Constraint/OneStage_Constraint/X1 (x) [1/s]' cannot be added. ??? Error while evaluating uimenu Callback.   You also get an empty Simulink block, rather than a block containing the constraint.
Workaround: A patch is available that will fix this error in v3.0. To install this fix: Rename the file $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcdesign\@conbase\mv2sl.m to $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcdesign\@conbase\mv2sl.m.old. Download the file attachment_294452_20060106.zip that is listed in the Attachments section.Unzip the contents of this file, mv2sl.m.Replace your installed version of the file $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcdesign\@conbase\mv2sl.m. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Code generation stops with base sample time error message
Description: When you generate code from your Simulink model, you get an error message that reads:Error building Real-Time Workshop target for block diagram 'untitled'. MATLAB error message:Error using ==> checkModelAndSystem_DSPtargetThe base sample time of the model is too long to fit in the 32-bit timer period register.  Changing the model base sample rate does not fix the problem and Simulink repeatedly displays the message. You get this error because the base rate of your model is too low. Your base rate specifies a sample period that overflows the 32-bit timer period register used to store the CPU timer sample period.If you encounter this problem, save your work, close Embedded Target for TI C6000, exit MATLAB, and restart.

Summary: CLEAR CLASSES after loading Simulink gives warnings
Description: If you issue the command clear classes  after loading Simulink, you will see the following warnings in the MATLAB command window: Warning: Objects of mpt.SLDataObjectCustomizer class exist - not clearing this class or any of its super-classes Warning: Objects of mpt.RTWBuildCustomizer class exist - not clearing this class or any of its super-classes Warning: Objects of mpt.MiscCustomizer class exist - not clearing this class or any of its super-classes

Summary: Resizing a figure with a legend with custom font settings and best location can error
Description: Resizing a figure containing a legend with custom font settings and with the 'Location'  property set to 'best'  may cause the legend to revert to the default size.
Workaround: You can either position the figure before creating the legend or setting the font properties, or set the legend 'Location' property to something other than 'best'.

Summary: In the SCI-Based Host-Target Communication demo, the Port setting is hard-coded to COM2
Description: In the block masks for the Host-side SCI Transmit and Receive blocks in the SCI-Based Host-Target Communication demo, it appears that you can set the COM Port to COM1 , COM2 , COM3 , or COM4 . But changing the value of this setting in the block mask actually has no effect. This value is hard-coded to COM2 . Therefore the SCI-Based Host-Target Communication demo works properly only if the serial port of your host PC is configured to COM2. Otherwise, the demo will not be able to establish proper RS232 communications between the target and the host PC, and the demo will generate the following error message: Port: COM2 is not available.
Workaround: A simple workaround is to change the COM port setting on the host PC to COM2. This workaround lets you run the demo without downloading a fix.Open the Control Panel from the Start menu.Double-click System to open the System Properties dialog box.In the the System Properties dialog box, click the Hardware tab, then the Device Manager button. The Device Manager window opens.Open the Ports (COM & LPT) node and double click on the Communications Port device. The Communications Port Properties dialog box opens.Click the Port Settings tab and click the Advanced button. the Advanced Settings dialog box opens.In the Advanced Settings dialog, change the COM Port Number to COM2. Click OK to close the Advanced Settings dialog.Click OK to close the Communications Port Properties dialog.In the main menu of the Device Manager window, select Action > Scan for hardware changes.Close the Device Manager window.Click OK to close the System Properties window. Close the Control Panel.To fix the problem permanently, install the attached files. After installing these files, the demo will be fixed and COM port settings on the SCI blocks will work properly.After downloading the three attached files:Close MATLAB.Browse to the following directory:matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000blksCreate backup copies of hostside_sci_tx_callback.m and hostside_sci_rx_callback.m by renaming the files to hostside_sci_tx_callback_bak.m and hostside_sci_tx_callback_bak.m.Download hostside_sci_rx_callback.m and hostside_sci_tx_callback.m in the directory.Browse to the following directory:matlabroot\toolbox\shared\etargets\etargets\@UARTCreate a backup copy of enableSerialobj.m by renaming the file to enableSerialobj_bak.m.Download enableSerialobj.m in the directory.Open MATLAB, type rehash toolboxcache in the Command Window, then press return.

Summary: addtodate returns incorrect date when adding more than 115 months
Description: The addtodate  function returns an incorrect answer if you attempt to add more than approximately 115 months to the given date. The command fails because addtodate  rolls the date number over to a previous date. The correct return value for the following call to addtodate  is 737334, but MATLAB returns 729542 instead:    astart = 733682;   addtodate(astart, 120, 'month')   ans =      729542  This bug only shows up when adding months; it does not affect adding years or days.
Workaround: If it is necessary to add more than 110 months, break up the operation into steps of no more than 110.

Summary: Clearing a breakpoint in a built-in function that does not have breakpoints causes a segmentation violation
Description: If you try to clear a breakpoint in a built-in function that does not have any breakpoints, MATLAB produces a segmentation violation. For example, dbclear all clear  produces a segmentation violation. Try to save your work and restart MATLAB.

Summary: mcc fails to create COM components when MATLAB is installed in a directory containing spaces.
Description: When MATLAB is installed in directory containing spaces, the following error occurs when trying to build COM components:? --> "midl /nologo /win32 /I "D:\Program Files\MATLAB71\extern\include"  /D "NDEBUG" "mwcomtypes.idl""  include fatal error C1083: Cannot open source file: 'Files\MATLAB71\extern\include': No such file or directory mwcomtypes.idl Processing D:\Program Files\MATLAB71\extern\include\mwcomtypes.idl midl : command line error MIDL1003 : error returned by the C preprocessor (2)    D:\PROGRAM FILES\MATLAB71\BIN\WIN32\\..\MEX.PL: Error: IDL compile of 'mwcomtypes.idl' failed.
Workaround: This is due a bug in MSVC 6.0. To work around this problem do either of the following:    Install MATLAB in a directory without spaces.OR?    Identify all the directories, in the path where MATLAB is installed (refered to as <matlabroot> in the rest of the solution), that have spaces in them.    From DOS prompt, CD into the parent directories of each of the directories identified above.    Use    dir /x    to determine the 8.3 name for the concerned directory (This name is shown in the column before the actual name of the directory)    Recreate the path to <matlabroot> by replacing all the directories that have spaces in them with their 8.3 names.    Modify the    set MATLAB=%MATLAB%    line (#12) in <matlabroot>\bin\win32\mbuildopts\msvc60compp.bat with the path created in the above step.    Rerun    mbuild -setup    OR?    Upgrade to Microsoft Visual Studio 7.1 or later.

Summary: Cannot create a one-stage response model from the command-line
Description: If you try to create a new response model in a one-stage test plan from the command-line, you get the following error:  ??? Input argument "GlobalModel" is undefined.

Summary: dicomread returns "Not enough compressed data" error
Description: When importing images that have been compressed using Run Length Encoding (RLE) from a DICOM file, dicomread  may produce the following error:   ??? Error using ==> medformats\private\dicom_read_encapsulated  Not enough compressed data. This error only occurs when the encoded data has a run of one pixel for the last entry in the RLE datastream.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the private MEX-file used by dicomread with the updated version that is contained in the attached file.Quit MATLAB.In the directory $MATLAB/toolbox/images/medformats/private, rename the existing dicom_decode_rle_segment.mex* file to dicom_decode_rle_segment.mex.old.Download the attachment and copy the appropriate MEX-file for your platform to the medformats/private directory.Restart MATLAB and issue the command rehash toolbox.

Summary: Ampersand (&) does not appear in mask description or parameter prompt strings
Description: The parameter prompts and description of a mask dialog box do not display ampersands (&).
Workaround: Double up the ampersand (&) to work around the issue.  For instance, if you wanted your masked block's widget promt to display:Min & Maxenter the following string into the Prompt field of the Mask Editor:Min && Max

Summary: Transition priority numbers may not show up in printouts
Description: Transition priority numbers don't appear in printouts of Stateflow charts that have never been viewed.
Workaround: Open charts and view them before printing.

Summary: Asynchronous Machine wound rotor block cannot be used in a phasor simulation
Description: The Asynchronous Machine block, when defined as a wound rotor machine, cannot be simulated using the phasor solution. Currently, the correct error message is not displayed.

Summary: procedure field in the optimValues structure for the output function is incorrectly active for the lsqnonlin, lsqcurvefit, and fsolve functions
Description: The field procedure  in the optimValues  structure for  the output function is incorrectly active for the lsqnonlin , lsqcurvefit , and fsolve  functions. This field should not apply for these functions.
Workaround: Do not use the field optimValues.procedure in the output function for lsqnonlin, lsqcurvefit, and fsolve functions.

Summary: impixelinfo errors when used with four or more subplots
Description: When I display four or more images in subplots and call impixelinfo , the function errors as follows:  ??? Error using ==> diffToo many input arguments.Error in ==> imuitools\private\reactToImageChangesInFig at 35  if any(diff(hFig{:}))Error in ==> impixelinfoval at 91reactToImageChangesInFig(himage,@deleteFcn);Error in ==> impixelinfo>createPanel at 142        hPixelInfoValue = impixelinfoval(hPixInfoPanel,imageHandles);Error in ==> impixelinfo at 82hPixInfoPanel = createPanel;
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attachment which contains a fixed version of the reactToImageChangesInFig.m file. Here are the required steps: Quit MATLAB.Rename the reactToImageChangesInFig.m in your current MATLAB installation to any other name, such as reactToImageChangesInFig.m.old. This file resides in $MATLAB\toolbox\images\imuitools\private, where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Download the attached file, extract reactToImageChangesInFig.m and place it in the above directory.Restart MATLABAfter restarting MATLAB, issue the command rehash toolboxcache at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Non-double expression values specified for Stateflow data initial value, min, or max properties cause incorrect behavior
Description: The initial value , min , and max  properties of Stateflow data objects can be specified using a parameter expression. When the parameter expression evaluates to a value whose type is not double , Stateflow misinterpreted the value, resulting in incorrect simulation results.
Workaround: There are two ways to work around this issue:Make sure the parameter expression evaluates to the double datatype.Change the parameter expression in the Stateflow data dialog to have an explicit cast. For example, change (x+y) to double(x+y).

Summary: The Device type parameter does not work for some blocks in the Electric Drive library
Description: In the AC3, AC5, and AC6 blocks in the Electric Drives library, changing the value of Device type  parameter on the Converter and DC bus  tab has no effect on the model. No matter what the setting is, the IGBT/Diodes  device is always used by the model.

Summary: Memory usage: Real-Time Workshop uses more memory for signals with elements that occupy discontiguous areas of memory
Description: For signals with elements that occupy discontiguous areas of memory, such as signals created with Mux and Selector blocks, Real-Time Workshop can create more local variables and, thus, use more memory than in previous releases.

Summary: Array Editor: Cannot Plot Structure Fields for Large Arrays
Description: When editing structures in the Array Editor, selecting a field with a plottable value (such as a double array) should enable the graph button in the Array Editor toolbar. This does not happen for large arrays which display "summaries" of their variables rather than expressions directly showing their values.
Workaround: To plot a field of a structure array, use the appropriate plotting command in the MATLAB Command Window.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB Function blocks may generate unused functions
Description: The code generated from an Embedded MATLAB function block may include functions that are not referenced. This does not result in incorrect behavior, but may use more memory than necessary.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: cccsdsp method returns error message "could not locate vbd.dll"
Description: This error message happens when your project includes DSP/BIOS with CCS 3.1.When you use ccsdsp  to open Code Composer Studio, and you use any DSP/BIOS features in your project, or you use the method profile  with a project that includes DSP/BIOS, you get an error message that says the file vbd.dll  could not be loaded.
Workaround: Work around this by opening CCS manually and then using ccsdsp to create the link object.

Summary: The interfileinfo function in Image Processing Toolbox 5.1 (R14SP3) does not support comments
Description: The interfileinfo  function in Image Processing Toolbox 5.1 (R14SP3) does not support comments. If a semicolon (";") is used in the header file to denote the beginning of a comment, the function returns the following error message:  ??? Error using ==> interfileinfo Invalid expression in line 20 of foo.hdr.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the existing version of the interfileinfo.m file with the updated version included in the attached file.Make a back-up copy of the MATLABROOT\toolbox\images\medformats\interfileinfo.m file, where MATLABROOT represents your existing MATLAB installation, as returned using the matlabroot function. For example, make a copy of the file called interfileinfo.m.OLD.Download the attached file, extract interfileinfo.m and place it in the above directory.At the MATLAB command prompt, issue the following command: >> rehash toolbox

Summary: Segmentation fault when resizing or repainting a figure with ActiveX in a a deployed application
Description: If you deploy an application created with MCC, and if the application contains a figure containing an ActiveX control, and if the figure is resized or repainted, the application will terminate because of a segmentation violation. The problem occurs even though the application might execute without any problems in MATLAB.
Workaround: Extract the attached file (patch.zip)to the following location:MCRROOT/java/where MCRROOT is the location of the MCR used to run the compiled app.  This operation should create the following files:MCRROOT/java/patch/com/mathworks/hg/peer/ActiveXCanvas.classMCRROOT/java/patch/com/mathworks/hg/peer/ActiveXCanvas$1.classMCRROOT/java/patch/com/mathworks/hg/peer/ActiveXCanvas$2.classIf the user wishes to test compiled apps directly against a MATLAB installation, then that installation must be patched by extracting patch.zip to MATLABROOT/java/.

Summary: C++ S-functions: link error due to unresolved external symbol
Description: If you configure your model for C++ code generation and the model uses C++ S-functions, a link error occurs during a build due to an unresolved external symbol. More specifically, an extern "C"  declaration is missing from the area of generated code that includes the header file cg_sfun.h  or from inside cg_sfun.h .
Workaround: Open the provided file matlab/rtw/c/src/cg_sfun.h. Search for the following line of code void S_FUNCTION_NAME(SimStruct *S) Just before the line of code you found in step 2, add#ifdef __cplusplus extern "C" #endif

Summary: Why do I receive errors when trying to acquire data using the PC2-Camlink?
Description: When I try to acquire data from the PC2-Camlink, I receive errors from the Sapera libraries, such as: Warning: Message from the Sapera low-level driver:Error in "CorXferConnect"  - Software error (86)Warning: Message from the Sapera low-level driver:SapTransfer::Grab => Cannot be called before the Connect methodWarning: Message from the Sapera low-level driver:SapTransfer::Freeze => Cannot be called before the Connect method  In these cases no data is collected.
Workaround: This is an intermittent problem. Trying to restart the acquisition will generally succeed. Alternatively, disabling hyper-threading on the affected PC seems to resolve this issue.

Summary: Default HDL optimizations for direct-form FIR transposed filter can cause results from generated code to differ from MATLAB filter results
Description: In HDL code generation for direct-form FIR transposed (dfilt.dffirt ) filters, a default optimization is performed in which taps that have coefficients of the same absolute value share a multiplier. For coefficients that have the same absolute value but opposite signs, a unary minus (negation) operation is applied after the multiplier to get the product values of corresponding coefficients. In cases where the multiplier bits are fewer than required for full precision, the result can be rounded to the most negative number that can be represented by the multiplier bits. In such cases, the unary minus operation produces an incorrect result, because saturation occurs when trying to represent the result of the negation.

Summary: Incorrect thermal noise power when the reference impedance is not 50 ohm.
Description: The S-Parameters Amplifier and the S-Parameters Mixer blocks generate incorrect thermal noise power when the reference impedance is not 50 ohm.

Summary: lsqnonlin and lsqcurvefit output function: incorrect residual and resnorm fields for optimValues
Description: For both the lsqnonlin  and lsqcurvefit  functions (using either medium- or large-scale versions), two fields in the optimValues  structure of the output function contain errors. The error descriptions are as follows:1. The field residual  returns the sum of squares instead of the residual vector. The residual vector is returned in the field fval ; however, fval  should not apply to either of these functions.2. There is no resnorm  field containing the sum of squares.
Workaround: Obtain the value of the residual vector from optimValues.fval and the sum of squares, the resnorm field, by computing it inside your output function from the residual vector:residual = optimValues.fval;resnorm = sum(residual.^2);

Summary: Duplicate directory warning when using MATLAB Compiler
Description: addpath  commands in startup.m  files cause 'duplicate directory' warning messages when running mcc . For example, if a startup.m  file contains:addpath /my/path/and/dir You may see this warning message when running mcc :Warning: Duplicate directory name: /my/path/and/dir This warning message is harmless. The mcc  command will complete successfully even if these warnings appear.
Workaround: Protect the use of addpath in startup.m with ~isdeployed. For example, if you have an addpath command such as:addpath /my/path/and/dirChange it to:if ~isdeployed    addpath /my/path/and/direndto suppress the warning message.

Summary: CCSDSP command fails with error message
Description: When you issue the commandcc=ccsdsp you get an error message that Link for CCS could not start a MATLAB component:??? Error using ==> ccs.ccsdsp.initializeCcsdsp>DetectConfigErrorsCould not start the MATLAB component in CCS.Please use Link for CCS FAQ to troubleshoot the problem.
Workaround: Link for CCS uses two plugin components that need to be registered and enabled with CCS--LinkCCS.dll and MWCCSStu.ocx.To fix this CCSDSP command problem, perform the following steps to register and enable the Link for CCS components.Note: To complete these steps to register and enable the components, you must have permission to modify the registry (write permission). If you do not have this permission,find someone who does have permission, such as your system administrator, and have that person perform this registration procedure.1.
