Register the Link for CCS componentsa. Close all instances of Code Composer Studio.b. Close all instances of MATLAB.c. Open a Microsoft Windows Command Prompt by clicking Start and then Programs > Accessories > Command Prompt.d. In the Command Prompt, enter the following command to register the LinkCCS.dll component.regsvr32 [MATLABROOT]\bin\win32\LinkCCS.dllwhere [MATLABROOT] represents the root directory of your MATLAB installation. Entering the above command opens a dialog box. Verify that the following message appears. DllRegisterServer in [MATLABROOT]\bin\win32\LinkCCS.dll succeeded e. In the Command Prompt, enter the following command to register the MWCCSStu.ocx component.regsvr32 [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\ccslink\bin\win32\MWCCSStu.ocxEntering the above command opens a dialog box. Verify that the following message appears. DllRegisterServer in [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\ccslink\bin\win32\MWCCSStu.ocx succeeded2. Enable the Link For CCS componenta. Open MATLABb. Enter cc = ccsdsp at the MATLAB prompt.CCS starts and prompts that "New components were detected."c. Click Yes to enable components for all compatible CCS releases.

Summary: MPI library dependence upon libcrypto
Description: UNIX computers require version 0.9.8 of libcrypto.so to run parallel jobs.
Workaround: To run parallel jobs with DCT/MDCE Version 2.0 on UNIX computers, you need to have libcrypto library version 0.9.8 or 0.9.7 available on your computers. This library comes as a part of the OpenSSL package. If you are using the MathWorks job manager to start parallel jobs, you need this library on all worker computers; if using mpiexec, you need the library on all worker computers and on all client computers. If you are using 64-bit Linux, you must use a 64-bit build of libcrypto.By default, DCT/MDCE Version 2.0 on Linux and Solaris uses libcrypto.so.0.9.8, but you can download an attached update to DCT/MDCE Version 2.0 that uses libcrypto.so.0.9.7.On Mac OS X, DCT/MDCE Version 2.0 uses libcrypto.0.9.7.dylib.To install the update that uses libcrypto.so.0.9.7, download the appropriate tar file for your computer architecture. Unpack the tar file, and copy the three files into $MATLABROOT/bin/$ARCH.The library libcrypto.so.0.9.X (where X is 7 or 8) must be available by the following means according to which scheduler you are using:I.  If using The MathWorks job manager, do one of the following:Make libcrypto.so.0.9.X available in the default library search path (which usually includes /lib and /usr/lib; and possibly /lib64 and /usr/lib64 on 64-bit Linux systems).Copy or make a symbolic link of libcrypto.so.0.9.X into $MATLABROOT/bin/$ARCH/.II. If using mpiexec and smpd, do one of the following:Make libcrypto.so.0.9.X available in the default library search path (which usually includes /lib and /usr/lib; and possibly /lib64 and /usr/lib64 on 64-bit Linux systems).Copy or make a symbolic link of libcrypto.so.0.9.X into $MATLABROOT/bin/$ARCH/ AND put the location of libcrypto.so.0.9.X into LD_LIBRARY_PATH (or DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH on the Macintosh) prior to starting smpd.Put the location of libcrypto.so.0.9.X into LD_LIBRARY_PATH (or DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH on the Macintosh) prior to starting smpd and MATLAB clients.

Summary: Minor Undocumented Change in R14: Output Arguments in Function Definition
Description: In R14 and later releases, you have more flexibility in the way you specify output arguments in a function definition statement. This is a relatively minor change and the new syntax is optional, however it was not documented. More importantly, this new syntax is not backward-compatible. If you are writing a new function that will be used not only in R14 or later, but also in versions of MATLAB prior to R14, you cannot use this new syntax. The following release note describes the new optional syntax in the function definition line and the incompatibility if used in a release that is earlier than R14. This note will appear in the R2006b documentation: Comma Separators Not Required in Function Declaration As of Release 14, the function definition line in a function MATLAB-file no longer requires commas separating output variables. This now makes the function definition syntax the same as the syntax used when calling an MATLAB-file function:    function [A B C] = myfun(x, y) Compatibility Considerations This new syntax is not valid in MATLAB versions earlier than Release 14. When writing an MATLAB file that you expect to run on versions both earlier and later than R14, be sure to separate any output variables in the function definition line with commas:    function [A, B, C] = myfun(x, y)

Summary: Copying curves from one axes and pasting them to another in a different figure causes an error
Description: After loading a figure containing curves generated by tools such as "Basic Fitting" or "Data Statistics", copying a curve and pasting it into another axes (in a second figure window) using MATLAB 7.1 (R14SP3) will generate the following error:??? No constructor scribe.scribeevent with matching signature foundError in ==> \\mathworks\devel\archive\R14\sp3\ship\win32\dist\toolbox\matlab\graph2d\plotedit.p>scribe_paste at 1536  methods(scribeax,'paste_copybuffer',fig);Error in ==> \\mathworks\devel\archive\R14\sp3\ship\win32\dist\toolbox\matlab\graph2d\plotedit.p>plotedit at 250    scribe_paste(fig);Error in ==> editmenufcn at 44        plotedit(hfig,'Paste');??? Error while evaluating uimenu Callback.
Workaround: This is a bug in MATLAB 7.1 (R14SP3).One workaround is to perform copy/paste using the findobj and copyobj functions. You can do this as follows: Open the first figure Run the following line at the MATLAB command prompt:curve_handle = findobj(gca,'Type','Line'); Open the second figure Execute the following at the MATLAB command prompt:copyobj(curve_handle,gca)Note that this will copy all line objects, should there be more than one.An alternative workaround is to execute the following lines of code after loading each figure:if isappdata(gcf, 'PlotManager'), rmappdata(gcf, 'PlotManager'); endThis will ensure that invalid application data is removed from the newly loaded figure. Performing the copy/paste operation should now work as expected.

Summary: Error running a model with an unconnected Self-Controlled Synchronous Motor Drive (AC5) block
Description: If you try to run a model with a Self-Controlled Synchronous Motor Drive (AC5) block when its A B C terminals are left unconnected, you get the following error: ??? Error using ==> horzcat All matrices on a row in the bracketed expression must have the same number of rows.
Workaround: The workaround is to connect the A B C terminals to any SimPowerSystems block, for example the Three Phase Source block from the powerlib library.

Summary: Receive "NIDAQmx error: Requested value is not a supported value for this property" when adding an invalid channel number.
Description: When using National Instruments hardware that uses the NI-DAQmx interface, attempting to add more channels than are supported causes an ambiguous error message. % This device only supports 4 channels.  ai = analoginput('nidaq', 'Dev1');  addchannel(ai, 5);  ??? Error using ==> daqdevice.addchannel   NIDAQmx error : Requested value is not a supported value for this property. Property: DAQmx_AI_TermCfg You Have Requested: DAQmx_Val_Diff You Can Select: DAQmx_Val_RSE  Channel Name: Dev1/ai4  Task Name: MWDAT0021  Status Code: -200077
Workaround: Use only valid channel IDs as described by your hardware specifications.  Note that many devices reduce the number of valid channels when the device is configured for differential versus single-ended mode.

Summary: Parallel jobs with job manager limited to fewer than 50 labs
Description: When running a parallel job with the MathWorks job manager, the number of labs running the job must be less than 50. If a parallel job is run on more than 50 labs, the job will fail and the tasks will be canceled.
Workaround: To work around this problem, download the appropriate patch file for your platform, and install on all the worker machines. Note that this  patch also incorporates the change to use libcrypto version 0.9.7 as described in  Bug Report 287885.To install the update, download the appropriate tar or zip file for your computer architecture. Unpack the tar or zip file, and copy the  files into $MATLABROOT/bin/$ARCH.Alternatively, use an MPIExec scheduler to run your parallel jobs. This limitation on the number of labs (workers) that a parallel job can run on does not exist when using an MPIEXEC scheduler.

Summary: MPIExec scheduler does not work with clients and labs on the same Solaris host
Description: Due to limitations in the MPICH2 build of mpiexec  shipped with the distributed computing products, it is not possible to submit jobs from a Solaris client for execution on the local machine.In this case, the job object remains in the queued  state, and the debug log shows errors generated by MPIExec.

Summary: MPI communications might fail for large messages on Windows
Description: When sending large messages (over approximately 50 MB) on Windows clusters using either labSend /labReceive  or labBroadcast , the communication might fail. During a call to labSend , this failure results in an error message similar to:MPI Communication Error: Internal MPI error!, error stack:MPI_Send(177): MPI_Send(buf=0x11EA0030, count=32000064, MPI_UNSIGNED_CHAR, dest=0, tag=0, comm=0x84000007) failed(unknown)(): Internal MPI error!
Workaround: This problem is caused by an underlying problem in the MPICH2 MPI libraries. To work around this problem: Download and install on all worker machines the latest full version of MPICH2 from the MPICH2 Web page. Create a custom version of mpiLibConf as described in the Distributed Computing Toolbox documentation that refers to one of the following MPI implementation libraries:           mpich2ssm.dll            mpich2sshm.dll           mpich2shm.dll The MPICH2 Installer's Guide on the download site details which of these is appropriate for your cluster.With the current release of MPICH2 (1.0.2), this workaround only works with the mpiexec scheduler, as none of the above mentioned alternative MPI libraries implements "MPI_Open_port". It is expected that later releases of MPICH2 will support "MPI_Open_port" in all libraries.

Summary: Signal labels may not propagate in some cases
Description: After updating a diagram, the label for a signal connected to a signal routing block, such as a Mux block, may fail to appear downstream from the signal routing block in some cases, for example, when a nonvirtual block, such as a Unit Delay block, feeds the signal routing block.

Summary: State space and transfer function blocks: blocks violate direct feedthrough rule
Description: You can use the following Simulink blocks to represent linear system equations: Continuous State-Space Continuous Transfer Fcn Continuous Zero-Pole Discrete State-Space Discrete Transfer Fcn Discrete Zero-Pole Discrete Filter  Whether these blocks have direct feedthrough depends on your equation. For example, a state space equation has direct feedthrough if any element in the D-matrix is non-zero.  A strictly proper transfer function does not have direct feedthrough. Simulink determines a block's direct feedthrough property when compiling the model, and you cannot change it during simulation or after code generation. For blocks that do not have direct feedthrough, Real-Time Workshop generates code that can violate the rule that a block's output equation must not read the input value.   For example, for a Discrete State-Space block with no direct feedthrough, Real-Time Workshop incorrectly generates the following output equation:     y = C*x + D*u This equation violates the rule of not reading the input u .  Instead, Real-Time Workshop should generate the following simplified output equation:     y = C*x Similar errors affect the other blocks listed above. One potential consequences is a numerical error in the block's output.  This occurs only when the value of the input is Inf  or  NaN .  When there is no direct feedthrough, D = 0 .   When D = 0 , there is no numerical difference between y = C*x + D*u  and y = C*x , except when u = Inf  or  u = NaN .  The design defines y = C*x  to be the correct numerical answer when there is no direct feedthrough.The other consequence is that if you use these blocks with Model blocks or when generating an S-Function from a subsystem, diagnostics detect the direct feedthrough violation and cause errors to occur.

Summary: Memory leak with figure windows on Unix.
Description: A small amount of memory is leaked when creating and destroying figure windows on Unix. After many windows are created and destroyed - perhaps several hundred, depending on available memory - the X server runs out of memory causing MATLAB to exit, typically with a Segmentation Violation.
Workaround: There is no known work around for this issue.

Summary: Small measurement errors possible with National Instruments PCI-6036E and DAQCard-6036E boards.
Description: There may be a small measurement error (one percent or less) when measuring analog inputs with the National Instruments PCI-6036E and DAQCard-6036E boards. This error is possible because the driver does not calibrate the board, which it previously did. The result may be incorrect calibration of the analog input circuit.

Summary: DRIVE.DriveBlock not found when simulating driveline model
Description: SimDriveline occasionally fails to update a diagram or run a model, instead reporting the following error:Error using ==> drive_solverblockPhysical Modeling exception: cannot compile solver block Undefined function or variable 'DRIVE.DriveBlock'.
Workaround: Exit the MATLAB session where this error has happened. In a new MATLAB session, before attempting to run any models containing SimDriveline blocks, enter at the command line:>> drive_compilerThis ensures that the appropriate SimDriveline classes are loaded before they are needed.

Summary: Opening the tools from the Powergui block resets the simulation results of the model
Description: Every time an analysis tool is selected in the Powergui block (Steady-State Voltages and Currents , Initial State Settings , etc.), SimPowerSystems performs a 0 second simulation of the model to collect important information about the model that is requested to perform the analysis.This 0 second simulation erases the old simulation results displayed in the scopes, and also overwrites any To Workspace  data generated from the previous simulation.
Workaround: There are no workarounds other that to run another simulation after working with the Powergui block.

Summary: Multiple SimEvents models cannot run or perform graphical update simultaneously
Description: Simulating or performing graphical updates simultaneously for more than one model that contains SimEvents blocks can cause an exception. Operations that cause a graphical update includeSimulation > Start (Ctrl+T) Edit > Update Diagram (Ctrl+D) Format > Port/Signal Displays > Sample Time Colors Format > Port/Signal Displays > Port Data Types Format > Port/Signal Displays > Signal Dimensions Format > Port/Signal Displays > Storage Class Format > Port/Signal Displays > Wide Nonscalar Lines Format > Block Displays > Sorted Order Format > Block Displays > Execution Context Indicator
Workaround: When working with multiple SimEvents models, do not perform any combination of the listed operations for more than one of the models at the same time.

Summary: Simulation failure if mask parameters for SimMechanics blocks defined in a function
Description: Simulation fails if SimMechanics block mask parameters are defined in a function, and the simulation is started from within the function, even if the simulation parameter SrcWorkspace  is set to 'current' .
Workaround: Place a SimMechanics block whose parameters are variables defined in the function inside a masked subsystem. ThenSet the subsystem's parameters to the variables defined in the function.Set the SimMechanics block's parameters to the corresponding parameters in the subsystem.Refer to the attached files for an example.  Running pendulum_test('pendulum_unmasked') exhibits the problem with the Joint Initial Condition block, and running pendulum_test('pendulum_masked') illustrates the workaround applied to the block.

Summary: Issues in generated code with support of stop time and Simulink External Mode
Description: A Simulink model's stop time is not honored in code generated from the model when the model contains no continuous states. The stop time is not initialized and the generated executable does not run the model's model _step  function. This issue affects The tornado.tlc  target and ERT (ert.tlc ) based targets when the GRT compatible call interface  configuration option is selected External mode use with ERT based targets, including the BareBoardExample  and VxWorksExample  generated example main programs  Additionally, there is an issue with external mode data uploading when the TID01EQ  flag is true. The TID01EQ  flag indicates whether the sampling rates of the continuous task and the fastest discrete task are equal. When the TID01EQ  flag is true, external mode will not upload the data for these rates; only the discrete subrates are uploaded. This issue affects External mode use when the GRT compatible call interface  configuration option is selected External mode use with ERT based targets, including the BareBoardExample  and VxWorksExample  generated example main programs
Workaround: To work around these issues in R2006a or R2006a+, you can update your MATLAB installation tree with the attached files, as follows:Determine your MATLAB root directory, matlabroot, by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.Quit MATLAB.In the directory matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw, rename the files ertlib.tlc, ertextmode.tlc, ertmainlib.tlc and ertmain_grtinterface.tlc to backup names, for example by appending .old to each.Download the attached files and place them in the same directory as the files you renamed.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Printing figures on Unix changes PaperSize
Description: Printing a figure on Unix with the PaperUnits  property set to something other 'inches'  changes the PaperSize  property. The problem can be reproduced on the PC using the print command printdlg -crossplatform .
Workaround: Set the figure Units to 'inches'.

Summary: Digital input bits are not consistently configured
Description: When input digital lines are created, the hardware is not always configured for digital input.  In this example, we configure the line to be a digital output, and then try to configure it to be a digital input.  This assumes that pin 0, port 0 is connected to pin 0, port 1, and that the device is port configurable.  However, this applies to pin configurable devices as well:% force hardware to output state (we do this for illustrative purposes)dio = digitalio('mcc',2);addline(dio,0,0,'Out');delete(dio)% now try to create a digital input (and output to drive it)dio = digitalio('mcc',2);addline(dio,0,1,'Out');addline(dio,0,0,'In');  %
Workaround: Create the line as an output, and then reconfigure it as an input.  This will ensure that the hardware is configured correctly:% Force the bit to output state (we do this for illustrative purposes)dio = digitalio('mcc',2);addline(dio,0,0,'Out');delete(dio)% Now create a digital input (and output to drive it)dio = digitalio('mcc',2);addline(dio,0,1,'Out');addline(dio,0,0,'Out');  % Even though we want this to be an input, momentarily set it to be an outputdio.Line(2).Direction = 'In'; % Now, set it to be an inputputvalue(dio.Line(1),1); % set the output line to 1getvalue(dio)  % Should return [1 1]ans =     1     1

Summary: Referenced models containing non-inlined S-functions may fail to simulate in accelerator mode
Description: If a model contains a non-inlined S-function or a Transport Delay block: Real-Time Workshop fails to generate and build standalone code for the model if the Real-Time Workshop interface option C-API  is selected.  Targets for which this occurs include GRT (grt.tlc ) and ERT (ert.tlc ). The referencing parent model may fail to simulate in accelerator mode.  Real-Time Workshop may display a compiler parse error in model _private.h , for example, In file included from vdp.c:15:vdp_private.h:59: error: parse error before "if"make: *** [vdp.o] Error 1
Workaround: To work around the problem, perform the following steps:Edit the file matlabroot/rtw/c/src/rtw_modelmap.h, where matlabroot specifies your MATLAB root directory, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.Change the following two lines# define  utFree(arg)    if (arg) free(arg)# define  utMalloc(arg)  malloc(arg)to#ifndef __RTW_UTFREE__# define __RTW_UTFREE__# define  utFree(arg)    if (arg) free(arg)# define  utMalloc(arg)  malloc(arg)#endifSave the file and restart MATLAB.

Summary: Cannot compile Fortran MEX-files when MATLAB installation pathname contains spaces
Description: When the pathname of the MATLAB installation directory contains spaces, an attempt to compile Fortran MEX-files using Compaq Visual Fortran or Digital Visual Fortran results in the following error: D:\PROGRAM FILES\MATLAB704\BIN\WIN32\MEX.PL:Error: Compile of 'yprimef.F' failed.  ??? Error using ==> mexUnable to complete successfully The same error results when you build an engine or MAT-file program using Compaq Visual Fortran or Digital Visual Fortran. This error occurs because the Fortran preprocessor in some versions of Compaq Visual Fortran and Digital Visual Fortran does not support pathnames that contain spaces.
Workaround: To work around this issue, take one of these steps:Install MATLAB in a directory whose pathname does not contain spaces.If you do not need to run the Fortran preprocessor to build your MEX-file or program:Locate the options file you are using.Find the line in the options file beginning:set COMPFLAGS=Carefully delete the following portion of that line (not the entire line):/fpp:"/m /S%MATLAB%/extern/include"Save the options file and rebuild your program.Note: If you are using the MWPOINTER preprocessor macro to build for 32-bit and 64-bit platforms without changing your source code, you do need to run the Fortran preprocessor.Use a?different supported Fortran compiler. For example, if you are using a MATLAB release that supports Intel Visual Fortran, use that compiler.

Summary: Maximum number of referenced models limited on Windows platforms
Description: The number of distinct models that can be referenced on Windows platforms is restricted by command line length limitations during the compilation and linking process. Given an average model name length of 25 characters, the limits on the number of distinct submodels able to be referenced is approximately 50 on Windows 2000 and 400 on Windows XP. If your model references more than the maximum supported, building the model's simulation or Real-Time Workshop target causes a compilation error similar to either one of the following: The input line is too long.:getarg was unexpected at this time.gmake: *** [..\..\../mr_lat_aero_msf.mexw32] Error 0xfforNMAKE : fatal error U1095: expanded command line 'set INCLUDE=.;..\..\..;<long include path>' too long Stop. The make command returned an error of 2 'An_error_occurred_during_the_call_to_make' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file. ### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: 'model_name' aborted due to an error.
Workaround: Limit the length of model names used in Model blocks in order to minimize the command line length of the compile and link commands. Note that these limitations do not exist on other platforms.

Summary: createSoapMessage cannot handle arrays of strings as inputs
Description: There is no syntax for calling a SOAP service with an array of strings as input.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: ADC Input block gives an incorrect result when using pins other than PAD00 or PAD08
Description: If you use the ADC Input block in your model with any of the ADC channels other than PAD00 or PAD08, you will receive an incorrect result equivalent to 0V. The incorrect result occurs because the C code is reading from the incorrect result register.
Workaround: The attached file hc12_sfcn_adc_v.tlc resolves the issue with the result register.  It enables you to use any valid bank and channel combination with the ADC Input block and get the expected result. To use the file hc12_sfcn_adc_v.tlc, you will need to download it and place it in the following directory under "matlabroot": \toolbox\rtw\targets\hc12\blocks. You should take care to backup the original file in this location (e.g., by renaming it) before replacing it with the downloaded file.

Summary: Measurement Computing Minilab 1008 output lines cannot be set individually
Description: The following code should result in HW lines 0 and 3 being set high.  Instead, all lines are set to 0.  This issue is being investigated by our development team, but appears to be a limitation of the hardware.dio = digitalio('mcc',0);%Add four lines from port 0%The device is a Measurment Computing Minilab 1008, so port 0 is a %line-configurable device with four lines. Give them unique names zero%through three.addline(dio,0:3,0,'out',{'zero','one','two','three'});%Assign the value [1 0 1 0] to the port.putvalue(dio.one,1);putvalue(dio.two,0);putvalue(dio.three,1);putvalue(dio.four,0);%Get the port data and display it.getvalue(dio)ans =     0     0     0     0
Workaround: You can use the vector form of putvalue (e.g., putvalue(dio,[1 0 1 0]) as a workaround, as shown in the following the example.dio = digitalio('mcc',0);addline(dio,0:3,0,'out',{'zero','one','two','three'});putvalue(dio,[0 1 0 1]) ;getvalue(dio)ans =     0     1     0     1

Summary: Real-Time Workshop error occurs when generating R13sp1+ or R13sp2 code for an ERT-based Simulink model created in R14 or later
Description: If you use Release 14 or later to save an ERT-based Simulink model into R13sp1 format (using Simulink File > Save As > Save as type ), and then try to generate code for the model under R13sp1+ or R13sp2, the following error is displayed: Error executing build command: Error using ==> make_rtw Error using ==> tlc_c Error using ==> tlc_c (InvokeTLC) Error:  Real-Time Workshop Error: Unable to locate ERT header file banner template: ert_code_template.cgt.
Workaround: To work around this problem in R13sp1+ or R13sp2, download and install one of the attached files below.In R13sp1+:Go to the directory matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw (where matlabroot is the MATLAB root directory of your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt) and rename the file setuplib.tlc to setuplib.tlc.old. Download the file setuplib_sp1plus.tlc, listed below under Attachments, and place the file in the same directory as the file you renamed.Rename the downloaded file to setuplib.tlc.  In R13sp2:Go to the directory matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw (where matlabroot is the MATLAB root directory of your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt) and rename the file setuplib.tlc to setuplib.tlc.old. Download the file setuplib_sp2.tlc, listed below under Attachments, and place the file in the same directory as the file you renamed.Rename the downloaded file to setuplib.tlc.

Summary: Opening some Simulink GUIs on Mac OS X 10.4 may crash Simulink
Description: On Mac OS X 10.4 (Tiger), opening a block parameter dialog box, the Model Explorer, and certain other dialog boxes may cause a memory segmentation fault, producing a stack trace similar to the following:Stack Trace:  [0] libqt-mt.3.dylib:0x10e3efcc  [1] libqt-mt.3.dylib:0x10e3feb8  [2] libqt-mt.3.dylib:0x10dd7c54  [3] libqt-mt.3.dylib:0x10e381b0  [4] libqt-mt.3.dylib:0x10f1abb4  ... This appears to be caused by a corrupted font directory in OS 10.4's default X11 installation.
Workaround: To avoid this problem:Quit MATLAB if it is running.Use a command shell, such as Terminal or xterm, to issue thefollowing commands:cd /usr/X11R6/lib/X11/fonts sudo mkfontdir * sudo rm -f */fonts.cache*sudo fc-cache -f -vNote You need local administrator privileges to run sudo.Quit and relaunch the X11 application and MATLAB

Summary: Figure windows incorrectly display some Eastern European and Russian characters
Description: Text in figure windows does not correctly display Russian, Czech, and other Eastern European characters for most fonts. Only the MS Serif and MS Sans Serif fonts display correctly.
Workaround: For R2006a, run the following statement in the Command Window prior to performing any actions that use figure windows:setenv('MATLAB_USE_NATIVE_FONTS','1')For releases prior to R2006a, there is no known workaround.

Summary: MPC555 PIL link error when non-default parameter storage classes are used
Description: A link error similar to that below may occur when building the MPC555 PIL s-function if non-default parameter storage classes are used.   This error occurs when non-default storage classes define parameters within the $(model).c, which is not linked against during the build.Link error:Subsystem_pil_sf.obj : error LNK2001: unresolved external symbol _Param ..\Subsystem_pil_sf.dll : fatal error LNK1120: 1 unresolved externals   D:\APPLICATIONS\MATLAB704\BIN\WIN32\MEX.PL: Error: Link of '..\Subsystem_pil_sf.dll' failed.
Workaround: If a non-default parameter storage class is to be used, make sure the storage class defines the parameter in a file other than the $(MODEL).c file.As an example, with an mpt.Parameter object, you could specify a non-default "Definition file" such as "mydata.c".   This will define your parameter data in the file "mydata.c", and this file will be linked against during the PIL s-function build.

Summary: Printing on Linux or Mac with no printer installed causes Java exception
Description: Printing from Print dialog or toolbar print icon on Linux or Mac when no printers are installed causes the following Java exception:java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: services must be non-null and non-empty
Workaround: Choose among three workarounds:Have your system administrator install printers on your machine. If you already have printers installed, uninstall them and then reinstall them.Print from the command line using the -P option to specify the printer you want to use instead of using the Print dialog box or toolbar print icon. For example,print(gcf,'-PprinterName');If your system is using CUPS printing services set the AuthType in CUPS to None, as described below.Changing CUPS Authentication TypeMATLAB cross platform print dialogs (used on non-windows machines) use Java to determine what printers are available. If CUPS is running, Java attempts to get that information from the CUPS service. If that service has been configured to require authentication, the Java code will fail and MATLAB will report an error.To disable the authentication requirement for CUPS you need to edit the CUPS configuration file and then restart the CUPS service. These operations typically require root permission. The following explains how to make this change. Note that the paths and commands used below may be different in your environment.First, make a backup copy of the current file: cp -p /etc/cups/cupsd.conf  /etc/cups/cupsd.conf.saveEdit the file /etc/cups/cupsd.conf  and look for a section that looks something like the following: <Location />Order Deny,AllowDeny From AllAllow From 127.0.0.1Allow From 127.0.0.2Allow From @LOCAL</Location>Note that the contents may be different in your environment. The key point is to find the section that starts with the  <Location /> tag.Edit this section, and add the following AuthType entry above the </Location>  tag: AuthType NoneIf an AuthType entry already exists, change it to AuthType None.Save the file.Restart CUPS: /etc/init.d/cups restart

Summary: HTML code generation report: missing pound (#) character
Description: The HTML code generation report that Real-Time Workshop generates does not display the pound (#) character when the character exists in the middle of a line of generated code. If the character is at the beginning of a line of code (for example, #include ), the report displays the character correctly.

Summary: Some ICC Version 4 profiles created with iccwrite are incompatible with other software products
Description: Some ICC Version 4 profiles created with the iccwrite  function are incompatible with other applications, such as Adobe Photoshop. Specifically, iccwrite  produces incorrect profiles when using a tone curve (Bcurve) in the mAB  or mBA  tags. In addition, mAB  and mBA  tags with only one point do not have the correct byte alignment.
Workaround: There is no known workaround.

Summary: Unhandled exception when debugging a MEX-file in Microsoft Visual Studio C++ IDE
Description: When debugging a MEX-file in the Microsoft Visual Studio C++ IDE, the following message is thrown: "Unhandled exception at 0x7c901230 in matlab.exe: User breakpoint." The problem is caused by a timing bug in a 3rd party library used by MATLAB, which occasionally corrupts freed memory. When running under a debugger, Microsoft's heap manager detects this corruption and issues the above exception.
Workaround: To work around this issue, start MATLAB from outside Visual Studio and  attach to the running process.

Summary: MPC555 processor-in-the-loop (PIL) does not support complex signals at the PIL subsystem boundary.
Description: Embedded Target for Motorola MPC555 PIL does not support complex signals at the PIL subsystem boundary. In such cases, when the complex signal is transferred from the host to the target (or from the target to the host) the byte swapping is not done correctly, leading to incorrect results. Complex signals internally to the PIL subsystem are supported.
Workaround: As a workaround, please separate the real and imaginary parts of a signal before crossing PIL subsystem boundary.

Summary: Linux? dual-monitor machines show fonts too large in the desktop
Description: On some Linux machines with dual monitors, the fonts in the MATLAB? desktop appear unusually large. For example, when the font size is set to 10 pt in MATLAB Fonts preferences, the fonts that appear in the Command Window, are much larger.
Workaround: As a workaround, change the font size preference to a smaller value, such as 4 pt, by performing these steps:In the desktop, select File -> Preferences and then select Fonts in the left pane.Change the size of the Desktop code font or the Desktop text font, depending on the desktop tools whose size is too large, to a smaller value, such as 4.Click Apply. View the desktop tools to see how the new size looks and either continue adjusting the size, or click OK to accept the change.

Summary: Figure KeyPressFcn callback does not support recursion
Description: If a key press event occurs on a figure while its KeyPressFcn  callback is being executed, another KeyPressFcn  callback is not executed.

Summary: Demo model c166_fuelsys does not build without Stateflow license
Description: If you do not have a license for Stateflow, you will be unable to generate code from the Fault-Tolerant Fuel Control System demo c166_fuelsys.mdl. The documentation for this demo states that it requires Simulink Fixed Point, but it also requires a Stateflow license to generate code (although you can still explore the model in Simulink).

Summary: Error when models run from networked drives
Description: If you run (simulate) a model from a Universal Naming Convention (UNC)network directory, such as \\Server\user\work, the following error is produced: MATLAB error message: Error using ==> dosDOS commands may not be executed when the current directory is a UNC pathname>
Workaround: You can work around this by changing the current MATLAB directory to a local directory, or use a network drive mapped to the current directory. Similarly to the paths specified in your Target Preferences, model directories must be located either an actual hard drive on your PC, or a mapped drive. Do not use a UNC network path.

Summary: Connection line attached to a Body port disconnects if you switch the Port Side
Description: Through the Body block dialog, you can switch the Port Side  of a Body coordinate system (CS) port on a Body block. If you have attached a line to this CS port and you switch port sides, the port will change sides, but the line will be disconnected.
Workaround: Reattach the disconnected connection line to the Body CS port.

Summary: Help browser, Profiler, or Web browser printing might terminate MATLAB on Windows
Description: On some Windows systems, when you print a second time from the Help browser, Profiler, or Web browser, MATLAB might unexpectedly terminate.

Summary: Saving and loading a figure with a colorbar changes axes size
Description: If a figure with a colorbar is saved to a FIG file, when the file is next loaded the size of the data axes will shrink if the value of the ActivePositionProperty  property of the data axes is 'position' .
Workaround: To maintain the same axis size, set the ActivePositionProperty property of the data axes to 'outerposition' before saving or after loading the figure .

Summary: Spaces in MiniMon path cause failures
Description: If the third party download utility MiniMon is installed in a directory whose name contains spaces, it will not work with the Embedded Target for Infineon C166 Microcontrollers.
Workaround: To download your generated applications using MiniMon, please install MiniMon in a directory without spaces.

Summary: Printing a GUI that contains a panel with no title may throw an error.
Description: If you print a GUI that contains a panel with no title, MATLAB may throw an error with the following message:??? Error using ==> printError using ==> graphics\private\prepareError using ==> capturescreenRectangle width and height must be at least 1 pixel.
Workaround: For GUIDE GUIs, open the GUI in GUIDE and double click on the panel that has no title to open the Property Inspector. You can do one of the following:Introduce a space character as the value of the Title property.Delete the empty lines. To do this:Click the small button on the Title property line to open the Title editor.Press Ctrl+A to select all.Press Delete to delete all the empty lines.Save the GUI.

Summary: RTDX-based demos do not work on the C6701 EVM in CCS 3.0
Description: The demos rtdxtutorial  and rtdxlmsdemo  do not work on the C6701 EVM in CCS 3.0.With the CCS 3.0 release, TI has confirmed that there is a bug in RTDX host-to-target communication on the C6701 EVM. For that reason, the RTDX-based demos shipped with the Link for CCS product do not work properly.

Summary: stdsdemread truncates SRPE DEMs having elevation units of decimal meters
Description: When function sdtsdemread  imports elevation values from DEMs that use the SDTS Raster Profile and Extensions (SRPE, in use since Jan 2001) with elevations encoded in 32-bit floating point (units of "decimal meters"), they are truncated to the nearest integer. This is not a problem for SRPE rasters using 16-bit integer encoding (units of "feet" or "meters").

Summary: Using a Simulink.Parameter object to specify a model configuration parameter causes an error
Description: Using a Simulink.Parameter object to specify a model configuration parameter causes an error. For example, suppose that K  is a Simulink.Parameter object whose Value  property equals 20 . Specifying K  as the value of a model's StopTime  parameter causes the following error: Invalid Stop time specified in the Configuration Parameters dialog for block diagram 'modelname'.
Workaround: Specify the value of the model configuration parameter as 'p.Value' where p is the name of the parameter object, for example:StopTime = 'K.Value';NOTE This workaround works only in releases that have the bug and will stop working once the bug is fixed.

Summary: Worker session overwrites its log files when restarted
Description: A worker session overwrites its own log file (for example, in C:\TEMP\MDCE\Log ) each time the worker session is reset by a job's RestartWorker  property.
Workaround: As a workaround, you can access the same log information in the mdce-service.log file, which is not overwritten.

Summary: "Can't find executable file" Error When Using the Metrowerks CodeWarrior Debugger
Description: The CodeWarrior error "Can't find executable file" when attempting to download an application (.elf file) to the target via a BDM connection is known to occur under the following circumstances:1) The filename of the .elf file is long (approximately 32 characters or more)2) The .elf file does not exist
Workaround: In the CodeWarrior error dialog associated with this error, if the filename of your .elf file appears truncated, then it is likely that the filename exceeds the maximum length that can be handled by the CodeWarrior debugger.In this case, you should shorten the name of the Simulink model that was used to create the .elf file.If the .elf file is missing, the most likely cause is an incorrect CodeWarrior configuration.   Please check the Embedded Target for Motorola MPC555 documentation for details of how to configure the "Build before running" setting to "Never". This is a build setting preference found in the CodeWarrior IDE.Once CodeWarrior is correctly configured, you should be able to rebuild your application from the Simulink model.   It should now download successfully.In some cases, having the incorrect CodeWarrior configuration may have done some damage to your Embedded Target for Motorola MPC555 installation.   In particular, certain board support applications (like the bootcode, and flash programmer) may have been deleted.   Even after configuring CodeWarrior correctly you may still receive this error when trying to download pre-built applications such as the bootcode or test application.   In this case, we recommend that you reinstall the Embedded Target for Motorola MPC555.

Summary: Changing SimMechanics block parameters in configurable subsystems
Description: Do not directly change the parameters of SimMechanics blocks in a configurable subsystem or in a target subsystem to which the configurable subsystem is pointing.  There is a risk that the changes will not be propagated correctly in copies of the configurable subsystem.
Workaround: Instead, work around this limitation by masking the target subsystem and defining mask variables.  Within the SimMechanics block fields of the subsystem, refer to these mask variables.  Change their values by changing the corresponding mask dialog entry.

Summary: Linux and Solaris? platforms: Exceptions when selecting files in dialog boxes
Description: When selecting a file or folder in a dialog box, such as the dialog box that appears when you select File > Open  from a desktop tool, MATLAB might report an exception like the following:  java.lang.InterruptedExceptionat javax.swing.filechooser.FileSystemView.getFilesat javax.swing.plaf.basic.BasicDirectoryModel$LoadFilesThread.run
Workaround: As a workaround, use another method to accomplish the task, such as opening a file by double-clicking it in the Current Directory browser. Or use function equivalents, such as addpath.On Linux Redhat platforms, some users were able to work around this issue by running the following at the Linux shell prompt before launching MATLAB:unsetenv LANG

Summary: Cannot run models having too many signal ports on SimEvents blocks
Description: If your model includes a large number of SimEvents blocks that have signal ports, you might see an error message when you run the simulation or update the diagram. The error message might reference the add_block  or des_setDesSfunPorts  function.
Workaround: To work around this problem:Quit MATLAB.Rename the following file by appending .old to the filename: $MATLAB\toolbox\des\desblks\des_setDesSfunPorts.p$MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by entering matlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window.Download the attached file and place it in the directory mentioned in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Enter the following in the MATLAB Command Window: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Physical modeling connection lines domain-indeterminate when model opens
Description: When you first open a model with a mixture of Physical Modeling blocks  (for example, a mixture of SimMechanics and SimPowerSystems blocks), the Physical Modeling connection lines do not have their domain determined if some or all of the Physical Modeling blocks are in subsystems. This condition allows you to make physically meaningless connections between, e.g., mechanical and power systems blocks and lines.
Workaround: To avoid problems arising from this indeterminacy, force Simulink to recognize the block and connection lines by updating your diagram through the model Edit menu or entering Ctrl-D at the keyboard.

Summary: MATLAB 7 may be more sensitive to spaces than earlier versions
Description: We have made some changes in the way that MATLAB distinguishes between command dual statements like     hold on and expressions. In particular, we now use a heuristic that behaves like earlier MATLAB versions most of the time, but can occasionally interpret something as command dual when an expression was intended. For example,    xxx {1} used to check the workspace, and consider this an expression if xxx  was defined, and a command otherwise. Now it attempts to call the function called xxx  and pass it the argument {1}. The rationale for this change is that there are programs such as the MATLAB editor and M-Lint that may be run outside of MATLAB, where no workspace or path are available. The new heuristic allows these programs to recognize commands without using the workspace or path. If you want to access a variable called xxx , you should write it as    xxx{1}
Workaround: If you want to access a variable called xxx, you should write it as   xxx{1}

Summary: Using a wildcard (*) to retrieve a file with no extension
Description: If you use the uigetfile function, i.e., the standard file selection dialog box, to retrieve a file that has no extension and use a wildcard as the filter string, you get a warning dialog similar to:  <'path of file'> already exists. Do you want to replace it?
Workaround: Ignore the warning dialog by selecting Yes and the application should do the right thing. ?

Summary: Windows? XP look and feel changes default appearance of UIcontrols
Description: On Windows machines, when the screen display theme is set to Windows XP, UIcontrols now use the XP look and feel. This changes the look for some UIcontrols. For example, if you set the background color of a push button or toggle button to be a color other than "white", only the border displays the specified color.
Workaround: If you want the background to display in the specified color, use one of the following workarounds: If you are running MATLAB? 7.2 (R2006a), you can create backward compatible push button UIcontrols with specified background colors. These buttons look like MATLAB push buttons except that they are not three-dimensional. They support all push button UIcontrol properties. Use the patch files, toggleuicontrolbgcompatibility.m and setbgcompatibility.p, included in the attached .zip file to set the push button backward compatible mode on or off for the figure. Push buttons you create with the backward compatible mode set to on will have the specified background color. Push buttons you create with the backward compatible mode set to off will appear in standard XP format. To use the patch files:Download the  attached .zip file, attachment_194025_20060419.zip.Extract the patch files from the .zip file and save them where your code can access them, such as on your path. Type path at the MATLAB prompt to see your path.Update the code for your GUI to call toggleuicontrolbgcompatibility wherever you want to turn this backward compatibility on or off.Type help toggleuicontrolbgcompatibility at the MATLAB prompt for information about the syntax and more details about using the patch.Note that this workaround does not apply to toggle buttons.For MATLAB 7.0 and later, add a java.opts file which contains the statement: -Dswing.noxp=trueto your MATLAB startup directory or your $MATLAB\bin\win32 directory, where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. Note that after installing a new version of MATLAB, you should copy this file from your previous MATLAB version to your new MATLAB version to ensure that the workaround continues to function.For all MATLAB releases, set the screen display to Windows Classic style: Right click on the desktop and select PropertiesSelect the Appearance tabUnder Windows and buttons, select Windows Classic style

Summary: Cursor blinks in MATLAB desktop tools despite being disabled in Windows system setting
Description: The cursor blinks in some desktop tools, including the Command Window and Editor/Debugger, even though the Windows system setting for cursor blinking is turned off.
Workaround: There is a workaround available for MATLAB V7.2 (R2006a) and V7.1 (R14SP3). Follow these instructions using the appropriate attached patch file: noblink.zip for V7.1 (R14SP3) and noblink2006a.zip for V7.2 (R2006a)The attached patch file disables the blinking cursor for the MATLAB desktop tools that do not honor the Windows system setting. To install the patch and turn off the blinking cursor for MATLAB, perform these steps:Quit MATLAB.Save the attached patch file to matlabroot\java file, where matlabroot is the directory in which you installed MATLAB.  Double-click the zip-file in Windows Explorer to open WinZip.Click the Extract button.You are prompted to extract to matlabroot\java\. Click Extract.Verify the extraction was successful by viewing the contents of matlabroot\java\patch. It should contain the directory com. The com directory should contain mwt, mwswing, and mde.Turn off the system blinking cursor for the desktop components that do honor system settings by following these steps:From the Windows Start button, select Settings > Control Panel > Accessibility Options.Select the Display tab.Under Cursor Options, use the slider to set Blink Rate to None. This turns off the blinking cursor for all applications that honor it, including some desktop tools in MATLAB.Start MATLAB. The cursor should not blink in any MATLAB desktop tools.

Summary: Simulink model parameters are limited to 64 kBytes
Description: Assigning a model parameter (such as the description of a masked subsystem) a value grater than 64 kilobytes may cause the model file to become corrupted, and any data values above 64 kilobytes will not be saved.
Workaround: Do not allow model parameters to exceed 64 kilobytes.?To determine the size of a model parameter, create it first as a variable in MATLAB and use the whos command to get the size:>> description_string = repmat('a', 1, 30000);>> whos?? Name??????????????????? Size?????????????? Bytes? Class??? Attributes ? description_string????? 1x30000??????????? 60000? char?In this case, the variable description_string is less than 64 kilobytes. Use the set_param command?to set this model parameter:>> set_param(<path_to_my_block>, 'Description', description_string)

Summary: Using the cassini, eqaconic, eqdconic, lambert, and polycon projections with offset origins
Description: Projection functions in the Mapping Toolbox, excludingtranmerc  and those in the PROJ.4 library, implementchanges in map origin as a solid-body rotation of the sphere.This differs from the standard formulation of the followingprojections, when the origin latitude is nonzero:cassini  (Soldner-Cassini)eqaconic  (Albers Equal-Area Conic)eqdconic  (Equidistant Conic)lambert  (Lambert Conformal Conic)polycon  (Polyconic) Results can be substantially different, and the differences are of the same order in both the spherical and ellipsoidal cases. This can cause misregistration when data output from these projections combined with other geodata that has been preprojected to the same projection.
Workaround: If these projections must be used with a non-zero originlatitude and the results must be combined with other,pre-projected data (for example when using lambertto project to a State Plane zone), users should preprocess their datawith function projfwd (or, if an inverse transformationis required, with  projinv).  Then the preprojecteddata can be displayed directly in map (x-y) coordinates usingmapshow, for example.

Summary: Macintosh: printing from Command Window or Editor/Debugger in landscape mode not supported
Description: On Macintosh platforms, you cannot print a page from the Command Window or Editor/Debugger in landscape mode.
Workaround: As a workaround, print the file from a different application. For the Command Window, first copy and paste the contents to a file.

Summary: Web browser does not support HTML files generated from Microsoft Word and PowerPoint
Description: If you save Microsoft Word and PowerPoint files as Web pages (HTML format), the HTML files might not display in the MATLAB Web browser. HTML files generated by Microsoft applications contain markup that is not recognized by the MATLAB Web browser, which supports the HTML 4.01 specification.
Workaround: As a workaround, open the files using Internet Explorer instead of the MATLAB Web browser, or modify the HTML files to remove unsupported markup.

Summary: C6701 EVM big-endian issues when using LED block
Description: In big-endian mode, two problems affect the LED block for the C6701 EVM. They are:The C6701 EVM LED block provides the option to light only the external LED on the board. When your board is in big-endian mode and you select the external LED option, the block lights both the internal and external LEDs.In some cases, having a C6701 EVM LED block in your model can cause the codec on the board to fail. This is not a problem in little-endian operation.

Summary: Internal viewer does not display large scale worlds
Description: Due to a limitation in the internal viewer, scenes that have large scale worlds may not display correctly. Objects that are either far from the viewpoint or too close may be partially or fully clipped away.
Workaround: Edit your virtual world through the V-Realm Builder or a text editor. Increase the visibilityLimit field of the NavigationInfo node to a number that is greater than the longest distance in the virtual world. In order to eliminate clipping of close objects, define also the avatarSize field and set its first component to a number that corresponds to the distance between the viewpoint and the closest objects in the view. For example, NavigationInfo  {                                                              ??...   ??visibilityLimit 1e6   ??avatarSize [1, 1.6, 0.75]   ??... }

Summary: Help and Web browser printouts lose spaces with 12 point fonts
Description: When you print from the Help browser or Web browser and the font sizepreference is set to 12 pt, some spaces between words are lost, making that portion of the text hard to read.
Workaround: As a workaround, change the font size preference for HTML Proportional Text to another size, such as 10 pt, to make the printing legible. In Version 6.5 (R13), the font size preference you need to change is for the Help browser.

Summary: Menu figure window does not close after using Ctrl+C
Description: If you use the menu  command to create a standalone menu figure window and then type Ctrl+C , you cannot close the menu window and you cannot exit MATLAB.  Typing close all  at the command line does not close the menu window.
Workaround: You can close the menu figure window in either of these ways:Use the Windows Task Manager to end the MATLAB task.Type the following at the command line to force closing of the menu.f = findall(0,'type','figure','Name','MENU'); delete(f);

Summary: The most up-to-date residuals are not returned for medium-scale versions of the lsqnonlin and lsqcurvefit functions, and the Levenberg-Marquardt and Gauss-Newton versions of the fsolve function
Description: The medium-scale version of the nonlinear least squares functions lsqnonlin  and lsqcurvefit , and the Levenberg-Marquardt and Gauss-Newton versions of the nonlinear equation solver fsolve  are not returning the most up-to-date residual vector in the outputs residual  and resnorm .
Workaround: When calling the medium-scale version of lsqnonlin or lsqcurvefit, recompute the residual and its norm by evaluating the objective function at the returned solution, x, using the following:options = optimset('LargeScale','off');[x,resnorm,residual] = lsqnonlin(fun,x0,[],[],options);residual = fun(x);resnorm = norm(residual);Analogously, for fsolve, use:options = optimset('NonlEqnAlgorithm','lm'); % same with 'gn'[x,fval] = fsolve(fun,x0,options);fval = fun(x);
